title: Fruit Fly Trap — US Market Analysis
subtitle: Data: Helium 10 X-Ray

reviews.cpSummary: Customer profile based on 5315 reviews across 40 ASINs. The most common buyer is a homeowner dealing with flies, most purchases happen within days of spotting flies, traps are placed near fruit, and the dominant concern is effectiveness.

usageScenarios.label: Kitchen fruit fly control
usageScenarios.reason: primary use case — traps placed near fruit bowls, countertops, and sinks where fruit flies congregate.

usageScenarios.label: Replacing DIY vinegar traps
usageScenarios.reason: customers tired of homemade apple cider vinegar + dish soap setups seek a cleaner, more convenient commercial alternative.

usageScenarios.label: Summer infestation response
usageScenarios.reason: warm weather triggers sudden fruit fly explosions, driving impulse purchases for quick control.

usageScenarios.label: Eco-friendly pest control
usageScenarios.reason: customers seek non-toxic, chemical-free solutions safe for homes with children and pets.

usageScenarios.label: Compost and waste area
usageScenarios.reason: placement near compost bins, trash cans, and organic waste areas where fruit flies breed.

usageScenarios.label: Persistent multi-week use
usageScenarios.reason: customers deploy traps over weeks for ongoing control, evaluating effectiveness over extended periods.

usageScenarios.label: Electric trap for broad coverage
usageScenarios.reason: UV/LED light traps used for broader insect control including gnats, mosquitoes, and drain flies.

usageScenarios.label: Discreet home decor
usageScenarios.reason: customers value traps that blend into kitchen decor and don't look like traditional pest control.

usageScenarios.label: Gift or recommendation
usageScenarios.reason: buyers frequently purchase after friend recommendation or as a practical gift for someone with fly problems.

usageScenarios.label: Quick overnight solution
usageScenarios.reason: buyers expect visible results by next morning, evaluating within first 12-24 hours.

reviews.csSummary: Sentiment analysis from 5315 reviews. 262 positive (4-5 star), 5053 negative (1-3 star). Avg rating: 1.93.

Many compare unfavorably to DIY vinegar traps
Emotional language: 'laughing,' 'dancing around,' 'waste'
quotes: "its not working effectively."

"Does not work the best only a few gnats go in, otherwise they sit on the lid."

negativeInsights.finding: 34.3% cite zero catches. #1 complaint and biggest threat to category listings.
negativeInsights.implication: Product R&D: attractant reformulation. A+ content: realistic expectations.

negativeInsights.finding: 14.6% say vinegar works better. Recipe sharing creates free competitor awareness.
negativeInsights.implication: A+ content: 'Why commercial traps outperform DIY' — cleaner, longer lasting.

negativeInsights.finding: 13.4% have sticky trap issues — adhesive, curling, not catching.
negativeInsights.implication: Adhesive quality audit. Consider alternative trap mechanisms.

negativeInsights.finding: 9.0% say overpriced. Price + no effect = maximum frustration.
negativeInsights.implication: Fix efficacy first → price complaints drop. Value multi-pack SKU.

negativeInsights.finding: 7.2% report unpleasant smell. Kitchen placement makes it critical.
4.1% receive damaged/leaking products. First impression drives 1-star ratings.
negativeInsights.implication: Packaging upgrade: sealed inner bag, reinforced cap.
State duration clearly. Extend active life as differentiator.

negativeInsights.finding: Multiple reviews question positive ratings. Scepticism undermines listing.
negativeInsights.implication: Verified photo reviews. Respond to negatives publicly.

positiveInsights.implication: Lead with 'visible results in 24 hours' in A+ content.

positiveInsights.finding: 13.4% mention child/pet safety as purchase driver.
positiveInsights.implication: Safety badges in listing images. Target parents in PPC.

positiveInsights.finding: 13% praise discreet design. Aesthetics differentiate from DIY.
positiveInsights.implication: Lifestyle imagery: trap blending into modern kitchens.

positiveInsights.finding: 9.2% value ease of use. Anti-DIY argument.
6.9% praise fast results. Counters 'no effect' narrative.
11.5% mention no smell positively vs 7.2% complain.

reviews.t: I'm sure they works for someone but not for me. Not one single FF entered.
its not working effectively.

I would not purchase this product again. It was not very effective. It drew fruit flies to the little red apples but the liquid you put in them dried up very quickly. Did not last as long as it said it would. Could not return them for a refund as I did not keep the box they came in. I believed the product was going to work. It took time to realize the product would not work as long as they said it would.

I got tired of making my own traps with paper cones at the top and apple cider vinegar/dish soap. So I ordered these, for ease. They are easy to set up but the flies just go in and out. Only a few end up getting trapped in the liquid. My homemade traps worked much better.
Does not work the best only a few gnats go in, otherwise they sit on the lid.
I had some forgotten bananas hidden on a shelf and got some fruit flies. I've used them many years back and they were great. This time, they didn't work at all.
Either wrong fly but it didn't work and one only lasted a week

I have been using the sticky pads that plug in a wall socket and shine a blue light. They usually get filled quickly. I saw these and thought I'd give them a try since they cost less, don't use a wall socket, and had great reviews. I put one near my counter top small compost bin and one near the bananas. I placed 2 others near where I have seen the gnats or fruit flies. The only one that caught anything was the one near the bananas. The others have no more than one dead fruit fly and one has none. So, not buying these again. I'll stick with the sticky pads with the blue light.

I am so Disappointed. We have some sort of infestation of gnats, drain flies or fruit flies. I put out jars w vinegar and dish soap and while they were helping, didn't eliminate the problem. Set the Terro traps and only caught a few, whereas my DIY traps caught significantly more. I just ordered a different kind of trap to hopefully end the problem. Bottom line, save your money and skip this item.
Ordered the 6 pack, ended up only getting a 3 pack.

It does its job but it might take a while to accumulate flies. I bought this in the summer when I had lots of fruit flies at home. After installation over the 3-4 days it only accumulated few flies so I had to buy apple traps and those are way more effective, killed a bunch in first 24hr. This thing helped too eventually , but if we split efficiency I would say Apple traps vs this is like 80/20 so get it if you buy it together with those, not just as a sole solution

I really had high hopes for this and saw the positive reviews but I guess my gnats are smarter. The 1st day I noticed that this didn’t work, then I tried putting apple cider vinegar next to the traps and I feel that helped the gnats accidentally fly in to the trap. Next I tossed the light and stuck the sticky sheets directly on the apple cider vinegar cup side and that worked well.

Not a single fruit fly so far, and we have a lot in our house right now. Would not buy this. UPDATE: I originally gave this 1 star. For whatever reason, this did not work in our kitchen where we have the most fruit flies. But it works great in our bathroom. I think it may work better on a white wall because it shines brighter. But I also taped a white piece of paper on the backsplash shown in the attached picture (that’s before I taped the paper) and it still didn’t work great in our kitchen. I think it’s worth the price to try this device, as we also purchased a Zevo and have been disappointed so far.

It hasn't caught anything. However, I haven't seen any flies since I put it up, so I really can't say if it is effective or not. I can tell what the product is. Basically just a light with a very small piece of sticky paper directly behind the light. It sounds good in theory. I'll continue to leave it plugged in and see what happens. I noticed flies in front of my door leading into the house. I don't see anything in the vicinity that would be attracting them. If one makes it into the house I'll see what happens with this product.

I found this product not to be as good as others and I won't be using it again. It is difficult to get the sticky paper into the slot and the blue light doesn't seem to attract as many bugs as other brands do that I have used. Live and learn.

ending Sumer/fall with lots of gnats still. I wish I could say this was 5 stars. I have moved it around kitchen which is only 7x11 feet and still have gnats The trap has caught many gnats but no enough to like this product
It has been working well to catch all the small flying insects, and I’ve even managed to catch a few flies with it.

That’s are sticky to use and easy to get stuck on your fingers. Doesn’t really attract little fruit bugs or flies well unless they just happen to land on them. Probably would not purchase again unless I had a major flying insect issue.

These plug ins weren't attracting the flies so I took another reviewer's advice and put a little sweet wine in a pint glass and laid the sticky cards across the rim of the glass and that worked great! The fruit fly issue was under control within 24 hrs. and eliminated by 72. If I could, I would buy a box of the sticky cards, as the UV plugs were useless for me. I tried using packing tape with the wine/glass trick with no luck. Not sticky enough. Plus, the sticky cards have something that attracts pests.
They do light up. They are sticky. But my fruit flies will not land on them. I don't know why. Returned these and bought another brand, which is working a bit better.

Unlike the larger bug zappers, this one doesn’t seem quite powerful enough to kill most bugs with one zap. And in addition to that, the bugs seem to get zapped multiple times because of its design. With most bug zappers the bugs get zapped once, and they fall into a tray at the base of the zapper. With this bug zapper, they get zapped multiple times as they tumble through its architecture of wires. Often as they are tumbling through its wires, they literally catch on fire and they are often alive through part of this process. So what you end up getting with this product is this cacophony of sparks as an insect tumbles through the wires buzzing loudly for a large part of that time and then as it literally catches on fires and burns, you get this awful smell that you can smell 30 feet away. As I said before with a typical bug zapper the bugs get zapped one time and then they fall to a tray at the base of the zapper and that’s it. There’s no fire, there’s no multiple zaps there’s no ridiculously horrible smell filling the area. All that being said, I started to research bug zappers after the horrible performance of this one and I found out that they don’t even do very well in zapping mosquitoes or biting flies. Those type of bugs just aren’t very attracted to them. They actually end up zapping a lot of moths and other harmless bugs. If you’re trying to trap mosquitoes or flies, there are bug traps and repellents specifically built for them, and I would suggest you get those instead.

This zapper looks nice, but it doesn't work well. The biggest problem is the design. The white plastic rim sticks out farther than the metal part that actually zaps bugs. It's also curved, so when you try to press it against a wall or floor, the zapper part doesn’t even touch the bug. I included a video where I tried to zap a spider on the wall, but the spider kept walking, totally unbothered. I stopped recording just so I could grab my slipper and smack it because the zapper was doing absolutely nothing. This zapper only works if you swing it through the air and hit a bug mid-flight. Even when you do manage to swing and hit something, small bugs like fruit flies usually don’t get zapped and slip through. Super frustrating. Also, the built-in light is useless. It doesn’t attract bugs well. When I’m sitting outside with the zapper nearby, bugs still fly around me and attack my face. TL;DR: Looks nice but not worth buying. Doesn’t do the job and bugs are still terrorizing me.

worked good at first but like other reviews stopped working after two months. the probably do this so you could buy again and earn more money. don't recommend. for $20 it should last for years, garbage.

Bought this one thinking it was the same as the one I bought before but nope. Didn’t even kill one fly so I kept using my old one and am still using my old one that I was trying to replace with this new one.

Definitely will NOT kill a fly on the first hit or with a simple swat. You'll hear an absurdly loud zap, but you'll be lucky if it even stuns them...most of the time they just keep on flying. The only way to kill a fly with this is to trap it against a wall or window so it hits the mesh a few times, or if you're lucky enough to have a fly land on the racket while it's lying flat on a table. You'll need at least 4-5 zaps to actually kill the fly, and you get to enjoy the smell of burning fly at that point. Might be more effective with mosquitoes but for all the noise this thing makes, it's pretty amazing that flies just bounce off it like it's nothing.
They zap good but the light is very dim. Not really bright enough to attract bugs. Reache dout to customer service but no response. Ordered another brand and they're much brighter. Will be returning.

If there were a "no star" review, this one would get none. After trying the product 2 times, with a defective "net" that did not light up BOTH times, it's officially not worth a third attempt. Contacting the company as the instructions said, garnered no response. If you are a gambler, you still may not win with this one. Nope, not going for a third shot.
This item stopped working less than 3 months after my purchase. It could no longer hold a charge and fails to work. Terrible product.

I almost never write reviews for products, but due to the ineffectiveness of this product, I decided to hopefully deter the few that read this from buying. I have literally swatted the same fly with this zapper 5+ times and it does not kill. This happens frequently and the only way I have found to effectively kill the flies are to trap them with the swatter and zap them repeatedly until they smoke. Perhaps these work better on mosquitoes, but if you are buying to kill flies, I recommend avoiding.
Not even a month later the it doesn’t work. It has been fully charged and as soon as I remove from charger it is dead. Waste of a purchase.

I’m appreciative of the fact that I have something ready to catch the fruit flies around my home, though I’ve noticed it only catches the super tiny ones and not the larger fruit flies. Most of the time I unplug it from the wall and wave it over the fly to catch it in its stick pad and that’s how I’ve caught 75% of the insects pictured. Outside of the light, theres nothing really special that draws the flies to the trap so I would think about pairing it with an apple cider vinegar trap to lure them in and hope they get stuck in the trap. The video included is how I catch them manually. It seems seem for animals and children and does not emit any smell. As far as quality, I rate it 1-2 stars as a standalone device, 3 stars is because with my fly catching idea, at least it is catching something. It almost makes me want to buy just the refills and have them on standby to catch on my own. I did expect more out of the quality coming from the Zevo brand. It does not kill the bugs, it just makes them stuck until they die. I’ve had this for about 2 - 3 weeks and this is all it’s caught (small bugs on it’s own, larger flies with my assistance).

I’ve ordered this item twice because it does the job and is much more pleasant than fly tape. 3 stars out of 5 because you’re paying for the light bulb and overall aesthetic. My 2nd delivery arrived in a large plain paper envelope; the product box was somewhat crushed and the device was cracked. The plug was rendered loose but since the bulb was not broken and the overall device still worked I didn’t want to hassle with exchanging it. So it’s an OK solution but is spendy to continually replace, plus depending on packaging, it may or may not arrive damaged.
Though I have a whole bunch of fruitflies near my kitchen sink where the device is. It caught only some of them.

We primarily bought it to catch house flies - We get waves of house flies on occasion so I purchased this - we tried it in 3 different locations in our family room - did not catch a one - we put up fly strips later which are ugly and they worked- We also got quite a few old school with a fly swatter. However since our family room is windows all around maybe the light from the Zevo was not attracting them - we placed the sticky strips near the windows- Back to a positive - The Zevo did catch little gnats? As well as the fly strips put up later.

Maybe a tad overproduced and wasteful but it works well enough in the summer in a home kitchen. Doubles a night light. You may find more effective solutions building your own traps using mason jars (look up a guide)

Just put it out last week and so far am on my second screen so it is attracting the gnats and fruit flys very well BUT when I turn on the kitchen light, they are still swarming. Will it take care of them finally once and for all and, if so, how long does that take? If, when, it does, it would be a five star review.

The attraction and catch rate could be better, but I am grateful for how they fit in my needed locations in each space. Just started using them so the lifespan, durability quality and effectiveness is still in observation.
The trap works as intended. However, we had a lot of fruit flies so the trap wasn’t quite as effective. I ended up needing to buy another trap that had apple cider and vinegar in it.
Light burn out after less than a year.

Light didn't even last a year. I really liked this trap. It worked great through the spring, summer and fall season with all the fruit flies, flies, mosquitoes and even a couple of spiders but then the light died and now it doesn't attract the insects. It's awful pricey for barely 9 months worth of use.

The cute little red plastic apples are a great design. It’s too bad they are paired with lure which does not work. I’ve placed four of the six apples in areas of breeding and concentration. One on the table with plants, one right in front of me on my desk where I work, one by the kitchen sink, and one on the kitchen island next to another plant. They have been out for a couple weeks now. And not a single fruit fly can be seen inside any of the traps! In fact, I’ve already killed two flying round me this morning with the apple trap less than two feet away. Yesterday I found one fruit fly crawling around the window sill in the kitchen mere inches away from the trap. It never did go in the trap! I don’t know if the lure expires or not. It does have a faint vinegary smell. But as these were sold to me, they are absolutely worthless!

Totally garbage item and waste of money. I might as well of just reached In the cupboard and poured apple cider vinegar in a cup. House is totally full of fruit flies and after days this thing has trapped one. Will never buy again, I’ll stick to using zevo spray instead that actually kills on contact. I have it one star for the cute little apple.

The squirt bottle insert worked a lot better than the plastic slider insert. Maybe it was more potent in the squirt bottle insert but its been out for over a day and not a single fly but before I would have multiple flies dead

My oldest son suggested I try these because they worked for him when a condo neighbor left their trash out overnight for early morning concierge pick up. I live up in the mountains on a farm with fruit trees, cattle & lots of land used for gardening. Sometimes when you open the door, you think they must have been lined up just waiting to enter the RV. Annoying little creatures they are. I ordered one set of these, but somehow I must have ordered another since I have two and have tried to cancel one before they got here but it didn't take. I'll keep the one 6 pack, but sending the other back, and not going to keep the subscription unless they start working, at that point I will update. For now, the gnats will fly around it, land on it, but not go in it.

it says 270 days of lure supply but on the box that came it says 90 days of lure supply and theres only 3 little bottles and you fill each one of the holders up with 1/2 the bottle. 270 days of supply is a total lie

This product didn’t work… can easily spill out the liquid and cause stickiness… dont get it, probably call a Plummer cuz if you have allot of fruit flies out of the blue you might have mold and larva….

Bought this expecting a solution to my fruit fly problem. Instead, I’ve accidentally launched the hottest vacation destination for flies on the planet. These traps? Oh, they attract fruit flies alright… not to kill them, but to pamper them. I’m pretty sure they come for the lure and stay for the vibes. I’ve got fruit flies sipping fermented apple juice, hooking up, raising families, and probably opening a gift shop near my sink. I swear one of them made eye contact and winked at me. It’s been a week. They’re multiplying. I’m not even sure I live here anymore—I’m just staff now. Would not recommend unless you're trying to open a 5-star fly resort with 270 days of all-inclusive buffet access.
The product didnt work at all. Not even a single fruit fly is catched. We are totally disappointed with this product.

My two‑star rating reflects an average between the first and second versions of the Zevo trap, because the two products performed very differently. First Version – No Longer Available, and Misleading The original version is no longer listed, so I can’t leave a review specifically for it. If I could, it wouldn’t get more than one star. The main reason I bought it was the claim that it also worked for mosquitoes. After using it for a year and a half, I can confirm that this was completely false. It catches plenty of other flying insects, but never mosquitoes, which made the original purchase pointless for my needs. Second Version – Accurate Description, Works Properly The updated version no longer claims to trap mosquitoes—likely removed after customer complaints. And this time, the product performs exactly as described. For the insects it lists, it works reliably, and I would normally rate this version five stars on its own. Final Rating Logic Because I can’t leave a separate review for the discontinued first version, and because the original misleading claims were the reason I bought the product in the first place, I’m forced to average the experience. I can’t give 2.5 stars, so I’m leaving 2 stars to reflect the accurate performance of the new version and the misleading marketing of the old one.

I gave Zevo these two out of five stars because this trap only catches 2 out of 5 Gnats. I placed two devices where Gnats love to gather. One in the Kitchen, One near the bathroom. I check every day, as I also kill Gnats by hand every day. I have a very clean home; I use a garbage disposal and do not store food that can rot in the kitchen bin. I store all fruit and vegetables in the refrigerator. Yet every time that I leave a door or window open for fresh air, these little critters gather. (Even with screens). I have tried everything. But this was the most expensive and least successful. I will return to flying insect sprays. I do not know what so called "Genius" thought that insects like "blue light." But I do know, he/she is not very intelligent. I also have 2 blue light/ light bulbs, that are also zappers... also no results. My recommendations, keep a clean house, wash and dry all fruit and vegetables and store them appropriately, (never store fruit or any food in open air). And my latest discovery... If you use a citrus dish cleaning liquid, keep the bottle clean and the top tight. It is weird, but I have recently discovered that Gnats are attracted to the artificial scent.

It's basically expensive fly paper. The blue light is supposed to attract flying bugs and it does - but only mildly. The height of fruit fly season runs from August to October. There were so many fruit flies in my house, it was hard to have a conversation without waiving the little buggers away. Enter Zevo - which I have seen being advertised aggressively on TV as being the greatest flying insect eradicator ever. So I shelled out the money for 2 blue lit insect traps and 2 refill cartridges, assembled, plugged in and sat back waiting for the fruit fly fog to clear. It never did. I broke down and added 2 bowls filled with Apple Cider Vinegar, topped with tightly covered saran wrap, poked some tiny holes in the wrap and watched to see what worked better. Scot 1 for the Apple Cider Vinegar. I collected 100x more fruit flies in 2 days than I did the entire time I had the cartridges plugged in. The blue lights are pretty - I'll give it that. But it's passively effective, not aggressively effective, so I'll continue to use my tried and true, albeit kinda gross, method.

It’s gnat season, and after yet another round of vinegar pots, I decided to go high tech with these Zevo plug-in traps. I put one upstairs, one downstairs, and waited for the carnage. In practice? Not much carnage. After a month (and a house so thick with gnats you could smack them out of the air just walking by), I’ve caught… maybe 15. I watch the bugs chilling in the blue light like it’s happy hour, but they refuse to land on the sticky trap. Maybe my gnats are too smart, maybe my walls are extra tasty, or maybe I’m just more attractive than UV. Whatever it is, these things aren’t worth the money or the hassle of a bright blue beacon glowing 24/7 on both floors of my house. To be fair: The design is pretty cool. The sticky refills are easy to replace, and you don’t have to touch the goo. But I could easily catch more bugs with my bare hands in three minutes than these have snagged in three weeks. If the point is to catch bugs, this thing missed the mark. Unless you’re looking to start your own insect sanctuary, I’d keep looking.

Don't work very well in the kitchen, but works in a smaller room like a bathroom, they did not say that in their advertisement and I spend over $65, which is very upsetting, but I am leaving user review it's not a very good product unless you have 7 or 8 of them. Around two hundred feet space

Check the review pictures. It catches a bunch of fruit flies, some midges, a couple of mosquitoes, and in my case, one enterprising moth. But despite having a bunch of houseflies in my house and this plug being very visible for almost two weeks, NO HOUSEFLIES CAUGHT. I had a friend that raved about this catching all their flies. And despite what I considered a high price tag for a disposable-cartridge type product I went for it. Because we have a ton of flies (it's fall and I live in a rural area.) About a week after this has been in my kitchen, I also put up a traditional strip of flypaper which now has a dozen flies on it. Still no houseflies in the zevo. I'm on the verge of saying one star, but I'll give it two because it does catch a bunch of fruit flies that I didn't know we're so bad.

Purchased this over 6 months ago and have tried them in different rooms, areas, and heights, and I don't see them doing anything great. I had them in a spot for over a month and maybe one or two little noes see em bugs land on one by the door. Flies don't go on them I have watched them land or scurry right around it like it had no interest at all to them. Also, the way it is curved like a quarter moon is so close to the wall when you plug it in I don't think any bugs will go behind the cover then jump onto the pad. Most of the time there is never anything on them after a month even. It reminds me of those ways you catch a mouse glue pad type that is put in with a light. I am going to buy the mouse catch pads and put a night light by it. I think I will have better luck with that or a fly bug strip. And they don't last that long before you have to replace the pad and the price for the couple of replacements is way over priced.

My daughter suggested I get this device because she has had very good results at her beach house. However, I do not have a flying insect problem (which my daughter thinks I have since one day I had an over ripe banana on the kitchen counter), or the Zero just doesn't work at at my house. In a week, I've only found two little insects on the sticky thing. It takes up the entire outlet on the backsplash is is awkward to move when I need to use the outlet. I bought an extra set of cartridges, which I assume I won't need anytime soon.

Didn’t work for us. The only reason I gave 2 stars is because it’s at least “sticky.” We had these up for a while and it caught hardly any fruit flies or gnats. I literally took the sticky part off and waved it in the air to catch them. The light didn’t attract the insects at all. These were placed in clear view right behind our fruit basket where we had a ton of the flies, unobstructed. Disappointed for the price.

If you buy this to catch mosquitoes,you are probably not going to be pleased. It does attract tiny flying insects that don't suck blood,however- teensy critters and small moths for example ,are intrigued in, but nothing that buzzes in your ear and terrorizes you nightly, and bite and suck your blood,sadly, these are not seduced by Zevo are fooled

I’m having an issue with fruit flies and I think it’s because I’ve been doing a lot of food prep lately and I have two dogs and leave my patio door open for them to go in and out so I’m trying to figure out where they are coming from because I do my dishes right away The extra star is because it does look very sleek and I like the little light that it has and it looks so much nicer than anything else on the market, but I have shifted it around to four different outlets and I have caught two fruit flies in the past couple weeks… and then I took a piece of tape and looped it around on a popsicle stick and place it in a little glass filled with maple syrup and I caught like 10 more over the past 24 hours so I’m bummed that it didn’t work

Item does work but it is advised that you swab the bait with apple cider vinegar to increase the attractant. Also, unit is plug in and thus might not be movable closer to where bugs might appear. I ordered this on November 26th & received it on November 29th 2025. One fruit fly trap works the other does not. I haven’t noticed any fruit flies or flying insects collected yet. I’ll have to return because the blue light does not work. I tried different outlets.

I really wanted this to work, but unfortunately, it didn’t live up to the expectations. I set up both devices in my kitchen and bathroom where I’ve been having issues with fruit flies, but after several days, there was barely any sign of flies getting trapped. The UV light seems weak, and the sticky pads didn’t catch much of anything. I followed all the instructions and even moved the devices around to different spots, but the results were still minimal. The design is nice and it’s quiet, which I appreciate, but that doesn’t help much when the fruit flies are still buzzing around. Might work better in a completely dark space or with a smaller infestation, but for me, it just didn’t do the job. Disappointed overall.

The blue light does not attract fruit flies. This product is a waste of money. I placed the sticky pad next to open bottle of apple cider vinegar and caught a few fruit flies as they tried to get to the vinegar. Caught more flies in the open bottle of apple cider vinegar than on the sticky pad.

So I've officially had these plugged in for 17 days. (A little over 2 weeks) One in the kitchen and one on the wall between the living room and dining room. My apartment is small. We have/had a gnat problem in our house. First in the kitchen, and then in the bathroom. I bought a bug zapper for the kitchen, which worked well, but scares the heck out of you every time a bug gets zapped. Sam's also had a rotating bug trap that works pretty well. When the gnats moved to the bathroom, there wasn't enough space away from the sink, toilet, or bathtub to put the zapper. I went ahead and bought these traps to plug in there, as well as the spray that Zevo sells. I figured, given the small footprint of the bathroom, the plug-in trap would be perfect. The first night, it caught two gnats, and I thought, "Great, it works!" However, a week later, the same two gnats were in there, and no additional gnats. Another week, same two gnats. For whatever reason, the "night light" lure just didn't bring in any more gnats. I ended up killing the rest of the gnats either with the spray or with a swatter of some type (mostly my hand). Does it work? Sure, yes. Does it work well? Not really, at least not in my case. I did notice that both Sam's and Costco sell these as well, so I'm guessing they have to be popular enough, right?

The what? - It actually works better as a fly swatter catch. Whenever we see a flying insect, we pick this thing up and swipe at it. Works better than just leaving it plugged in 😅 - If that's the case, you are better off wrapping sticky tape and with some of the sticky side up, around a fly swatter at that point.

Although, it does attract bugs, they are few and far between. they attract fruit flies but that's all no good to trap them
Not helpful..
Takes a while to actually kill the flys most dont even go into the container but ill update a lil longer into the use of the product.
The traps are not very good at actually trapping the flies. They sort of just lay on top of the trap and sniff the vinegar from above. Maybe reformulate?

Yes, the product is effective, BUT! watch out when you’re adding the liquid into the little apples - the seals on the bottles are bad. All of them leaked - either slowly onto the top of the apple, or sprayed enough to hit me. I wasn’t squeezing hard either—so just be careful as you’re filling the apples up!

These traps were easy to set up and work great at catching fruit flies. Huge downside is, the concentrate you put in them dried out in about 2 weeks and there's no way to buy concentrate refills. We ended up purchasing a plugin fruit fly trap. The price of the traps is too high for how long they last.

I swear by Terro ant products so decided to try these for fruit flies. Came with no instructions but was easy to figure out. Pour 1/2 bottle of liquid into trap. It attracted fruit flies but it took them a while to go from top of trap into the holes on the trap. Traps stink but that is what attracts the fruit flies. Two were set out in different areas. Both got tipped over spilling smelly contents and dead fruit flies. Would be better if they were weighted instead of lightweight plastic.

I know most people will only read 5 star reviews and 1 start reviews but I have to give these 2 stars because it actually caught a few bugs. The thing is that people buy aftermarket items to save money over the name brand HOPING to get the close to the same quality as the name brand. Sometimes that works out and sometimes it doesn't. Unfortunately, I would have to agree with the 1 star reviews that say these refills are just not that sticky. I bought another aftermarket brand refill for the Z to see which worked better, so I have been carefully comparing them every day or so. Unfortunately, when looking at what was captured by this refill, I watched two little bugs slowly walk across the sticky surface. So, it's just not catching and trapping insects like it should for it to be of any use. The most disappointing thing is that I trusted that it would work as well as the other aftermarket brand and bought the 18 pack for a penny less than $18 and there really isn't any use for these things and I got 18 of them. At this point, I've used one in the test and gave away two more and I'm left trying to decide if it's worth trying to return. I think I will.

Once again read all the great reviews and ordered these.. well - These simply don’t work well compared to the name brand. The picture says it all. The inserts were put in at the same time for 3 weeks close to each other in the same room and the picture is the result. Both units were attracting similar amounts of the moths judging by the amount of moths on the wall by each unit. Maybe these work better for different insects but not with the small moths that get into our house, although the Zevo did a better job for house flys in the garage as well- sorry no pic but looked similar …. Fact is the Zevo brand is much stickier to the touch compared to these off brand versions and so the Zevo catches more. I was hoping to save some money on these silly things but I had to order the Zevo brand again, throw out the off brand as the Zevo caught probably 75-80% more.

They don't work nearly as well as the name brand refills. I barely see any bugs attached to the trap and I've tried several of the refills in the box. It may look like the name brand refill but it certainly doesn't function anywhere close.

Bought these a while ago and just tore into the box about 2 months ago. First cartridge out of the box caught a few moths and gnats. Earlier this week I noticed we had a lot of fruit flies around the kitchen. I looked at the trap cartridge and not a lot of new bugs on it, so today I changed the cartridge to a new one. I did an experiment, and I had a split tomato that I sat next to the outlet the trap was plugged into. After 30 minutes...nothing on the trap. I unplugged the trap and was literally hitting the fruit flies as they were flying and they weren't sticking to the trap. Won't waste my money again, I'll buy the brand name ones from now on.

The refills are much less expensive and less effective than the other brand which I used in the competitions package and holder. I don’t know if they are less attractive to the insects or don’t hold them sufficiently??? I have many still flying around in my house…

The refills fit the Zevo very well, and while they do catch bugs, they are not as sticky and effective as the name brand cartridges. We live in a buggy region, and I hoped to save some on the Zevo refills with this off-brand. Each of 4 Zevos have only caught a few bugs each over the last week (significantly fewer than normally caught), and we're noticing more flying in our home. Just now, my husband watched a drain fly walk across the sticky surface.....obviously not as sticky as name brand. If you perhaps have fewer bugs than we do, this might be adequate and save money. We will have to revert back to the name brand refills.
I’m not sure why, but these don’t work even remotely as well as the originals that came with the device.

I've had my unit plugged in for a week with these replacement refills and have not cut one fly yet. I don't have that many flies in my kitchen but I do have an ugly Pest Strip hanging down not far from this unit and it has caught the three flies that I've had in the past week. So, I invested 20 bucks and I'm not sure whether I threw my money away or not.

It does fit inside the brand name plug in which is why I gave it two stars instead of one. I bought these because they were a better value but will be going back to the brand name because these don't catch anything.
It doesn’t seem to hold the insects as well as the original. The price is cheaper per sticky pad, and you get a good supply of the sticky pad.

I bought both this Baimnocm off-brand refill pack as well as some Zevo name brand refills for my Zevo Insect Traps. I put this Baimnocm off-brand refill out where I knew there were bugs, checked back next morning and there were only two insects stuck to trap. However, I could see numerous bugs walking all over the “sticky” surface then fly away without any harm and definitely not trapped. My initial success with Zevo (including Zevo brand sticky trap portion) was excellent, so I wanted to compare To compare the two refills, I set up a trap with a Zevo name brand refill right next to the trap with the Baimnocm refill. I then watched bugs walking all over all over the Baimnocm “sticky” portion of the trap refill, fly off, land on the Zevo sticky portion of the refill and get stuck! I left them together for a day for a more complete comparison. After 24 hours, the Zevo refill sticky trap had 42 bugs trapped, whereas the Baimnocm refill trap had 3 measly bugs (including the 2 that had been trapped the day before). I read reviews where others had success with the off-brand refills and reviews where people had problems with the off-brand refills. Perhaps the off brand Baimnocm refills are just terribly inconsistent in quality—my Baimnocm trap refill batch certainly was poor quality. But my experience with the Zevo products has been that they work great every time. I’m sticking with the Zevo name brand refills. They may be more expensive, but the work well and consistently. The Baimnocm refill really didn’t even work— I think that’s more expensive, paying good money for something that just doesn’t deliver.

SUCKS....TOTAL WASTE OF BOTH TIME & MONEY. Not sticky enough...it has 1 job and cannot do it!!! I ordered these as a less expensive alternative. I want to SLAP myself....serioooously. Slap myself HARD!! Due to a rather unkept next door ex-neighbor who when moving out caused an exodus plague level swarm of fruit flies to invade my apartment(long-story), I began investing in ZEVO traps. At one point it was sooooo bad that I had to wear face masks to prevent them from flying up my nose or in my mouth. I was forced to eat every meal out--trust me it was necessary.... it's been absolute crazyyyyness!!! However the ZEVO traps were a God send..They were amazing!! I would put a trap down and by the next morning have to replace it..fully covered from end to end. Unfortunately I soon got tired of spending $4.50 per trap, $50-60 dollars every other day(for the last 3 weeks). It's clear they cost less than $0.10 to manufacture so paying such a crazyyyy set price was ridiculous, hence these guys. The box arrived yesterday.. Long story short...I have spent the last hour watching the majority of the flies land on the sticky area just to walk themselves over to the gray plastic part...and take off to fly again COMPLETELY UNHARMED. Only the tiniest flies (infant stage ones) are sticking......such a WASTE!!! I have an instacart order on the way for more ZEVO as I type this review. Trust me, don't waste your time or money. Invest in the ZEVO's. ***I loaded some photos of the traps at my regular refill time. There are the last 3 ZEVOs that I put out along side 3 of these guys...same length of time. Now it is true they were in different areas however with the number of pest that I am combating.....please! There is noo excuse, Smh. I'm looking for a refund. Fingers crossed!

I bought these when I ran out of the Zevo refills, trying to save a little money. Boy, was that a mistake!! The Zevo machine and refills work great! Living in the south, whenever you bring in fresh fruits and vegetables, you will inevitably get what Southerners call drunkards (aka small fruit flies). The Zevo machine and refills took care of them very quickly. However, when I purchased these knockoffs to save some money, they did nothing at all. I received these on June 5th, and immediately put one in, replacing the Zevo refill that was covered in dead bugs. It has been in my machine for almost 6 weeks, and has literally caught only 4 bugs! DO NOT WASTE YOUR TIME OR MONEY ON THIS PRODUCT!! They don’t work and I’m stuck with 15 of them!!

My garden has been overflowing with tomatoes and summer squash and my counters were accumulating bowls of produce faster than I could can or freeze them. Suddenly I had an infestation of fruit flies. Prior to using ZEVO, I was using an old home remedy for trapping fruit flies. I placed a piece of banana in a jar, covered the opening with plastic wrap, and punched a couple of slits in the plastic wrap. In a second jar I placed pieces of tomato. The fruit flies can get into the jar through the slits but can’t get out. My ZEVO order arrived two days after I set out the jars and I eagerly put the ZEVO traps to work. The next morning I checked the traps. The ZEVO traps had only 5 or 6 fruit flies attached to the sticky trap. The jars which had been sitting out for 2 days, had a dozen or so fruit fries and my counters were still full of flies. The next morning, the ZEVO traps had a few more flies but the jars were full of flies. Now, four days later, the ZEVO traps continue to underperform. I replaced the banana with a little vinegar and it’s like a fruit fly magnet.

So far I am not at all impressed. Not sure how we got them but all of a sudden we had a ton of fruit flies. (Despite no fruit out and rotting. Nothing we could find. Unless they were in the drain….i have no clue where they came from) We did a kitchen and fridge deep clean. We did drain cleaning. Garbage can bleaching. Etc. Bought these. Plugged one in in out kitchen. A few days later…nothing. Not one single fruit fly was caught. Opened and plugged in the second one as well. Two days later….still nothing. I killed more with my hands than these caught. I gave up and called my mom and ended up doing the old wives take ACV and dish soap with plastic wrap and holes in it over it. I have 10 over night that way. Two days later they are all dead in my ACV trap. Too bad these $35 traps couldn’t have caught and killed a single one. Maybe they work better with different bugs than fruit flies but….they absolutely did nothing for us.

Many reviewers praise this fly trap and have had success with it, but my experience has been that the fly trap is very ineffective. I have numerous house flies in my condo, but they ignore the Zevo fly trap and do not seem attracted to the UV light. I have tried placing the fly trap right where the flies are, tried dimming the lights and darkening the room to make the UV light more visible, and have tried moving the trap around to several different locations. None of these approaches work. At the moment, there are approximately 10 flies in my small condo. They fly around and near the trap, but ignore it and do not seem attracted to it at all. For me, this was a fairly expensive investment to help control the flies. I was optimistic it would help based on all the positive reviews, but it did not work for me. I'm already calling it a loss and looking for another alternative.

It’s not exactly what it represents is in the box, at least the one I got. Now my box had been torn open, so I may be wrong. Yes there was 2 traps, but no pads were in them, so the 2 “refill pads” actually should be a part of the traps, so no refill pads are part of the package!

I’d give it 0 Stars if possible. I’ve had one plugged in one foot from a bowl I keep onions, avocados and other various vegetables from time to time and not one single fruit fly or any other flying insect has found its way too. My roommate and I have each killed a handful during this time. We have another in my bathroom where I am constantly finding a tiny flying insect and it has also not attacked a single one in 10 days!! It is a complete waste of money!!!

Over the course of three days using these in hopes of dealing with an infestation of gnats, the one in the bathroom caught a gnat and a mosquito. Then, someone suggested I simply put some apple cider vinegar in a glass, with a drop of Dawn dish soap, rubber band a cover of plastic wrap and punch some holes in it. I did this in both the bathroom and the kitchen. In two days in the bathroom, I caught six gnats. In the same period in the kitchen, I caught more than I could count - probably 50 or more. The flying insect traps are still plugged in but haven't caught any additional gnats (not too many around since I put out the apple cider vinegar.

We have lots of gnats flying around in our house. Though this would be better than a bug bomb. We bought the two pack. 2 days and not a single catch. Situation: Fruit flies had gotten out of hand in the kitchen and other parts of the house. Tried other home remedies with mixed results Got the Zevo and well…i’s a nice night light. That’s it. Over the course of around three weeks, sitting in my kitchen, it caught and killed 4-5 fruit flies. It was easy to assemble and set up, but it just didn’t work as expected and especially in regards to price point. Save your money. I was using one of those Terro Apples and a Raid Yellow fly stick (with apple cider vinegar poured in the bottom of it) and this is the order of effectiveness: 1) Raid yellow stick is the best for fruit flies. 2) Terro Apple is effective over the long haul but if you need to kill quickly, it’s Raid. 3) Zevo is shiny and neat looking and it’s a decent night light but it only killed around 3-4 fruit flies and did that mostly at night.

Had a fruit flie issue. These did not work on them. Had it up for 2 1/2 weeks right by where they were. Didn’t trap a single one. Just pulled out and put in the draw. Made a home remedy that I found on the internet-!: that work way better.

This product did not work as expected . I was very disappointed the first few day no one Nat was in that tape and it was plugged into the outlet right above my problem area .. Vinegar and dish soap concoction was much more effective. Packing tape would probably be as effective at a fraction of the price. Will not purchase again.

We have a total infestation of fruit flies and in the couple weeks we’ve had it it’s only collected a small amount. Pics show what it looks like after weeks of use. I was so excited to get this due to the positive reviews and I was being invaded by small flying insects that bite. After following the included directions and placing the device in the outlet at the end of the kitchen island directly across from the kitchen sink with the trash enclosed underneath. The device has trapped maybe 6 insects at most while I sit on the couch about 6 feet away being swarmed by theses little insects that ARE NOT mosquitoes.

Edit: I plugged it into a charge port that was USBC PD capable and it seems that it didn't negotiate it's power level down because in about 30 seconds, the zapper had become the zapee.. It made a horrible smell and got *extremely* hot... Surprisingly it was still working for a couple of days after, but that charge was the last charge it would ever take. I'm lucky I didn't plug it in and leave or it would have almost certainly caught fire and possibly burned the house down. Aside from that they're pretty good. I'm probably going to buy another to replace it and just be concious of where and how I charge it. I'll probably charge it outside or in the all cement (even the ceiling) garage from now on. I have put a serious dent into the fly population in just a few days now... The rackets recharge nicely with USB-C and if you like, you can just leave them on all the time with the charge wire in and they'll stay charged and keep zapping thing constantly. That said, they don't seem to attract flies with their light very well, but I have seen very few things that do. They do occasionally get something on their own though. The battery life seems fantastic so far. I don't really know what it is because I've never seen them get below a full charge indicated since their initial charge, but I've only had them off of the charger and powered up for about 1/2 an hour or so before, so I really don't know... but they seem like they can't be too bad. I'm sure if someone tried hard enough they'd manage to zap themselves with the racket, but I've intentionally touched the screen to see if their claim was true or not, and I was not zapped... I'd say it would probably be really common to zap yourself through incidental/accidental contact. I'm sure that makes it a *little* bit harder to zap a fly out of the air, but it's definitely not hard to do. If you manage to get them in the area of the screen then they're gonna get zapped... All around I was nicely surprised with how well these work.

Contrary to implications in the manufacturers description *and* some of "impartial" reviewers' videos, the "fly swatter" is NOT a swatter. I can't bring it down on a fly like a swatter because the rim prevents contact of the electrified screen with whatever surface the fly is on. Nor does it catch any flies when I wave it slowly, like one of the reviewers claims. If a fly finds it's way to the screen, it will be stunned, possibly electrocuted. But I still get lots of flies inside my house, even though I leave two of these plugged in their stands (NOT chargers, though they might look that way in photos) in the kitchen and living room. Why didn't I return them? I had used them a few times and I can't be sure other brands will be different.

These work fine, but the charge port in in the racket. The old ones used the base to charge and this was much better. If I see a mosquito, I want to grab the racket quickly. When the charger was in the base, I could just grab it. With the chord plugged into the side, I have to carefully unplug it first and those 3-5 seconds have meant many mosquitoes have gotten away. Update: after months of use, these don't attract mosquitoes like the old ones did. The light is more blue and my old one that worked well was purple.

We have these little bugs that fly paper doesn’t seem to work with and they’re multiplying somewhere, but I can’t figure out where. It also crackled for a day the first time I plugged it in Even though there were no flies near it, and that was somewhat concerning whether we leave it plugged in at night when we’re sleeping. it’s worth mentioning that they do not provide the Base that they require in order to charge their device efficiently. And none of the ones I had were weak enough so we had to source one. Keep that in mind when you’re considering your budget. Also, the battery does not stay charged for very long so the thought of having it out on the porch with you while you’re enjoying a night with your family or at the campsite etc., is pretty much BS unless you’re planning to charge it every couple of hours or an extension cord, which defeats the purpose in my opinion

Buyer BEWARE if you have a dog in your home. Nice features. I like the glow/always on mode to act as one of those electrified light traps, but haven’t seen yet how effective this mode is. Don’t know if the designers had to lower the voltage for safety reasons, the ones I had while growing up in Asia always killed flies with the first zap. But with this I gotta chase the fly around and get 4-5 zaps in before it’ll finally stop flying. Returning it.

I tried 3 different brands of liquid fruit fly attractant: in this home for 25 yrs but had never had a fruit fly problem in my life, they must have come in w/fruit which we don't buy a lot of: found out one female lays 500 eggs...This product, Super Ninja Fruit fly traps- I put several of these pre-filled bottles out for days but SUPER NINJA FRUIT FLY TRAPS DID NOT CATCH A SINGLE FRUIT FLY, compared to Terro brand used in the red plastic tomatoes, which worked well. I even tried the Super Ninja bait in the red Terro plastic tomatoe containers as some ads claimed the color + shape of the vessel played a role in attracting bugs but still no luck. The excellent Terro brand even worked well in a clear glass baby food jar. Terro works well, I see several of the fruit flies in the liquid. However, the tomatoe shaped plastic holders can tip easy, its a sticky, stinky liquid for which the company in South America would not tell me if it has a percent of poison in it- many of their products do, + they only give the number of poison control, + insist that the formula is 'proprietary information'. But after trying 3 brands, will stick w/Terro even though it does not sell a large container of liquid (I do not need more plastic tomatoe holders plus glass baby food jars worked just as well) Have put the Terro liquid bait-filled plastic tomatoes in clear plastic cups (clear so insects could see the red fruit shape), as the tomatoes have many preferations, which is good, but they are light-weight, tip + spill easily. However, the flat rectangular shape of the bottles of the Super Ninja Fruit fly Traps, though the liquid they came with is useless, the bottles w/their flat walls can be attached to any flat surface or wall w/a sticky strip; That had been my intent when I ordered them so I did not have to worry about containers spilling or space to put them down in or my cat getting to one... As the Ninja contents have proved useless, I will empty + rinse them out so I can fill them w/Terro attractant. will report if the insects will use these glass containers. While I had planned to take advantage of the flat walls of the Ninja glass containers + a photo in the Ad may have suggested they could be stuck to a wall, I was surprised that they came w/a pre-attached silicone sticky strip pre-attached for that purpose (one should use a spatula to remove the sticky strip and bottle intact from the wall (they are silicone w/adhesive + residue can be easily removed). There was one other brand of 25 vials which also did not catch a single fruit fly- even when used in Terro's tomatoe shape, which I also do not recommend, 'Qualirey 20 Pack Fruit Fly Trap Refill Liquid'... Do not recommend the Super Ninja Fly Traps because the bait does not work, at least for my fruit flies in Connecticut; but I still need to substitute Terro liquid bait in their their rectangular glass containers a+ see if the insects will then use them before I formally recommend the containers themselves- they have a yellow funnel on top. However, $39.99 plus tax is a high price to pay for the 12 glass containers which I will need to empty out to use with Terro brand liquid- if the bugs will use that container w/Terro bait.

Even the cheap light sticky trap is phenomenal compared to these bottles.

I recently went out of town for a week and left the wife on her own. We already had a couple fruit flies when I left (maybe 3-5 that I noticed), but this isn't unusual since we always buy fruit as it comes into season and this brings fruit fly eggs into the house. Before I left I just reminded her that the trap needed to be setup and she said it wouldn't be a problem. By trap, I mean the apple shaped trap you can get at just about any store where you put a little vinegar and dish soap in and it just "makes the problem go away." I have used those traps for years and they work great, I just put one on the table near the fruit bowl and one near the kitchen sink. Well, I get home and it seems the wife stopped doing dishes and taking out the trash for a week and and fruit flies had gone ham in the sink. Absolute infestation. Swarms plume from the sink when you approach it. The little apples couldn't keep up. So I figured I would try something new. The new UV trap has been setup for 4 days/3 nights. The trap is plugged in right next to the kitchen sink and all the lights are off at night so this trap has had the best chance of succeeding. It has caught, a disappointing number of flies. Maybe 15-20 in that number of days when the number in the house is multiple hundred. I actually just before writing this unplugged it and waved it around over the sink and caught 4 time more in 30 seconds waving this thing around than I have in 4 days. This is probably fine as a very minor preventative, but it costs way more than the little apples, is far more noticeable and not any more effective. I do not recommend.

Look, the idea of trying to get rid of those annoying fruit flies makes my skin crawl and my blood boil at the same time. I hate them. Most insect such as those I despise. But nothing I hate more than fruit flies. This product took AGES to catch even a couple of them, let alone the thousands that are made per day sometimes. If any of you reading this have had them, you know what I mean. They are obnoxious. Here is the thing: the quicker you kill them off, the better. This will not do it, not even close. Left them on for a month and when I went to just toss them in the garbage, I could literally count the fruit flies and other things that somehow found there way behind this to get stuck. Here is how you get rid of fruit flies MUCH QUICKER and for WAY LESS MONEY: Apple cider vinegar + ramekin or other small/medium container + clear plastic wrap + toothpick. Fill half of the ramekin with the vinegar, stretch film over the top. Poke little holes in the saran wrap. Place near infected area. After a day or two, you will be unable to count how many of those little ******* are dead in there. Rinse and repeat when it starts to get too full. I have probably killed millions with this little death pit. These are a gimmick, stay away. Especially for fruit flies. Not sure how well they work for mosquitos.

This doesn’t even deserve 1 star. It’s officially been 24hrs since it was delivered and plugged in. It has been in my kitchen, directly next to the fruit that sits on the counter. I currently have an INSANE amount of fruit flies due to “someone” leaving an opened applesauce in the pantry…. I did a test. I put a small medicine cap filled with apple cider vinegar and Dawn dish soap in a separate portion of my house, where I have seen the least activity of fruit flies. The kitchen area is over run with fruit flies. The photos speak for themselves. For fun, I even took a piece of cardboard and did a layer of Elmer school glue right after I plugged in the blue light. Again, the photos speak for themselves. You see how many fruit flies were trapped by the Elmers glue makeshift trap, in the short time it took to dry, compared to this $20 piece of junk. I truly don’t understand how this has so many great reviews. Photo 1 is the small cap filled with ACV and Dawn (the cloudiness are drowned fruit flies). Photo 2 is the school glue, photo 3 is this product 🙄🤨 I will be returning this!

I recently purchased the ZEVO Fly Trap after reading numerous glowing reviews that touted its effectiveness in catching flies. With summer in full swing and the usual influx of pesky insects, I was eager to try out this product. Unfortunately, my experience has been far from the exceptional results I had hoped for. Performance: Over the course of a week, I placed the ZEVO Fly Trap in a strategic location where I typically see a lot of fly activity. To my disappointment, it only caught one fly during that entire time. I had expected a much higher catch rate based on the reviews I had read, which made me question the reliability of those testimonials. Expectations vs. Reality: Given the rave reviews, I anticipated a significant reduction in the fly population in my home. Instead, I found myself swatting flies as usual, with the trap seemingly doing little to help. It’s puzzling how others have had such positive experiences while mine was lackluster at best. Conclusion: While the ZEVO Fly Trap may work for some, my experience has been disappointing. I had high hopes based on the exceptional reviews, but the results did not match the hype. If you’re considering this product, I recommend tempering your expectations and perhaps exploring other options if you’re dealing with a significant fly problem. For me, it’s back to the drawing board in the search for an effective fly control solution.

I am underwhelmed. In FIVE days it only caught THREE flies. The old fashioned ugly-ass fly strips that are coated with the sticky attractant are just a few dollars for a pack of ten that will last several months and they work better than this useless contraption. Very disappointed. All those 5 stars Amazon reviews must be AI generated? Right now I am sitting on my couch in my living room and there are 8 flies buzzing around me driving me CRAZY. Flies are strongly attracted to carbon dioxide that humans breathe out. They will land on us and eat the dead skin cells and sebum on our skin. No matter how much you swat them away they keep coming back. This gadget looked impressive - the blue light is supposed to attract the fly into the sticky trap BUT THAT DOES NOT WORK except in a very minimal ineffectual manner. Seriously! For what this costs to only catch THREE flies in FIVE days is unacceptable. I did better putting out a dish of vinegar with sugar as an attractant but put a tbsp of Dawn dish soap in the bowl - that attraxts the fly and they drown. I'm sending it back for a full refund. I also bought a box of refills and that goes back to Amazon. BADLY DONE! 1 star is too high a rating

Completely useless. I set it up on the most infested area of the kitchen. Left it there for two weeks. Checked the trap, only one fly on the sticky stuff. One!!! Back to my own homemade waaay cheaper traps of apple cider vinegar and a drop of dish soap. The flies love it! Don't waste your money on this.

DO NOT waste your money on this product. I have had two of these since September. The picture is after 6 months. There is nothing but cat hair. I always have an issue when I bring home fruit. I usually wash it but that still does not guarantee no gnats. The best way to get rid of gnats is this. I get a quart bottle of cider vinegar. I put 2tbs of sugar, and 5 drops of dish detergent in the bottle. The detergent changes the viscosity of the mix so once they get in they can't get out. Put a piece of saran wrap on the top and hold it in place with a rubber band. Poke 3 or 4 holes in the top and leave it next to the source or close to the area that is infested with gnats. I thought these would be great as my wife hates the vinegar smell. But they do nothing. It's a great night light, but I have fairy lights for night lights. Don't waste your time or money on this product if you are trying to kill gnats. I don't know how this product works with mosquitoes, as I have no mosquito problem in the house.

DO NOT waste your money…doesn’t work! Had it plugged in for a 2 weeks during the height of fruit fly & pantry moth season. The flies/moths were all over my kitchen. Plugged it in near the basket & didn’t attract even one fly or moth. Put out a Dr Killigan’s trap before bed time; when I got up the next morning the trap was plastered with the flies & moths.

Do Not Recommend - Poor Product Performance & No Refund Policy I’m giving this product the lowest rating possible. The product completely failed to deliver on its promised results, which was disappointing enough. However, what made this experience worse was the company’s refund policy. When I attempted to return the product because it didn’t work as advertised, I was told that no refund would be issued *because* I had tried using it. This creates an impossible situation - you can’t know the product doesn’t work without trying it, but once you’ve tried it, you’re apparently stuck with it. I strongly advise others to avoid this product and this company. Save your money and look for alternatives from brands that stand behind their products and have fair return policy!

So far it doesn't seem to be working at all. Have been using it for 2 weeks. I have it positioned by the sink where plenty of little gnats are and I think it has caught 2 gnats and that's it. I know we have a lot more than that in the house right now so I would not recommend this.

Put these around our office as we have a lot of trash trucks on site. They barely killed any fruit flies compared to other more potent liquid traps. Sure the mainstream ones have a strong vinegar smell, but they also worked a million times better then these tiny things. Never buying these again

Pros: the liquids scent to me wasn't overpowering, the size of the bottle makes it easy to overlook if you don't want it to be noticed by others, and I like the stick on part on the back of the bottle for a quick and easy install. Cons: Biggest problem I found was it only caught a few fruitflies despite having installed it right above my open lid trashcan. The adhesive part doesn't work on every surface unless you don't mind having to scrape it off. Due to the fact that I bought a four pack instead of a two pack to test out, I ended up spending too much on a product that does very little. The only reason it isn't power than two is because it did catch some of the flies and didn't smell abnormal. Next time, I'll stick with the Terro Fruit Fly Traps instead for it performed with better results.

I thought that maybe the elements were old, so I ordered a fresh set of elements. I replaced those, and nothing since. On the meantime, I have killed 15-20 flies the old-fashioned way (swatter). put it in the holder in a kitchen filled with fruitflies. turned it on. removed the cover so the sticky part faced the light. and..... nothing. not a single bug on it after a wk. they are still buzzing around. colorblind?

I bought these along with the other brand to see if they worked better than the homemade traps, they do very good catching them but the problem is the holes are way too big. My problem got worse as they continued to breed and I could not find out how I poured boiling hot water down my drains everything was clean until last night I noticed upstair's in the bathroom they were going in and out of the trap especially the large center hole and there was larvae in it. I threw all traps away and am using my diy traps and the issue is already getting better. Search diy fruit fly traps and you will have much better luck I like to rinse out and drill small holes and I mean small not like this companies in 2 liter bottles along the top sides and the cap and put apple cider vinegar with a drop of dish soap and some sugar in it if you have some fermented juice they love it too basically anything fermented you can add to the mix. It also helps to wrap or stick sticky traps around the traps to catch even more. Be aware they breed in house plant soil (how I got mine but I've sense taken them outside), drains, trash cans and keep everything clean you can also pour bleach down drains. This company is just trying to get you to keep buying these things because you can't reuse them they just keep breeding and you just keep buying more it's a never ending cycle even though your killing them they just multiply. I can't speak for the other company that sells a similar product but I think the same thing happens with that.

Absolutely. Turning the sarcasm dial to *unreasonable*: --- I am currently living in an active infestation. I have no idea where these creatures came from or how they managed to establish a thriving civilization so quickly, having deep-cleaned my room and offering absolutely no food, water, or emotional support to these bugs. And yet, they have moved in and declared sovereignty. In a panic, I bought several traps. This one now lives on my work desk, because nothing says “professional” like taking meetings while insects launch themselves into your face like kamikaze pilots. The trap smells VILE, like rotting fruit and decay. I can smell it the moment I enter the room. The bugs are clearly obsessed with the scent, but instead of going inside the trap like advertised, they just loiter around it like it’s a sketchy bar. After more than 24 hours, one trap caught **five** bugs. The other caught **none**. Meanwhile, a different brand trap elsewhere has racked up a body count, which confirms this product’s only real function is making my room smell awful. Would not buy again unless I decide my room needs to smell like death **and** still have bugs.

So I purchased the Terro fruit fly traps based on all the positive reviews. They arrived promptly and was very excited to try them at our ice cream and smoothies store. We sometimes have 1-2 fruit flies but not very often. Either way doesn't look good, no customer wants to be bother by little pesky flies. We keep everything in the store spotless, but we'll still get a couple of those little visitors from time to time. I placed 2 traps and after a week, zero got caught. I then added the other 2 traps that came in the package. It has been a few more days since that, and still none has fallen into the trap. They liquid in them smells 100% like vinegar so that is the predominant smell in the room / area where the traps are placed. Looks like I'll be trying a different product because as I said, this one did not work for us at all.

DOES NOT WORK. I have a gnat infestation in my indoor office plants. Because they are in my office, where I spend 8 hours per day, I needed them gone, stat. I tried 3 different methods of gnat elimination: (1) Terro apple-shaped fly traps; (2) Shallow dishes of liquid dish soap; (3) Zevo plug-in flying insect trap. After 3 days, here are the results: (1) Terro apple-shaped fly traps: Easy to set up and fill with their "special" gnat-attracting formula which I am sure is just apple cider vinegar. The apple containers are small and cute. But, they don't work. I CAUGHT 1 (ONE) GNAT using 4 different traps placed on top of soil in my plant containers. (2) Shallow dishes of dawn dish soap: Easy to set up and fill. However, the liquid soap starts to dry out and harden after several days. They are a bit of a pain to wash out and refill, icks me out, plus I think it's a waste of dish soap. I CAUGHT 50 GNATS using this method--I used 4 different shallow containers placed on top of soil in my plant containers. (3) Zevo plug-in flying insect trap. This plug-in fly trap was super easy to set up. It's basically a blue light that attracts the insects and super sticky paper (comes with replacement inserts) to trap them. I CAUGHT 40 GNATS using this method. This trap is the furthest from my plants--it's about 6 feet away. If I move it closer, it will probably work even better. This is my favorite scent-free, mess-free method that actually works. If I didn't use the other 2 methods, I would have about 100 more gnats still flying around my office space, ick. Save your money and don't buy.

Two weeks of use, ZERO dead gnats. If you have fungus flies / gnats, this product won't help. Fruit flies seem to be attracted to the Apple cider vinegar and soap concoction, but overall not impressed for use against "fungus flies" (*aka tiny black flying bugs that buzz randomly around your face...). Would not recommend for this type of invasion. Next step will be throwing away all the house plants and starting over.

I've been using this product successfully for a couple of years. This year was an exception. The product had its sinus-clearing vinegar smell, but the fruit flies weren't buying it. I am not exaggerating -- I caught a single fruit fly in a month. I'm writing this review to encourage you to ask for a replacement product ASAP if you're not catching anything in the first couple of days. In the past, there are multiple fruit flies floating in the apple within the first 24 hours and, at minimum, multiple fruit flies congregating on the surface of the "apple." Or did they change the formula this year? Maybe I received a bad batch? Who knows. Until next summer when the fruit flies come back.

worthless. save your money. 1. The liguid solution that's supposed to last 180 days is not resealable, but is a twist off cap top. Once the cap top is removed there is no way to reseal it. 2. the product doesn't work. Caught no flies in 48 hours even though I deployed it in 4 locations in my house 3. Product is not eligible for returns Buy at electric fly zapper instead. They work great

Completely ineffective. I followed the instructions exactly and placed the traps near fruit and trash areas, but they did nothing to reduce or eliminate fruit flies. After giving them plenty of time, there was no noticeable improvement at all. Unfortunately, this product was a waste of money for me and did not work as advertised.

I was very dissatisfied with this product. It did not attract nor catch the flies. The fluid they give you isn't very much and it evaporates quickly - like a little over a week. I will not buy this product again. I have tried other products and should have stuck to just purchasing them, but I thought, "How cute, a little apple to sit on my counter.

Would Rate a zero if I could. Do not waste money on this product. Does not work. Did not catch a single gnat I installed zevo plug in insect traps instead and very quickly have captured thousands ofgnats around around my home. Apparently this Terro product is not returnable or refundable.

In all fairness, I bought the main Zevo plug-in piece from a box store, because it only had one sticky sheet. My wife and I wanted to try it before we invested in any of the refills. We plugged it in to the garage outlet, about 3 ft from the door into the house. We left it there almost 2 weeks. In that time, it caught 3 (yes, three) flies, 1 wasp and a bunch of gnats. My wife contacted Zevo and complained about the poor performance. They said it should really be plugged in in the kitchen, as the flies are (supposed to be) attracted to the blue light. Not quite sure why you would want to draw flies to the kitchen, where the food is prepared. Anyway, we changed the sticky insert and plugged it in beside the kitchen sink. Nearly 3 (yes, three) weeks later, it had caught one (yes, 1) fly. The other flies killed in the kitchen had to be hunted down with a flyswatter. Seems to be OK with gnats, but beyond that it's a waste of money. We do not recommend.

Product is great but, once again, it was lucky to make it here in one piece. Save your $. Buy the red terro traps. They are very effective, do not require a plug in. Cheaper. This one only works if the main source of your fruit flys is near an outlet. So there were a bunch on the floor near the cat's food. I had an outlet close to there. The Zevo worked well there. But while I had fruit flys elsewhere, Zero did not pick up many because I did not have a concentration of them elsewhere. On the other hand, the little red tomato looking things could be moved around easily and literally hundreds could be caught in those things. Many more than the outlet ones.

These do not work for fruit flies. I got them for my kitchen. After a few days I noticed the plug in didnt have any flies in it.
