Okay. Last I reviewed these or similar as I have used both Zevo lamps and stickers and off-brand lamps and stickers. I’ve used off-brand stickers in Zevo and I’ve used Zevo stickers in off-brand lamps. Results as follows….. No matter what you use, brand name or non-brand name, or in any combination, this product is 4 stars for fruit flies / gnats and about 2.75 - 3.0 stars for any other flying insects. It attracts and catches all - mosquitoes, flies, gnats / fruit flies, and other unknown flying insects however, gnats are its specialty. Clearly, my personal experience is stated here. I have a 2 story with about 1,000sf on the ground floor. With 2 units in the kitchen, 2 around my plants, and another in the bedroom I have a flying bug free environment. I use and highly recommend any. Although I have noticed side-by-side the Zevo lamps are a bit brighter but I can tell no difference in the amount of insects trapped with off-brand. Also, as stated (at least by Zevo) they work best when entire rooms can be darkened for the lamp to have the most effect. I suppose lastly - don’t know recommended change interval but I go 6 months before change and have no problems with diminished capacity for trapping.

I am giving these Zevo refills a 3-star rating because, frankly, they do not perform as powerfully as the product claims. I have several Zevo traps scattered all over the house, and while they do catch some flying insects, it’s not the strong, decisive attraction I was expecting. The traps are placed strategically, but the performance is consistently underwhelming—it's definitely not a strong enough deterrent or attractor to effectively reduce the overall number of gnats, mosquitos or flies. If you need a powerful solution, this isn't it. They are okay, but for the price and the description, I expected much better results.

Zevo is big and bulky, but works good. Works great manually. But does NOT work at all in auto mode - I meant Not at all. Better to get one with manual feature only.

The Super Ninja Fruit Fly Traps are just okay. They do catch some fruit flies, so they aren’t completely ineffective, but they don’t attract nearly as many as I expected. They’re easy to set up and they do work to a degree, just not as strongly as the product description or other reviews made it seem. About a month ago I suddenly and inexplicably noticed dozens of tiny little bugs flying around in my kitchen. Absolutely disgusting. Neurotic cleaning ensued, and, eventually, the flies dissipated, but not as quickly as I would have liked. I decided to purchase some fly traps to put my thumb on the scale. These light traps appeared to be working at first. They were definitely catching some flies, but the total number of flies was not visibly decreasing. Then I noticed that the flies stuck on the trap looked different than the ones still flying around. The light was definitely attracting one species of fly, but another species seemed completely unaffected. They were a lighter color, whereas the flies in the trap were darker. After a few days, the darker flies were gone, and the lighter flies appeared to have expanded their population to take advantage of their absence. So anyways, I suppose whether these traps work for you will depend on what species of fly you’re dealing with. For me, these traps were ineffective. I then purchased some of those liquid bait traps that look like tiny little apples, and they worked incredibly well. The liquid comes in a tiny little bottle and cannot be purchased separately, which is asinine, but apple cider vinegar with a little dish soap appears to work just as well as the bait that comes with the trap. I tried using apple cider vinegar with dish soap in a DIY trap before purchasing some traps, but this was not effective. I think it was too difficult for the flies to get to the bait. The premade traps with DIY bait appears to be the best of both worlds.

I don't know how to rate this product. I had a pretty significant fruit fly infestation for about a week. I cleaned and dried and moved anything resembling food into the fridge. The fruit flies persisted. They were even in my junk mail bin! I bought this, plugged it in, and... you guessed it, the fruit flies continued to hang around! On day one of use, the device caught 2 fruit flies, and on day 2, both fruit flies were gone! No longer stuck to the sticky backing! And more so odd, the infestation ceased! Fruit flies no where to be found! Perhaps they are scared of the devices appearance, or maybe the two escapees informed the general community that a dangerous trap had been installed? I don't know. To require the customer buy refills that are in a box with only 2 refills per box is asking a bit much. If one were able to buy 10 thin layers of resin with just a thin layer of resin one could then pull off and attach to the original it would be so much more sustainable. A window seals we put one in is COVERED in gnats, NOT ONE IN THE CONTAINER. Also have one on my desk, NOT ONE IN THE CONTAINER, but several in my water cup. Total waste of money!!!! 6 containers and not one gnat in any of them. Would like to add: a co worker told me to lean it up against a window, within a day we caught three gnats. Same co worker brought in a bottle of grape juice and caught about 50 in that day. She put a lid on it, poked some holes and it was like magic juice.
Cute little apples but they do not work at all. They are sitting around looking cute while fruit flies ignore it.

We’ve had this for about a week and a half now. Our house has a ton of flies in it. So far this has only been able to catch about two Nats in one fly. We’ve been having to swap most of the flies out of the sky. The ZEVO looks nice but it hasn’t been super effective in our house.

I was really hoping this would help with the gnat situation in my kitchen, but honestly, it didn’t do much. I left it plugged in 24/7 for days—right in the kitchen where the little bugs hang out—and it only caught a few here and there. Definitely not enough to justify the hype. I figured the light would attract more, but it just… didn’t. Kind of surprised honestly, especially seeing how many glowing reviews this thing has. Maybe I got a dud? Or maybe it just doesn't work that well. It looks nice, and I like that it’s mess-free and chemical-free, but beyond that? Not impressed.

I have another companies version of this product type that is superior to Zevo’s version. I owned it first but thought I’d give s vo a try. The rounded sticky pad and design is not as effective as capturing insects and this product is 2x’s the cost. It works, it just doesn’t work well and if I hadn’t opened and used the product I’d return it. If you could find a flat version, same principle, I’d go with that one because it works much better!
Like the product but when I received it the box was broken into and 2 refills missing. Needed them so I did not return.

I have fruit flies swarming in my her. It happens every year. They come in from the septic system. I had zappers that helped but not enough, so I thought I try Zevo. It didn’t work. I had both sitting side by side and the zappers worked better. It only caught a handful of flies when I have many dozens swarming near by. Not good at catching flies.

I bought this because I wanted to capture and kill annoying mosquitos in my home, but I guess the mosquitos were too smart and I only caught some gnats. I ended up returning this item since it did not suit my intended purpose of CAPTURING MOSQUITOS.... GAHHHH!!! It also can suit as a night light if you do decide to keep this item.
I put them in places that would attract more of the Nats, which is what we had happening and hardly any Nats got on it. I just got me some fly strips and cut more than the product gave us.
2 stars because it looks nice. Not sure it works bc haven’t trapped anything. I still see pests flying around

So far it does not seem to work well at all. Have a few flies in the house and they are still buzzing around. Will try to move it around and give it a few more days but prospect for catching any insects seems low.

Very misleading. Advertising says 2 traps + 2 refills! To me, it sounded like you got 2 complete traps + 2 refills. Not so! You get two complete traps and no refills. Plus they don’t work that well! Sorry I bought these!
I think those fruit flies has a mind of their own not one got caught 🤔 Still waiting to see.
This product did absolutely nothing! They sat on the apples as if they were a park bench. A total waist of money.

Cute concept, except not effective. It definitely attracts the gnats/fruit flies there to it but they just congregate and hang out on the top and not one actually goes inside. There have been 5+ at a time just hanging out. Resorted back to our bug zapper to help kill them. Was hopeful but disappointed.

I've had these for 3 days. Not very long but after not seeing seeing Flys trapped in the containers for 2 days I set out mouse glue traps. Clearly the glue traps get the bugs alot better and alot cheaper. I'll have to dump out the the useless liquid in these and return them. I guess Ill get more glue traps.
Barely catches or even attracts the flies. It is a good price but not worth it in my opinion. I tried using multiple and they just didn't do a good job.
Have had these out for several months and have refilled but only caught 2 fruit flies out of the 100s
I've had it up for a while now. And not a gnat in the trap. Plenty insight though. at least 1 or two flying in front of my face while trying to work. But none in the trap.

I had a gnat problem for weeks, and I used it exactly as described. Not a single gnat or food bug came close to it. It is a total waste sitting in all areas of the home and is also overpriced. You can buy three of these at Home Depot for $7.00/pack. It is going in the trash!
No luck. The gnats literally go into but then come back out alive!

All it did was give them a place to congregate and multiply. There may have been some dead inside but I guarantee there were 10 times more flying around right outside of the traps. I ended up throwing them all out within a day. Awful product. Didn't kill many, didn't prevent more, did nothing productive.

I bought 6 and put them throughout my townhome next to fruit and lights and have caught 0 flies. It’s been 3 days and most wasteful purchase. I would like a refund! Maybe in a studio these will work but they did nothing for me. Now having to spend more money on other things. Waste of a product.
don't buy

So I bought this because I had a terrible gnat infestation and needed them gone. This was not worth buying as it was not trapping them inside the container. They would just sit on top and fly off when I would come close.

I bought this for pesky fruit flies.... I emptied 1/2 bottle of their vinegar solution into each trap... No takers after 4 days.... Waste of money... Fruit fly will sniff fumes on top of the plastic apple, as if they were getting high... I want my money back..

I've had these set up in both the kitchen and bathroom for nearly a week, but they haven't caught a single fruit fly—they're still flying high! Really disappointed with this product. Unfortunately, I already threw away the packaging, so I can't even return it. Big thumbs down!I 👎
This does not kill any fruit flys, a huge rip off, do not buy.
I bought 6 of these traps and not one of them caught any fruit flys.

Set up multiple traps spread out in the room where I've been seeing them and in the span of a week, they've killed exactly 1. All of the other traps have been empty. I've had better luck killing them with my hands when they fly too close to me.

Had a pesky fly for a few days and ordered these for the little pest! 3 days that thing was plugged in and that fly never went near it! I finally caught him on my front door and opened it for him to escape. The zevo trap is still clean, I gave it 2 stars because the blue light is pretty but it does not work as advertized!

I knew this worked with a light but I had no idea the light would be as bright as it is. I sleep in a room that is susceptible to flies and mosquitoes (for a variety of reasons) and this device is so bright in the dark that it's ridiculous. The other device was plugged in to an outlet on my back porch (also susceptible to flies, etc. since I go in and out frequently to garden) and I like to sit there at night to enjoy the night sounds, etc. The brilliant blue light is ridiculous. I had both devices plugged in for 24 hours and one collected a gnat while a fly few around and the other collected nothing --also while a fly few around. So between the brightness and the less than stellar performance, I'm returning it. Unrelated to the product, I have to mention the packaging.

10 days since I made this purchase, wish I could get my money back. The blue light does literally nothing to attract bugs- and the adhesive does not actually trap the bugs, just hassles them and detains them like a bad traffic stop. Save your money, clean your damn space.

Had an issue with flies in my house so bought two. Did not catch one single fly. A few moths but that’s about it.
4-5 days is not a good test

I read a lot of the reviews and thought this was a no-brainer as we occasionally have a fruit fly party in our kitchen....packaging was good, setup was super easy, delivery was fast The first day/night I used this I saw that we had maybe 1 fruit fly that was caught....so I actually moved a small bowl of fruit, peels, etc that the flies were in directly under the Zevo and shut off all of the other lights in the kitchen. I left the bowl there for a couple days while the fruit flies were literally walking around the Zevo and hanging out on the outlet....after about a week of using this, I may have caught 10 fruit flies and probably doubled the population doing things to try and capture them. Not impressed and will be returning

Two stars because two flies is all that we caught. The other six flies avoided both traps in stealth-like flight patterns and ultimately were taken down with an old-fashioned fly swat. Zevo $37.47, fly swatter $0.99. I wouldn't waste your money.

Have this for 2 weeks. Haven't captured other flys like others I have used. Did collect some fruit flys. One in kitchen other in landry room. Both issue. If it doesn't collect better will send back since it cost so mich money.
Having an issue with fruit flys in my apartment, bought the 2 pack of Zevo. After 2 weeks, they've trapped maybe 4 out of 100+ fruit flys. Only positive is the nice blue nightlight

I had hoped that the Zevo would trap this annoying fly buzzing around my head and reading light this morning. I ordered the product just for this situation! Doesn’t work, placed two near where fruit flies and gnats where seen. Two days and no captures. Absolutely no insects captured!

Unfortunately we purchased these because we seem to have a moth infestation for every three four moths I catch,... in a few days there's double that so we purchased this item in the hopes that it would take care of the issue but to my surprise, no... there will be a moth right next to the z e v o plug-in,.. as if it's laughing at me! And then a few others on the wall a few feet away or across the room, the only lucky thing is that I catch him with a vacuum cleaner. So I guess this is a not so good review.

Two stars because at least it tells you it works best in dark spaces.
Over priced i bought a 2 trap 4 refill set for 10 dollars less than this sellers price. The product itself is amazing !
Noticed a few bugs caught but the majority still remained. Tried different locations with no better results. Would not purchase again.
To be fair I have yet to see any results. I still see a few gnats.

The gnats and tiny flies don’t go between Zevo and wall…nothing was caught and there are many teeny tiny gnats in the area so….i unplugged the Zevo and waved it around the critters and snagged them..but I bought two for one room and did not catch one when plugged in…I could have bought a fly swatter for much less money!!

Ordered this for what I thought was fruit flys but I’m starting to think it doesn’t work that great. It’s really bright at night and big. There are little gnat bugs tormenting my home. I’m pretty sure these are drain flys because they are in the bathroom too. Be sure to order drain fly killer too if you’re not sure because this light doesn’t seem to have any effect on them. Even the little red apple on the counter below it with ACV inside isn’t working. They just chill on top of it.

We bought this because it had so many amazing reviews, specifically for fruit flys. We went on vacation and sadly forgot to take out the recycling, when we got home our kitchen was filled with fruit flies. We used one of those electric swatters and got SO MANY, but there were still creeps lingering, so I bought this product, for two rooms (1000 sq ft apartment), and we have caught exactly one fly, but an actual, large one. So we still run around chasing fruit flies with our racket. So maybe it's great if you have the big guys, I don't recommend for the little ones.

I’ve seen ads for this product for a while so it immediately came to mind as a solution when we started having a fruit fly problem. I like the concept of this product but felt it wasn’t as effective as it should have been. I placed it where I had seen most of the fruit flies hanging out and it did catch a few, but I was a little disappointed that it wasn’t making much of a dent in the population. Something else I should have considered before purchasing was how brightly the blue light shines from this device. We avoid blue light around bedtime and dim the lights in our house in the evenings as we get ready to sleep and so this didn’t fit with my personal sleep hygiene preferences - but that’s honestly my fault for not thinking through how this product works before purchasing it. After giving Zevo a chance for a few days I decided to try the old apple cider vinegar in a jar with tiny holes poked in the lid method. The apple cider jar was the clear winner and captured at least 3-4 times more in a single night than the Zivo had in days. The homemade method was much cheaper too. If I could go back and do it all over again I would just do the jar trick and save money by not purchasing this product.
Not sure they are doing any good. We continue to see the same little gnats.

I'm not impressed. I live in the county, have a house dog, and flies. The minute I open my door, a couple of flies come in. So, I bought two of the large-size Zevo's. Installed them in my kitchen, as that seems to be where the flies go. I have captured 3 flies in these Zevos in the last 2 weeks. I have fly swattered many more than that. I don't want to chase flies around with a fly swatter. That's why I bought these Zevos. I feel that I wasted my money. My neighbor has good luck with her Zevos catching gnats. I don't have gnats. The Zevo has a good rating. So it's catching something. But I don't think it's flies.

I was hopeful about the Zevo Flying Insect Traps, but unfortunately, they didn’t work as well as I expected. I followed all the setup instructions and placed them where gnats are usually a problem, but they barely caught any. The light is supposed to attract flying insects, but it didn’t seem to draw in the gnats in my home. After several days, there were only a few bugs stuck to the cartridge. For the price, I was disappointed with the results. On the plus side, it’s easy to set up and looks clean and safe around kids and pets—but effectiveness was lacking for gnats or fruit flies in my case. I might try a different brand or stick with natural traps next time.

This review has nothing to do with the quality of the product. It works well. I am referring to one I had prior to my move. I had one delivered at my new address and Amazon sent me an open box with the carriages gone. I asked Amazon to replace the item and send it back t me as an exchange. They had the nerve to send me the same item back again. I am totally disappointed with the people of Amazon.

Maybe the flies up in the NW of the US are different but I have had them all over our house here recently with the heat and i had these plugged in all weekend, stickered peeled off and not a single insect on them. I even put some soda in little dish next to it the third day just to give them a reason to get close to the blue light and I found one house fly drowned in the soda but nothing stuck to the trap lol. another week in. Update it does seem to catch a few gnats and fruit flies but as for house flies, still nothing.

Did not catch a single fly in over a week. We live in southeast Louisiana, the flys during the summer always find their way in. After having friends over and everyone walking in and out of the house through the back door quite a few flies got inside probably about 10. I purchased two of these and put them in the area that the flies seem to be staying, which was in our kitchen around our sink. I put one on the side of our island and one on the counter wall outlet in an over a week. It did not catch a single fly. We resorted to the old faithful fly swaters

I was hoping this would work but these are virtually worthless. We have a garden so we have found fruit flies, and thought this would help. After checking the cartridges very little evidence of any flies. So I put out some small bowls with a mixture of vinegar and dish soap and covered them with plastic wrap and punched some holes. Caught one fruit fly not impressed! And fruit flies are all around it see pics
This is just a sticky flap, that sits on top of a plug in. UV light is minimal.

Not very effective
Doesn't work very good.
They work but they are a little awkward and unattractive
The light works. But bugs show no interest in the light. Nothing for a week. Last night a bug died of old age.
Works OK for gnats but not at all for fruit flies

Reasonable price and easy to assemble, these did not attract a single fly in 24 hours. I'm not sure whether it is the Gizmo itself or the type of flies. The less expensive fly traps, vinegar and soap, and sticky traps also worked poorly. I put up pictures of the flies. The sticky trap pulled more flies than anything else, but this is a drop in the bucket. In the last two days, these flies have become ubiquitous. I have now tried five methods. I wish I knew what else to try. #UPDATE: What finally worked? Located a rotting onion beneath the rest of my veggies and tossed it. Bye bye flies.
I just watched a fruit fly land on it and walk around. It isnt sticky enough to catch the fruit flies. There was one big fly stuck so it likely works for heavier bugs.

I dislike the product because it did not work. 5 baits and the apple; maybe 2 or 3 fruit flies caught in a week. Terro brand really let me down this time; great for ant problems but you really need to tweak the 'lure bait' for this product.
It doesn’t work!

Complete and total bust. Two weeks in and (I'm not exaggerating when I say this) not one single gnat, fly, fruit fly, or insect of any kind has ended up in one of these traps. Followed the directions to the letter and nothing. They fly right past it as tho it's not even there.
Didn’t work at all Why did you pick this product vs others?: Thought they would work
Does NOT work..

Purchased 6 traps. Arrived loose in a box, with none of the usual hard plastic protective wrap. One bottle of fluid had completely leaked away, and everything was soggy. So I have waited a few months to see the full results and I am here to tell you don't waste your money on this. A small glass with apple cider vinegar does a far superior job. I like the little apples and design unfortunately after 3 days there is 1 gnat in 1 and none in the others. I put the old soap and vinegar next to it and have several in less than a days time. This isn’t worth the $

The description states 2 Plug-in Bases + 2 Refill Cartridges. You expect that with the 2 Plug-In Bases you would get the Cartridges and 2 refills. You only get the bases no cartridges in the bases. The description is very tricky to read as your expecting the Plug-In bases to have everything to just plug the unit in and it will contain the fly trap. It does not come ready to use, you have to insert the "Refill Cartridge", so you only get 2 Plug-ins and no inserts, you have to use your refills, only then is it ready to use, the description is deceptive. In my opinion its shorting you 2 cartridges. In order to resolve this "Description Issue", Amazon or the Seller should change the description to "Zevo Flying Insect Trap, Fly Trap, Fruit Fly Trap (2 Plug-in Bases + 2 (Refill should be removed), Cartridges). This way the consumer will get a complete product and without "Refills".

Bought a couple of these for a store i work in. Its not very effective at attracting flies. We installed it in an area where we had a lot of fruit fly problems and while there were swarms of them in the area after several days this device only caught a handful of them.

The product is well made, the blue light stays on and the trap is sticky. I was disappointed in the products ability to attract fruit and house flies. After the first week I still have the household pests and the traps on both of the devices I plugged in are fairly empty.

It hasn’t caught a single thing. Now I haven’t noticed a lot of flies or gnats, but I’ll definitely be anxious to see if it catches them once they are in the house again. We’ve had some cooler days recently, and I haven’t had as much fruit out. I’m optimistic though.
The item Does not catch flies as advertised

So, overall, good for gnats, but no housefly or mosquito was trapped. :( It has been in operation for over a month. We live in central Florida.
Worked ok. Still had fruit flies with 2 units plugged in.
Not working, I’ve had them plugged in for a couple of weeks. Nothing gets attracted to it.

One of the two traps came DOA. The other one works as expected.
The traps themselves are great, although both are cracked & broken. Packed in plastic rather than a box. Bad move, Amazon!
Not sure if these work the best… but the fruit flies are starting to disappear. I have never had so many fruit flies ever, so I got these to try. I have 2 in my kitchen and still have a problem.

Did not do but so good on my gnats with two units. It did none thing for my Indian meal moths either. I have only seen about 3 bugs in both units together.

It catches Nats but not flies. Have the flies from outside that come in my rv door. It's great on Nats just not flies

I’m not very happy with this product. We plugged it in beside the fruit and there’s still flies everywhere and barely any on the sticky part of the Zevo. It’s been weeks and I am very disappointed. I’ve seen ads for this for many months and finally bought it when it was on sale, but I’m very disappointed and would not buy this again.
we have had these for 24 hours and have a huge mosquito issue in our area. not sure if we need more time but they haven't caught anything yet.

Does not work as described! Disappointed in performance! Lack of!
This product was useful, however I wish it were stronger and attracted more bugs than it did.

I have had a gnat problem for over a month now and I got tired of making the vinegar traps every few days so I figured this would be perfect because reviews were mostly good. I got these traps a few days ago and put them up immediately the first night they got a few bugs on each trap so I figured it just took a little to actually catch them but now 4 days later they haven’t caught any more. So last night I put out vinegar traps again because of this not working and my homemade traps have caught 10-15 in each cup and I have 5 cups…. These aren’t working at all so it was a waste of money.

This product is ok, on the plus side it is basically a fly trap with UV light to attract flying insects. Been using it for 2-3 weeks now, but so far...not too effective. On the negative, you have to continually buy more fly trap inserts.

I believe I was sent counterfeit filters with my box of 2 Zevo traps. The filters did not have Zevo on them. Other than that, things seem to work pretty well. I just hate I paid for the real thing and didn't receive it.

We had a problem with gnats/fruit flies and were trying everything we could to get rid of them.....its one of the bad things about have a septic tank. The traps use light to attract.....I don't think it really was attracting them fast enough. I was hoping for quicker and better results. One the gnat goes towards the light it gets trapped on a super-stick adhesive backing....which you will need to replace once it fills up. Looks almost like a night lights and there is no smell. It works ...just not as quick as you might want it to when dealing with pesty bugs.

I guess they were fairly effective so I'd give them that. Not overwhelmingly so, but good enough. What is so dismaying is the cost of replacement shields. Can't justify it for the results. Tried Apple Cider Vinegar, Dawn and water in a cut off plastic soda bottle and getting similar results. There's no way the results justify the cost.

I've had fruit flies lately so decided to try the Zevo bug catcher as my daughter had good luck with hers. I haven't seen very good results and most of the fruit flies end up in my drink glass or swarming around my food when I'm eating. bought 2 of them and so far each one has caught two or three of them. Maybe I don't have them in good places but they are near my fruit and where I eat. I wouldn't buy them again. I am going back to the vinegar/sugar in a glass method as that has worked better for me in the past.

It takes up a lot of space around the plug. I had to rearrange the items on my kitchen counter to allow easy access for the bugs to see the blue light. I live in a small house so placement was difficult.

I purchsed the Zevo fly traps pack of two several weeks ago. I have so many houseflys that come in every time the door is opened. After three days, I checked the traps and only one of them had a fly in it. This product advertises that the purple light attracts flies, but I did not see that happening. How, after these several weeks, there are a couple house flys and a lot of gnats. I am not sure if I would buy this product again. It was disappointing.
Don't know if they work yet but they are very bright.
I read the "2 refill cartridges" as 2 more cartridges. That's what refill means to replace that is what is used up. But you only get the two machines and 2 cartridges.
Worked okay, but not as quickly as I hoped. Found the apple cider vinegar diy fix worked better and faster.

Meh. It kind of works. There are a handful of fruit flies on the sticky surface but I didn’t notice a significant decline in them flying around like it promised. Like, I didn’t feel it attracted them.

Had house flies and small fruit flies in the house before we left for vacation, so I bought two of these. When we got back, the traps caught some bugs but no house flies. Still had fruit flies and some house flies flying around 😥

I wish this worked as described. I've had this unit plugged in for a week and caught 3 flies and 1 mosquito. We basically have a farm and flies are a menace. I was hoping this was the answer...don't waste your money!
Just meh…, our odorless hanging trap captures much more. Have had #2 of these up in my kitchen and have only captured a couple between the two.
We had an infestation of fly's put two traps up and only caught 3 in both traps and there were hundreds of fly's in the area, Now it did catch some Nat's

I am not satisfied with product performance. It did not attract houseflies as I let myself believe from TV and Written ads. May have worked better if I used an extension cord and set the device on the window sill. Flies are gone now. If they come back I'm going gas station bathroom style with strips of curly yellow fly paper.
Not sure about this. Still has to swat at a few flies.
Doesn't kill many flies, underwhelming performance.

I was hoping this would control the fruit flies that are constant at this time of year. Unfortunately, the trap has only caught a few in the three weeks I've had it plugged in. I'm disappointed that it hasn't caught more. Value for money? I see very little value in this product.
My houseflies fly right past them. No captures, all summer.
Battery life not impressive at all.
It only worked for a few months unfortunately but when it did work it worked great!
Power is great and kills on contact. But the edge depth makes it impossible to trap and zap. And the bugs often get caught in the mesh design.

This fly swatter helps deal with flies, but it’s not as effective as one might hope. Sometimes it kills, sometimes it stuns and you need to finish the fly off with a paper towel. The light that is supposedly there to attract flies is incredibly dim and doesn’t do anything. Overall it feels cheap and I am disappointed, but can make it work.
They work great but do not hold a charge very long.
Its okay I guess But it takes multiple hits to take down a disgusting annoying fly -_- I thought I would take them down in 1 hit But nooooooooooo
Worked well ,(almost 4months) but then would not charge at all. Disappointed!!

They were delivered on time and was exactly what I expected. However less than 2 months of getting them the power button came off 1 of them. I tried putting it back on but it doesn't work anymore. So considering I now only have 1 working out of 2 I feel it's not a good value for the money.

I returned this because the charging cord plugs directly into the unit instead of the base. If you have a fly or something, you can’t just grab it off the base - you have to unplug it first. Minor issue I know, but I the one I had previously charged from the base and was much more convenient to grab and go when you have an annoying pest flying around.

This product does work, but remember, it's the amount of amperage that kills, not the voltage. IME, this 4,000 voltage fly swatter doesn't have enough amperage to instantly kill most house flys unless they get entangled in wiring. The flies need to be zapped up to three times to kill them. But if you hate flies in the house, the zapping noise is satisfying perversely - you still need to hunt them down. UPDATE: After using it more, I am revising my rating to 4 stars. As a stand-alone unit for house flies, it is useless - not bright enough for flies and only seems to stun them. Swinging widely at flying flies doesn't work well because if you by chance hit them and they are not stuck to the racket, they are merely stunned. If you have flies on the windows - it does work better than a fly-swatter as you don't have a mess on the window.

I ordered a package of six traps and in oder to work must be filled with a bottle of liquid that should have comes with it. I received only 3 bottles and they didn't come in a package as shown. Just 6 traps thrown in box delivered with 3 bottles of liquid. As of right now they are not working.

I started using the traps on June 29th and by July 14th the liquid has dried out. So, it does not las for 45 days as per the description. As soon as the liquid is gone, the fruit flies are back! Will I buy it again? Probably not. Maybe I will try another product that will last more.

These were purchased to catch fruit flies. Filled with the provided very strong vinegar and placed right where fruit flies were and accomplished absolutely nothing for days. I finally added some honey to the vinegar and caught some fruit flies. Stars given because it's good to put working bait in and reusable.

Not great at catching flies but not bad either. It caught a couple of flies. It's basically vinegar solution in a pretty container. Would by again just to catch the odd fly but also buying one of those more pricey fly zappers. This would just be like an additional thing
I used to buy these all the time and they were incredibly effective. Now I’m lucky if 1 or 2 gnats fall in there. Not sure what happened.

Surprisingly one of the traps had several dead ants, didn't know I had that issue too. My other traps only had a few gnats after a week and the vinegar smell is awful! I have seen fewer gnats, but if the problem continues I'll be exploring other options.

The product itself is simple and works great for fruit flies. I will most likely buy again. However I think the claim of lasts up to 40 days is a hard stretch. Put mine out 7 days ago and it is nearly empty of liquid. Kind of feel scammed by that claim for that price.

Product headline (for 6) says "270 day lure supply". This is false, there is no refill at all. You do not get supplies to run all 6 traps for 270 days each, you get just exactly enough liquid to power each trap once, and you are instructed to throw it away after 45 days (if they last even that long).
The problem are the flies are certainly attracted to this, but they won’t get their little behinds in there and die. They’ll just lay about the ones I have trolling me.
Just received these and one of the bottles had spilled in the box and was completely empty. No time to return and wait for replacement!

Primero no enciende la luz violeta en modo normal al presionar el botón. No sé si es defecto o viene así. Las otras raquetas que tengo siempre encienden la luz. Segundo es algo pesada. Tiene buen acabado y se siente sólida. Apartando esas dos cosas funciona excelente

This product is just ok, the electricity is too weak to zap the fly in just one stroke. you need to at least swat it 2-3 times to kill it. Love the design though, it looks clean and love the option that you can just leave it on its stand.

Not great. I have bought two different brands of knock off inserts. This one is garbage. The flies stick gently, and when it gets full, you have to carefully change it or else you can knock the flies loose. I do not recommend these, nor would I ever buy them again.

The "ZAP" noise will make almost anyone jump. It is unsettling loud for no good reason. I would NOT recommend to use this as a "fly swatter". Nor would i want to leave it sitting anywhere and on as the random loud zap noise will break your focus on what ever else you may be doing each time it goes off. Duel purpose swatter, in a stand with blue lite on it attracks flys which seems to work well, problem is it isent strong enough to kill them, just knocks Them out temporarily. I don't want to knock them out I want to kill them

I was going to return it. It was not consistent. One would work while the other would not. Then it would keep sparking and make so much noise that it warns the bugs to get out of the way. But since I have used it to kill some bugs, I decided to just keep it. I still should have returned it.

That said, I wish the dog didn’t care because it’s quite satisfying killing bugs with this thing. If you’re not concerned about a pet freaking out at the sound of it, definitely give it a try

This is a terrible product. I bought 4 of these and then my wife bought another 4 unbeknownst to me. Will these kill fruit flies? Absolutely. So why the 1 star review? We went from having a dozen to dozens of dozens. Our kitchen is crawling with them. The traps allow the fruit flies to move in and out freely so the flies that don't die just reproduce more resistant flies. How did I end up fixing this Terro created problem? By making my own fruit fly traps with jars filled with red wine, plastic wrap over the top with a rubber band to hold it in place, and then poking holes through them. Cost me nothing since I had these objects already on hand. I will never buy this product again.

Worthless. Didn't catch a single fruit fly in 4 traps in a weeks time. Decided to then purchase a UV/blue light trap that caught over a dozen in under a day.

I thought I had fruit flies but I think I had gnats. These did not trap anything and the liquid provided just evaporates. Amazon would not take our return as you have to open the box to see the product. We then ordered the product that you plug into the wall that shines a blue light and captures gnats on a sticky surface. This second product is working beautifully. We have caught over 100 gnats in a few days. So, make sure you have fruit flies not gnats. Fruit flies and gnats look very similar, so don't make the same mistake we did, or you will be wasting your money like we did.

DO NOT BUY LIKE SERIOUSLY ITS SO BAD! I had a fly infestation and the liquid did nothing but evaporate (it was indoor too). I set it up where the flies were spawning from and it caught NOTHING. It's an awful trap. Definitely not worth the money. I wish I could give it zero stars. You can't even return or get refund because of transportation of hazardous materials which is so dumb because how did they send it in the first place. COMPLETE Waste of money. Go buy the light traps instead. I don't know how these other reviews were positive. I ended up having to kill all the flies myself. I killed 26 in one day and these apples sat as dumb decorations.
I found the product to be ineffective for my use case and is unable to be returned. Would not buy again and I wish I just went with sticky pads as I originally intended
I bought this because I have a lot of fruit flies recently. When I received this I set it up as noted in instructions and no fruit flies have been attracted to it. Waste of money!!

There are two traps in each box but only one tube of the liquid. Either the product is short one tube or your supposed to share the tube with both traps - it's not clear. I think there should be two tubes of liquid - one for each trap. Another thing to consider is that the trap does capture some fruit flies - however, most of the fruit flies that enter the trap fly back out of it. A better and more cost-effective way is to get a small jar of some sugar water in it and place something sticky like fly paper sticking out of the jar. This product works but barely. Not worth the cost.
After a month no fruit flies captured, I had this product a year ago and it worked that's why I decided to order it but this time no fruit flies eliminated and the liquid evaporated.
Doesn't really work. I have had four traps out for 24 hours and only caught a single fruit fly with them. Just make your own traps with apple cider vinegar, they work better

I’ve been brand-loyal to Terro for years. When I opened the box, I noticed new packaging for the liquid- and now I notice that it no longer traps flies like it used to. With the old bait (clear cylinder, blue screw lid), I would catch ALL of them. This packaging (clear squeeze tube with lid that needed to be cut off) must contain a different formulation because it doesn’t work at all. It also evaporates more quickly.
This did not work well. I had better results with vinegar and dish soap. The apples are cute, but that's about it.

These are crap. They definitely attract flies but there’s so little liquid that if the flies don’t go all the way in the trap and down the the very bottom, nothing’s going to happen. Them advertising the flies have no way back out is literally just false. The holes they go in through are so large they can just as easily get back out. Going into the little liquid given is the only thing that kills them.

This was a super easy to put together and aesthetically pleasing fly catcher, the only problem is that it doesn’t catch any flies. There’s not a single fruit fly in the container and although they seem to be hovering around the area—I’ve kept this catcher out for about half of the week and absolutely nothing. Save yourself some dollars and make a catcher yourself with sugar and apple cider vinegar.

Edit: Knocking off two more stars because the liquid evaporated in 2 days. 180 days? Nope. Noticed improvement within 12 hours, but only after placing two traps, one did not seem to be enough. I will also add that the solution seems to be your standard apple cider vinegar. If the apple trap is what you want specifically over a jar and saran wrap it's definitely worth it but otherwise there's nothing special about the product. Be sure to fill the apples away from any spot you want to save, I filled them at my work desk before moving them where they were needed and that was my mistake. The lingering smell of vinegar attracted them to that spot for hours and it was quite annoying.

These are simply tiny plastic containers filled with apple vinegar. Absolutely zero accountability or pride in their product. Buying these is identical to buying snake oil. Just but apple cider vinegar and sage your money. -CSL
I did exactly what it said on the box and it did not work at all .
buy a UV light trap. I had four of these and literally didn’t catch a single fruit fly in all of them and i must have caught 50+ when using a UV light trap. Ridiculous.
These did not work. Caught 2 gnats over 3 months of trying. Wish Icould return!

Terro Ant baits are my go-to for dealing with ant problems, so I assumed this product would work just as effectively for dealing with fruit flies. WRONG. The flies go in the plastic apple, but are able to fly right out. This product caught zero fruit flies. In contrast, our homemade fruitfly trap that we keep at the other end of our home behind a closed door works like a charm. We took an empty salsa glass jar (the kind that is short with a wider opening and is generally found next to the potato chips at the grocery store), and poked several small holes in the metal lid (I forgot whether we used a nail, or a drill), and filled it halfway with apple cider vinegar. Works like a charm and cost almost nothing to make. I bought the Terro fruitfly traps for the kitchen because I'm too lazy to make another homemade trap, and I thought the plastic apples would look nicer. But it ended up being a waste of money.
Doesn't seem to attract our flies as expected. After a week only 1 fly trapped. Multiple flies are present but they don't appear interested in this trap
It's fine. Works like it should. Don't believe everything has to be rated or bragged about. Hate this aspect of using Amazon.
They definitely work but not necessarily during the day.
Has not really had any impact on the number of flies in the house, but it is catching the tiny gnats we have had!
Product came already open and one of my cartridges is missing
Purchased to catch flys. 🪰 The small fruit flies are attracted to the traps, but the bigger flys avoid avoid them.
We have not captured very many insects with the Zevo gadget, and these refills are not inexpensive.
Very good price for four refills. Shipping was very fast. However, there were only three refills in the box, not four.
The more bugs it catches, you have to change the sticky card more often. So buy refills ahead of time. The area needs to be dark enough to attract bugs to the blue light.
Refills are not as effective as the original sticky pad. Refills have a white background. Original sticky pad was clear. Fruit flies are winning the battle.
I can ditto the other 1-star reviews of this product. INEFFECTIVE. Waste of money.
These are ineffective. I have fruit flies all over the house and had them in the worst areas for three weeks. I got one fly. Save your money
They don't work at all. Total waste of money!

Highly disappointed. Used this brand of ant traps/bait and they worked. I had this out for 2 weeks or more. Not one fruit fly caught, though there were many fruit flies to be had!! Just realized as I sit in parking lot of UPS trying to pull up return code that I can’t return this 4 pack of little red plastic apples - because that’s all this is. Nothing more than a cheap Dollar store item!! They really should have a zero star option. Yes, I’m annoyed!! 🤪

waste of money. Put two of these out, only caught one fruit fly before the lure dried up. I placed them close to the sink & trash can, where the fruit flies hang out. Left them there for a few weeks and was disappointed with the result. I caught more flies in my morning coffee than in these traps! Side note: I don't like that Terro doesn't list what the active ingredient is in their fly trap chemicals. I'd prefer to know what chemicals are in it before I leave it sitting out on my counters.

TLDR - if you have homeflies in your area, don't buy this. This was the biggest mistake I've made in years buying this product. If you have a fruit fly problem, buy a plastic cup and fill it with apple cider vinegar; it's the same thing. I live in an area with houseflies, this product made it so much worse. The smell of the apple cider vinegar attracted hundreds of houseflies. They actually started hanging around my door and finding the tiniest cracks to funnel through. They crawled through the screen door via the corners, through cracks in window AC units, etc. I spent hours catching them and had to buy a electric fly swatter. Its one thing to buy a product that doesn't work, its something completely different to create more problems and worsen it. Plus its already a way overpriced for a container and apple cider vinegar.
Not worth it. Ive left two traps for over 2 days and they’ve catched maybe 4 fruit flies all together. The flies literally stay on top of the plastic and dont fall inside. Not very effective
Useless product, in over 3 days of use, it only trapped a single gnat, while a swarm of it is flying around it

These do not work. Today I went back to my old method of apple cider vinegar and dish soap. I had more fruit flies in two hours than in 5 days with this product. Yes, they look cute, but a waste of money. I think it's because the surface area is too small for the flies to get attracted. The jar I'm using is completely open and works great.

THIS DOES NOT WORK! The one thing it’s supposed to do…..AND the bugs are ACTUALLY MOCKING ME. I don’t know who came up with this idea, or what research went in that says this is effective…. The catch ability is NON-EXISTENT!! As seen in the picture, they don’t die or get caught.

There are too many holes, and they are much too large. The fruit flies easily go in and out of the trap. The liquid evaporates too fast to last 30 days. The liquid was gone in just 2 weeks and only caught about 4 bugs in that amount of time. I then used my grandmother's version of a fruit fly trap using a small relish jar with 2 tiny holes drilled in the lid. The solution is apple cider vinegar. In just the 2weeks the Terror trap was in place, the homemade trap caught 2 dozen fruit flies & the liquid hasn't evaporated. In 30 days, the whole surface of the liquid in the glass jar was covered in fruit flies.

Frankly unimpressed. Before getting these, I used a bowl with a TBS of sugar, half a cup of warm water and a few tablespoons of apple cider vinegar with a few drops of Dawn. I also have three Zevo insect traps (basically very sticky plastic inside a plug in that has a warm blue light). The bowl traps were far more effective at getting a dozen of the buggers trapped (fruit fly invasion, and I do mean invasion). The "bait" in these apple traps obviously contains apple cider vinegar. But the flys would crawl around on the outside and only a few would actually end up in the trap. These traps are very small, and supposedly last 45 days which is ridiculous. After only a few days, they stopped working completely. The Zevo traps, on the other hand, had well over a hundred flies trapped. I had to change those traps out twice a week (it's normally like a month or two before they have to be changed, but INVASION of fruit flies). They are much more effective.
Didn't work as I expected it would. went back to a bowl of apple cider vinegar and a few drops of dish soap, and it seems to work a lot better.

For whatever reason these did not work. No fruit fly’s entered the traps and the fluid used to trap the flys dried up in a couple of days. (No extra liquid came in the package.) They knocked over easily and spilled out. Not worth the money.
No instructions on how to set up or use these was included. There was an insert, but it was just an add for another one of their products.

I ordered this fruit fly trap with high hopes, especially given its quick shipping and straightforward setup. The product arrived promptly and was easy to assemble – I simply poured the provided liquid into the apple-shaped trap. However, my excitement quickly turned to disappointment as the trap proved ineffective. Despite its appealing design, it failed to catch any fruit flies in my home. The lure liquid seems to be just vinegar, which doesn’t appear to attract the fruit flies as expected. A sweeter scent might have worked better. After giving it a fair try, I cannot recommend this product. It did not deliver the results I needed, and I would suggest looking for a more effective solution for your fruit fly problem.
These apple shaped traps fall over way too easy spilling the bait on counters or tables attracting MORE flies. Need a more stable base.

Maybe it is the type of flies that I had, whatever was infesting a new Boston Fern I just bought. This product did not work on them. Not even one fell for it. The only thing that did were sticky blue light traps. It's likely that this product would work better with your run of the mill fruit flies. The liquid that comes with these do smell like the homemade vinegar traps I have made in the past. If I get a chance to test this product on other flies and if it works, I will update my rating.
This does not really catch any fruit flies. It’s effective for about a day before it loses whatever causes it to work
Did absolutely nothing, would not recommend
Not successful with my gnat problem. Used these wax melts yellow sticky spikes. Much better results.
pretty much worthless, even in a small room

I bought these to combat fruit flies, they are very cute. Poured the apple cider vinegar into the apple and I could smell it pretty distinctly. I was hoping they would work, I brought one to work, 1 upstairs and 1 in the kitchen. Each one caught maybe 1 or 2. I would go with the plugins.

Writing this as two fruit flies buzz furiously around my face. I've had these for a week and the issue is not gone. I've gutted my room and there is nothing that could be harboring these flying monsters. I've used apple cider vinegar and dish soap in the past with success and thought these would work better; they do not.

I purchased this for my office, which is notorious for getting stupid little fruit flies. I only set up 2 of the 4 apples I received, and filled them using the first set of liquid bottles. In less than 2 weeks, I had to refill the apples again, when the trap fluid is supposed to last for 45 days. Now, a week later, the refill has already dried up again. So what was supposed to be 180 days of lure turned out to be 19 days of supply. I will say, though, that in those 19 days, the product does work. But the liquid just evaporates way too fast to be worth the money out on this.
The liquid used to lure the fruit flies evaporates within a few weeks, doesn't really work

Greenway traps each caught a dozen fruit flies in a couple of days. The only downside is that they don't catch other species of small bugs. (For those pour bleach down the drain.) My Terro traps caught only a few before the liquid evaporated. The Greenway bottle design keeps them from spilling, and also from evaporating. Terro traps have several big holes so they can spill, and that also allows the liquid to evaporate faster. Terro comes with refills but Greenway doesn't need them. The bugs can also get out of the Terro as easily as getting in, but the Greenway traps hold them until the fumes kill them. They can't climb out with the funnel opening for the same reason it doesn't spill.
Mildly effective, but didn't fully get rid of the fruit flies. Just culled their numbers for a bit until they learned how to walk out.

Love the cute, convenient containers. But it's literally just vinegar and dishwashing detergent that didn't work alone, and that's what I had tried on my own without much success before buying 4 of these traps for $14. After an entire day of catching NO fruit flies with these, I looked at some YouTube videos and saw a guy who tried BALSAMIC vinegar instead of white vinegar or apple cider vinegar (which is what I had used.) I added the Balsamic vinegar to the Terro solution and left the house to run some errands. When I returned just an hour later, one of the traps already had 10 flies in it. By last night (24 hours later) there were almost 20 in one of them and 10 in another. I'm hoping that by tonight our bizarre infestation will be almost eradicated. But it's the BALSAMIC VINEGAR that I added that made it effective. I don't regret my purchase because these containers were worth the initial investment. My brother said that these used to have a pheromone in them to attract fruit flies but apparently from the reviews, they changed the formula over the past couple of years, and there was nothing in the ingredient list besides the detergent and vinegar.
Easy to use, but they didn’t do much to reduce the fruit flies in my kitchen. Might work for light issues, but didn’t help my situation much.

They’re cute and I like how they look around kitchen but honestly they didn’t really work for me. I would maybe find one or two bugs using the liquid they provided. Maybe need to try something else inside.
Well, I had high hopes for these but the coffee attracted more fruit flies than these did. 8-1
Barley worked and the liquid is basically vinegar smell solution. When I put out a small little bowl of peroxide nearby it’s works so much better catching the flys than the Terro traps

Maybe it works for someone, nit for me. 4 traps, 2 liquids. Tried 2 in the kitchen. 1 near hydroponic farm and bananas. 1 on the other side of kitchen, where I eat. Bottle equally distributed. 1st is already liquid free after a week. No catch. Other - 1 catch. And 4-5 fruit flies constantly flying around. (Provably loughing). It seems aroma is just not attractive (or they like bananas more) Turns out Zevo with violet light os more atractive. Catches gnats and flies at astonishing rate. Planning to purchase more. This thing has way to improve. Starting with evaporation rate

The traps caught a few gnats for a few days - then nothing. Mine are bone dry after 19 days - nowhere near the 45 days claimed. I guess the attractive containers could be used with a vinegar and dish soap mix, but the plastic tub with a 4" diameter open top I used before getting these caught more gnats. My recommendation would be to spend your money on a large bottle of apple cider vinegar and plan to refresh your bait every week.
A home remedy worked better
Did not works as well as plugins

I put these by a sink that always has fruit flies around it. They just fly all around the trap but don’t go in it. In weeks I’ve only gotten 1 fruit fly in a trap. The apple shape is cuter than fly tape though.

Easily outdone by a bowl of apple cider and a bit of plastic wrap. Easy to use but not very effective and I not happy about tossing plastic into the trash. I'd rather throw the bugs into the trash and reuse the plastic

I’ve been using a “recipe” I found online for a few years now to get rid of fruit flies every time these darn things start popping up- a mixture of apple cider vinegar, sugar, dish soap, and water. It works AMAZINGLY every time. Seems lately however, I can’t keep up with refilling the dish, changing out the mixture for new stuff (they’re SO BAD right now!!). I was attracted to this stuff by its cute little apple container, and the fact that it claimed to last 45 days. Well, it’s been less than a week and I’ve already gone through all 4. They don’t seem to work as well as the DIY mixture I make, nor do they seem to attract as many fruit flies as when I just put the mixture in a small bowl. Should’ve known it wouldn’t work as well, but with all of the great reviews, I was hopeful! Just Google the “recipe” to make the stuff on your own. It’s a lot cheaper!

I did not find these very effective. I put up about a dozen of these things around my kitchen, and in a week's time, they caught about 10 fruit flies. At the same time, I put down three plastic containers with a few inches of apple cider vinegar and a drop or two of dish soap in each one, and they caught about a hundred or so fruit flies during that same stretch of time. Henceforth, I'll just use my homemade vinegar traps.
Do not work as well as other brands. Not nearly as sticky either. Won’t buy again
These aren't really working for my fruit fly problem. The flies don't stick to the yellow shape. I've trapped a few but the flies seem to be to light to stick. I've killed more with a hand vacuum.

Let’s get 1 thing straight. The butterfly design is largely for our eyes only. It serves no purpose other than looking cuter than a bland circle or square shape. No normal bug is going to see that giant yellow butterfly and make the decision to land on it based on the shape. That being said, putting these traps in the garden was a huge pain. The paper kept ripping and removing it [even following the manual] to set the trap was just an ordeal. The adhesive is strong but inconsistent, so when removing the paper sides carefully, the paper gets caught at varying points where the adhesive is stronger, ripping the paper. It could be worse, the paper could be torn at places that render parts of it useless. The trap base is so flimsy that the included shovel is comically a useless necessity. Even after loosening the soil, I find the trap difficult to insert into the dirt. The shovel falls apart easily when in use... so again, it’s useless in function but necessary to install the trap. The traps also sway easily in the wind even when inserted deep enough. Keep well away from the leaves as it can pull parts of the leaves off. Also if you don’t change it often enough, the caught bugs are essentially ant food. Somehow ants are immune to adhesive while larger bugs aren’t... I now have ants in my raised garden beds It does catch bugs though in a relatively short span of time. Holds up to watering the plants too

Exit: after a week of use, these traps started to loose stickiness. They are no longer catching gnats and the gnat infestation is returning. This product claims to be long lasting and both waterproof and UV resistant. I don’t know if it’s due to the water or the light/temperature that has caused them to loose the ability to catch gnats. I hope the seller will fix this by sending replacement as I was excited to give this product such a great review. But am now rethinking. In just 6 hours I’ve caught 92 fungus gnats. I paired these sticky traps up with diatomaceous earth and it has been working so well. It’s definitely worth your money. These will definitely make it easier to clean since the gnats won’t just die all over the house due to the diatomaceous earth. Unlike the vinegar method, these don’t smell up the house.
Didn’t work for us. Placed 10 yellow glue stakes by several house plant, so far nothing stuck after 30 days. Do see some gnats flying around freely in the surrounding areas.
These didn’t really get anything
Sticky, but hasn't caught any knats. Isti, Didn't catch k a Didn't catch knats

I followed the instructions to put the stickers in the refrigerator for some time to help with the stickiness… still so sticky I couldn’t get the paper off without getting it on my hands or getting it stuck to something else. They do what they’re supposed to, but work like all other traps that are less sticky.
It is sticky, but the gnats just walk off (room temp at 72°F). It might work better with other insects. This product just wasn't useful for fungal gnats.

Well, I got duped by this product. It's mostly my fault. I needed a way to get rid of fruit flies that made their way into my home. I had made my own trap with apple cider vinegar and dish soap, but the vinegar was really bad and stunk up the house. I searched for fruit fly traps and came across this. I gleaned over the description and didn't bother with the reviews (two things I usually never do) and ordered it. It arrived the next day (which is why I bought it) and I opened the package. Much to my surprise and disappointment, it was just a small bottle of apple cider vinegar with soap in it. Man, I felt dumb, but I decided to try it. The cap is a joke. It makes the bottle taller when open and easier to knock over so you can spill it everywhere. Also, it leaks if you close it and don't keep it upright. I poured some into a glass pudding bowl, covered it with plastic wrap and sealed it with a rubber band around the bowl. I poked four 3/8" holes in the wrap, set it out near where the flies are and left for a few minutes. When I came back the flies were all over the bowl. Once they make their way in through the holes they're essentially trapped. You will get the same results with apple cider vinegar and dish soap with this setup (because that's all it is) and over time it'll be cheaper. Don't buy this or any other similar brands. Just make your own trap and save the $8 that I stupidly spent.

I've used Aunt Fanny's ant spray in the past with good results. So I thought, why not try the fruit fly solution for those pesky pests? Well, here's a good reason: it doesn't work very well. Sure, I see some floating in the liquid, but there are at least ten times that are still flying all around my kitchen. Then I decided to try one that sticks to the wall with good reviews. But those fell off the wall and I will return them. So I will buy the ones I should have all along, Terro traps. They worked great and I'm embarrassed that I bothered with anything else. I just wish Terro sold the liquid which how this all began.

I had a THICK CLOUD of fruit flies descend in my kitchen! Researching my options, I found this product & ordered a bottle. While I was desperately awaiting its arrival, however, I fell back on 2 older remedies—beer and apple cider vinegar. Maybe 1/8 of a cup of each of these were poured into separate clean cat food tins with some dish soap for viscosity, then covered with plastic wrap, and punctured with small holes. Once the product arrived, I could easily compare the attraction each liquid possessed. Beer? The winner! Apple cider vinegar? A very close second! Lagging way WAY behind is this product. Granted, it’s got a small number of snagged victims, but nothing like the other two. Save yourself some money. You also get the freebie bonus of finishing off the beer that didn’t get poured into the can!
Doesn’t work any better than anything else, which isn’t saying much

This item did not work for me. We followed the instructions and while it did catch some fruit flies, they mostly just hung around the open lid and didn’t actually go into the container. I feel like the design is a bit flawed with that because if the flys go in there is nothing trapping them and they can just fly out. It is easy to use but not very effective. I think if you poured the liquid into something that would actually catch the flys themselves it would work great but again I think the design is a bit flawed.
Items arrived leaking, with one container's top fully off and the other half off and the plastic wrapping the two torn. The fruit flies were having a field day on my counter!

Don’t waste your money. Good old apple cider vinegar works better. Pour 1/4c of acv into a small bowl, cover with plastic wrap and poke small holes in the plastic. It actually works better and doesn’t emit any smell like this product.

Gnats (fruit flies) just hang around the liquid but don't attempt to get in. I suggest the company to make these containers very small with a sticky plastic inside so they can get trapped. The idea is great but the designed is terrible. I don't know what to do with it but I am probably just going to have to throw it away unless the company offers a refund.
Two of the four bottles had loose lids and leaked. Waste of my time and money. Need to return.
Didn't work well. Strong smell

I put 5 of them around the kitchen 2 days ago. We have quite an infestation so was anxious to see them caught quickly. So far, only 1 has flown to and stuck to the trap, and when I last looked, it must have gotten loose and flown away! Extremely disappointed, but at least it didn't cost very much.

I am sorry to say that these have not worked at all. The little flies are still around and none are on either of the sticky sides of these traps that I have left in several of our plants throughout the house. I was so hoping that they would have worked!

Something is off, the flys just flew by a hundreds of times and did not catch any of them. Maybe the shade of yellow is off. I had similar product from other vendor before (https://a.co/d/7tUHfP1) that one just kept catching thousands!

This product was ineffective against the gnats that were swarming the kitchen. Had I not used apple cider vinegar, this product would just be sitting just like it’s now in my kitchen. I had opened and used it, gave it few days, before figuring out it was useless. The designers either should have done a better job or marketed it for its color and shape. Don’t buy it, unless you have kids who want to play with sticky shape.

Since July 15, when I received this product, not one single fruit fly, gnat, lie, fly or anything has been caught. Still looks like it did the day I set it out. It is sitting by my sink where things go down the garbage disposal where sometimes it will have a couple of gnats but nothing is ever stuck, but I will see still see gnats and flies. If there was a zero rating, that’s what I would give.
This is worthless! It's just a 2 sided sticky cutout. Does not attract the gnats or anything. Had actually ordered something different with a plug in device.

These do not work! A complete waste of money. The fruit flies are out of control this year and they don’t even stick! I feel like they’re just laughing at me. I have the stickers on planters and all over the house w nothing on them. I saw a bunch in the sink so I put one in just to check and nothing!

Save your money and sanity. This product does not work. I had it sitting in the middle of a fruit fly cluster and not one fruit fly was trapped. I had it there for weeks. No flies in the trap but plenty still annoying me flying around my kitchen sink. I also tried another brand of sticky traps similar to this and they didn’t work either. I also tried Stem and Raid organic sprays and they are oily and smell horrible (citronella and I don’t know what). They gave me headaches. What did work somewhat were those little apple traps. But nothing has been magic. Most successful has been a homemade funnel trap with pineapple juice. Hope this helps.
Waste of money.

Rip off. Did not come in a box. Came in a zip lock bag where they hardly fit. Try fitting them back in the bag, with the stingy piece of wax paper... these things stick to everything. And they didn't work at all. They seemed to attract fruit flies into my room (from the kitchen), but in almost 3 weeks, not one fruit fly stuck to the thing. (I can prove this item attracted fruit flies into my room, because I stopped bringing fruit into my room 3 weeks ago, I bought a covered garbage can, and the fruit flies kept coming into my room kept annoying the heck out of me. Not to mention they're unhealthy. I threw away the sticky fruit fly catcher and bam, no more fruit flies in my room. Still in the kitchen, but not in my room anymore. Buy yourself a covered garbage can. Keep your sink clean. Leave fruit in refrigerator or in a bag until it's ripe and then put it in refrigerator. But mostly, don't buy this brand. Whoever sent these to me was NOT the company advertised. Again...see the photos. Another 3rd party Amazon rip off.

Almost impossible to remove the screen covering the liquid and tons and tons of warnings and after saying its safe around kids and pets but also keep away from everything. The smells is also to strong
It stinks really bad and it does not work maybe one or two flies will fall into the water, but the rest of them are smart enough to just sit around the room or run away

This item stinks and only attracted two flys in one month of trying to get them to go or drown in this stink pot. My recommendation is that I will not get this product ever again and I suggest you do the same.

El olor es lo de menos,!, pero mis mosquitos pequeños continuaron igual de molestos y hace un mes lo tengo en mis mosquitos pequeños Cosina, soy extremadamente limpia no tengo frutas ni alimentos a la vista y aún hay pensé se desaparecerían pero no!!! Quizás mis mosquitos son especiales?!!
This did not catch one fly while it was sitting in my bay window for an entire week! All it did was make my whole house smell like vinegar. Does not work save your money!
It's literally apple cider vinegar. Just put some in a jar and poke some holes in the lid
Waste of money had it sitting out for two weeks they would go around it but not inside

It doesn't work. I have had it for 3 days and it hasn't attracted any of the bugs. The vinegar smell is horrible and permeates the whole room. It's states the it is safe around kids and pets but it's an open jar of smelly vinegar that if knocked over would spill all over. I am surprised a Raid product is so poorly manufactured.

I am sorry to say, that this is nothing more than Apple Cider Vinegar poured into a small container. It was almost $7.... Has caught maybe 2 fruit flies in about 4 days. They land on the edge, get a drink & fly away. Buy a bottle of ACV & use your own small container, if you want to try this

I don't know what else to say other than don't waste your money. Get a bulk size of apple cider vinegar and swish some dish soap in it, because that's what this is (and you'll likely avoid an extreme pungent smell of the vinegar by choosing a strong-smelling dish soap, as this product's smell will knock your head back). There's also the matter of the size - it's overpriced for what you get and the shape (deep and narrow) seems off-putting to them. With this, yes it caught a few but I observed mostly them perching on the edges for a few moments then taking off. Just use a white/pale shallow dish/bowl with actual surface area for them to "rest" on.

This device worked to catch a few gnats, and I suspect might work for fruit flies but it does not work house flies like the description says it does. I had my doubts but decided to try it out as I was dealing with a lot of flies this season and they were consistently working their way into the house. I put up two of these and left them on 24x7 and checked the sticky pads daily and never caught a single fly with them despite being in a heavy fly traffic area in the house. I don't recommend if your looking to remove flies. I think the light is a blue LED not true UV but whatever the reason it fails to do what is claims in my experience.

I did not like this product. I bought the VEYOFLY Fly Trap Indoor to get rid of small flies (fruit flies) in my kitchen, but unfortunately I did not see any effectiveness. Even using the device continuously and placing it on the counter where I usually keep fruit, the small flies kept appearing as usual. I used it according to the instructions and with the included refills, but I did not notice any significant reduction in insects.

One star - Do not waste your money on this useless device. You have been warned. I bought this hoping to get rid of the annoying gnats and flies that seemed to have found their way in thanks to a party and a…let’s just say misguided person who didn’t have proper aim with their fruit. Needless to say we found a nice surprise behind the trashcan along with bugs. In any case, this thing turned out to be a complete disappointment. It does not catch any bugs, no matter what kind of bugs they are or where I plug it in. I tried the living room and the dining room and the kitchen. I even bought the two pack. It didn’t catch anything, no flies no gnats not even a spider or anything else. It is just a piece of plastic that has a steady blue light that does absolutely nothing but waste electricity and take up an outlet. I had more luck, making a concoction of apple cider vinegar, sugar, and dish soap then I had with this. (And that wasn’t as great as some have made it out to be) This is a just waste of money and time. I do not recommend this product to anyone. Save yourself the trouble and look for something else. This plug in bug catcher is a scam. The worst part is that I tend to give things time to work. I ended up giving it so much time that I’m past the return window. I’d just look elsewhere for something else. Edit: I have raised the rating to 2 starts since one of them caught 1, yes 1 gnat. No flies or anything else. I have seen other reviews where it has been successful so maybe it works in certain situations. I think the description needs to be updated to depict the scenario where it will actually be useful. I still would not recommend it at all.
Bought this to control fruit flies in my kitchen. Used it for one week and caught three flies. Didnt live up to the hype

The concept of these traps is simple and typically very effective at catching flying insects. We've used similar products with excellent results and I am fairly sure these would have also worked quite well. However, I ended up returning these and buying the Safer Home traps, which looks very similar to these because these have a (completely unnecessary IMO) ON/OFF button on them that does not remember what state it was in, so every single time the power goes out, even for the shortest amount of time, I have to go around and press the button on every trap to turn them back on. Seriously people, why??? It's an insect trap and its job is to be on catching insects, so why overcomplicate things by adding an ON/OFF button? And if you really must have an ON/OFF button, make it mechanical, so it will stay in the current state. Initially when I plugged the first one in, I thought it didn't work, then realized it had that button, which is not that noticeable (and you know I didn't read the instructions). Also, while I still think these would work, I feel that the LEDs on this trap are not as effective as those on the Safer Home units and these seemed to light up the room more, even though the LEDs don't seem as bright (I think they are a slightly lower wavelength of UV than the Safer Home units use. In any case, I cannot recommend this product due to the above, as there are other choices that are better designed and work exceptionally well in the same price range.

The ease of assembly and quality of the item is well made. I like the blue light on it for a night light. However, it only catches a few of the fruit flies and one mosquito in almost two weeks. I will change out the sticky pad in another week or so but I’m not impressed. I would not buy this item again. A little bit of red wine in the bottom of an open bottle catches more fruit flies and I’ll probably go back to that.
Didn’t catch flies that would sit next to it

Zevo has always been my go-to brand and I decided to give this one a shot instead. I ordered the two plug ins with six cartridge refills and after a few days of use, one of the plug ins stopped working altogether. The light wouldn't stay lit and you'd have to press the on button a dozen times for it to MAYBE light up, only to turn back off shortly after. As far as catching things, not one fly was caught.
