A small gnat was caught after a few days of use. I returned the product and bought Zevo. Within a matter of hours, I had caught more flying pests than what I did in three days with this product. I will not be purchasing this again. If you've been a Zevo customer in the past, you should stay one. I'm giving it two stars because it did at least catch a gnat.

Product is well made, easy to put together. This product has been in use for over a week and caught about 3 fruit flies. I made sure to follow instructions for best process to catch fruit flies, and still not working.
Bugs do stick to it but same as if you used regular fly paper the attraction light is really just a gimmick or at best a cool blue night light.
These work but it's always a fight to peel the paper off the butterfly shape. Would love to have the option for all 54 of just the flower shape.

I wanted these to be amazing, but I am slightly disappointed. I'm used to getting the "name brand" rectangular yellow sticky traps from my local hardware store, but saw these for much cheaper instead. I was hoping they would still do their job, and they do catch flies, pest, etc. However, The pieces are absolutely huge! I have cut the pieces down and attach them to the sticks in order to fit better with my indoor house plans. They still catch pests, however my rectangular sticky ones definitely catch more bugs more than these.
I bought these because they are cheaper and comes with a lot more but they are not as sticky, I would not buy this one again, should have paid a little more for the previous one I bought.

Good for what they are but I would not use these long term as they are not cost effective when compared to treating the cause of the reason you are using these. Would only recommend for short term use and for that they are great. They don't smell funny or anything like that but they do have a decent enough amount of adhesive to help catch insects aand work great. They last for quite a bit of catches and are small and fit nicely anywhere you want, very minimalistic design.

These are super sticky. I used them in my houseplants and and always came out COVERED in fungus gnats after a day or two when I had a pretty bad infestation. I went through several 48-packs before doing more research on fungus gnats - what really works is a product with BTI like Mosquito Dunks or Mosquito Bits. Use them in your watering can (let soak overnight, then water). After a few months you should see a dramatic decline. I still rate the sticky traps as good at their job, and they can help cull the problem because the Dunks kills a huge percentage of the larvae, while the sticky traps catch a good number of the adults. However I think these are only a part of the equation. Also, the 'star' shaped ones are fine, but the butterfly shapes are annoyingly difficult to remove the stickiness cover part without ripping (and then you have no tab to grab to remove the rest of it - just touching super super stickiness while you fight the product). It's like the people that designed these never actually used them. I ended up developing a very careful technique again after buying MULTIPLE of these 48 packs that works most of the time, but I'm surprised it takes so much effort just to remove the film from the sticky thing.

The traps I received do not have the non-sticky part that you normally can stick in the dirt. The entire surface is sticky and leaves the glue residue all over your fingers when you are trying to place them in the plants. You get like 6 of the clips for 96 traps. I have ~30 plants so most of these I had to place directly in the dirt. Will not purchase again. The do catch the fungus gnats though so they work as expected when it comes to that, so this is more of a inconvenience when compared to other gnat traps I've purchased on Amazon.

I've had them out for about a month now. I have a plethora of indoor plants and as such have the occasional fungus gnat. While inspecting them for bugs I watched a fungus gnat walk across the surface like it was nothing. So far I've only caught one. I'm a little disappointed. The product seems sticky enough. I'm not sure why I haven't caught more. My sundew has caught more than these traps have.

I ordered the 40 pack and while the issue was almost resolved, I figured I would get another pack. These work great. Very sticky. However the smaller pack has unsticky triangles to insert them into the dirt. These all have external ways of attaching them. My hands get sticky every time
May not work so well for fungus gnats. We got some, but they mostly avoided these it seems.

It's sticky and attracts the fungus gnats. However, the butterfly shape was super hard to peel off the paper. It ripped every time even though I tried to be careful. The other shapes were ok, but the base is too wide and won't go into the dirt easily. The little shovel was cute.

It worked, I suppose? I still have some fruit flies buzzing around, and even in a clean, closed room with three of these, they weren’t completely effective. I really didn’t care for the fact that it was just a wide-open jar. Most products like this have a topper with holes for the flies to go through, but this was just open with nothing on top. Did it catch some of my flies? Yes, it caught a bunch. Did it solve my problem? Not completely. Did it work any better than the little red Apple product? No, and I think the product really suffers from not having a cover on it. Usually, those help to trap whatever flies have scented the bait but haven’t yet drowned. Most of them can’t fly back out once they’ve flown in. This doesn’t have that. It was a bit of a flop for me, but it’s still useful.

This trap has about as much success as a timeshare salesman at a bankruptcy convention. Sure, it gets their attention. Fruit flies definitely show up to the party. They buzz around it like tourists at a gift shop, checking it out, maybe taking a few selfies, but actually committing to the product? That's where things fall apart. The fatal flaw is expecting these tiny anarchists to voluntarily take a swan dive into what's essentially their own demise. Most fruit flies are apparently smarter than we give them credit for. They hover, they consider, they probably write a few Amazon reviews of their own, then buzz off to raid your banana bowl instead. Pro tip: Slap some sticky traps around the perimeter. Think of it as setting up a security perimeter for the world's most underwhelming honey trap. Without backup, this thing is basically expensive fruit fly entertainment. Does it work? Eventually. Is it efficient? About as efficient as using a fork to eat soup. Your mileage may vary, but don't expect any fruit fly apocalypse scenarios
Apple cider vinegar with dawn worked way better. I bought this hoping the smell wouldn’t be as strong but equally or more effective at getting rid of fruit flies. It was neither.
Not bad for the price, but didn't attract fruit flies as well as I thought it would.

We work are a small retail shop. I forgot how bad apple cider vinegar smells. Also, most of the fruit flies landed on the edge of the cup, and probably 25% got caught in the fluid. However, the price was cheap, so I guess I got what I paid for.
I mean it was ok. It did catch a few, but only if they happen to land in it. I wouldn’t say it attracts them.

If you are considering purchasing these to try, I would advice don't. Basically all this is, is a jar of apple cider vinegar that has likely been sweetened. They did work pretty well, but no better than a homemade solutions of ACV with sweetener added to it. At the price of $6 and something a jar, I'll just buy the bottles of ACV and make my own solution.
Does a great job of attracting them but a lot of the gnats just don’t land. I put a yellow sticky trap (sold on Amazon in a 54 pack) inside the jar and it greatly increased the catch.

It does smell strongly of apple cider vinegar. I can see it attracts the fruit flies, but after 3 days I only see about 10 dead inside. The rest are still swarming around. No idea if they die from drinking the mixture or if it attracts them and a few happen to drown.
These work but smell rancid

FRUIT FLY SEASON IS RAPIDLY APPROACHING AND I'M DETERMINED TO PREVENT LAST SUMMER'S INFESTATION. Update : I contacted the company multiple ways and multiple times. Amazon and the company totally ignored my legitimate complaint. Obviously they don't care about their customers dissatisfaction. It's not about the money - it's about blatant misrepresentation and unethical behavior displayed by both companies. I couldn't even get Amazon to include the "frequently returned" box on the item's page. I bought three bottles of auntie's product on Amazon 6 days ago to replace my DIY liquid attractant of appl cider vinegar +brown sugar +dish detergent. It's been 5 days and can confirm the other negative reviews that the product CAN'T contain the same liquid attractant - the fruit flies feed on the liquid and are not trapped - they can fly away!!! I unintentionally performed a side by side experiment using this WORTHLESS product against my attractant. Both liquids were placed next to separate hanging cylinders of bright yellow sticky fly paper. I actually watched the flies fly from the auntie's product into my liquid. Unlike the auntie product, any flies that were not trapped in my DIY liquid were trapped in the fly paper. After 5 days I had to replace the fly paper adjacent to DIY liquid. in contrast, the 3 bottles of Auntie containing who knows what had A TOTAL OF LESS THAN 20 IN THE BOTTLES AND ABOUT 10 TO 20 TRAPPED BY THE FLY PAPER. I am so angry about wasting money on products from Amazon with good reviews. This product has overall rating of 3.8. But that rating is CUMULATIVE - The much older reviews are generally positive compared to more current negative reviews. I conclude this is due to a change in formulation that neither amazon or the seller made clear. I was so pleased that Good Housekeeping had recommended the best way to eliminates fruit fly infestations in a recent article. The GH panel incorrectly included this product as the only commercially effective liquid attractant since it contained malic acid as part of the formulation. The FRUIT FLY SEASON article made me remember that I had used Aunties successfully a few summers ago and loved their esthetics and effectiveness. I already emailed a request for them to provide evidence of its effectiveness using a recent bottle. I am requesting a refund from this seller based on my results and will update the status with both responses. Meanwhile if you are battling with fruit flies, infestations made worse by the higher temperature (I live in Connecticut) in the Northeast, read below. First understand that you must use the following steps throughout the fruit fly lifecycle (over several weeks so that the original fruit flies are trapped as well as the their progeny hatched from the hundreds of larvae deposited on whatever they fed on. {I am a biochemist and patent attorney so I needed to know what was going on}. Keep in mind for DIY solutions or other traps that you must attract and rid them from escaping. Otherwise you'll have dead flies near and flying around the attractant. A single fruit fly will deposit hundreds of larvae on fruit and vegetables and existing food sources. Follow these steps : 1. Clean all surfaces daily with your typical all purpose cleaner (I use Fantastic with bleach) wherever you find these annoying bugs. For us this is primarily the kitchen in addition to the bathroom and TV room. REMEMBER THEY ARE ATTRACTED TO LIGHT AND 1 FLY WILL DEPOSIT EGGS THAT MATURE IN about 5 to7 DAYS. 2. Unfortunately surface cleaning every 1-2 days is necessary to eliminate flying flies you see and larva that will hatch that you can't. 3. Immediately thoroughly wash all fruit and vegetables (existing or new) and refrigerate. 4. Eliminate All food debris from kitchen counters, microwave, garbage cans, toaster oven, pet water and food bowls, etc. and keep everything covered or washed like used knives, glassware and plates. 5. If you think this is drastic like I did, then you will see an infestation that feels as if those little things won... 6. in the article, Good housekeeping recommended this product as using malic acid which is found in apple cider vinegar. It was part of discussion on using a DIY solution see ingredients above. Any solution must also contain a detergent which causes the flies eating on the attractant liguid to sink in the solution. They can't fly away and begin a new cycle of depositing eggs. At the beginning of the infestation I used the ingredients above along with standing/hanging yellow sticky fly paper I also bought from Amazon. I am very frustrated that I didn't do the research and work upfront instead of wasting money trying to find the non-existent magic bullet all summer!! As much as I love tasty vine ripened tomatoes and other summer fruit and vegetables, it's just not worth it. I think the high temperatures in July throughout the North East as well as nationaly/globally contributed to the problem experienced by me and almost everyone I know.

What I liked: It's easy to use. What I disliked: It arrived 2 days late. It doesn't work. It's a spill hazard. It's extremely expensive considering it's vinegar, water, fruit juice and soap. I paid $80 to catch one fly. That about sums it up. We get these fruit flies every year but this year is the worst. I've had the drains cleaned and treated professionally and I'm pouring drain cleaner and fly treatment down the drains daily. I was going to buy the decoy traps that look like an apple. I've used them before and they were great. I couldn't find any in stock, except for one that had bad reviews. Then there was Aunt Fannie's. There's no special formula even though Aunt Fannie's has a patent on their formula. It's different because first, it's not a decorative decoy trap that looks like an apple or mini pumpkin. Aunt Fannie's looks like a spice bottle. When I opened the cap, I expected to see some kind of funnel mechanism on top. That's how the other traps work. The flies are attracted by the sweet formula inside. They slide down the funnel right into the solution and can't get out. Aunt Fannie's just has a wide-mouth opening that can very easily spill. I placed 4 in my kitchen, one in the dining room and one in a bathroom - where the flies congregate as the directions tell you. I watched the flies swarm around the traps and sat back waiting for them to dive in and never check out. They landed on the bottles, walked around, climbed down inside, climbed back out, walked around a little more, then swarmed around. One little fly fell in and couldn't swim. So $80, several days of waiting, then placing these open bottles of liquid all over the place, and I caught one, single fly. Just one. I was convinced to buy this product because I saw so many great reviews. It seemed like a no-brainer when I couldn't find the product I actually wanted. I've never had this happen before. I usually buy stuff with 4 stars or better and when I do, I agree with those reviews. In this case, I was completely wrong and I've wasted time and money.

I purchased this because I saw the reviews. BUT it did not work for my house not one fruit fly met its death with this stuff. I was thinking maybe its because the opening is the size of the packaging. Most fly traps have tiny openings so they cant get back out. I purchased the apples ones as well. Once those were opened they went right to them. Lots of dead flies in those! So I believe this is a hit or miss for anyone who purchases.

I had this near my bunny cage where i often get fruit flies. I will say I poured a little in one of those tomato looking fruit fly cups and left this open to see if there was a difference because this doesn’t not have holes, just a plain open cap, neither collected flies, even days later. I will not purchase again. Does not work.

Aunt Fannie’s Flying Punch is a joke. Three bottles out for one month while fruit flies are doing summersaults around my face taunting me as they exclaim “I’m s that all you got?” Seriously don’t buy this. It doesn’t work. Just donate the money to someone who needs it on the street.

I haven’t caught not one gnat or fly I placed it on my kitchen sink counter around my plants! I have a zevo and sticky traps that work. Every thing catching them except for the punch I even sat it in the pot of one of my plants and still didn’t catch not one gnats

My high rise condo is currently having trash chute issues, so I came home from a weekend trip to a fruit fly problem. I hastily bought this, a blue light/sticky trap, and placed out a few cups with old wine and a drop of dish soap. Left all of these out for 2 full days throughout the kitchen to eliminate my new roommates. The fruit flies are all now gone, but no thanks to this product (or the light trap, while I'm at it). The 2 cups of old wine, however, successfully caught and drowned dozens of fruit flies each. The light trap managed to catch one. This product caught zero. It also reeks of vinegar, strongly enough that I notice it immediately upon entering the apartment. I highly recommending skipping this entirely and putting out old wine or juice with dish soap instead, save your money and your sense of smell.

If I could rate this zero stars or negatives stars I would!!!! This sucks I have a fruit fly infestation I have let this out and the flies are going nowhere near it. It’s literally nothing but apple cider vinegar I coilda bought a bottle cheaper . I WANT MY MONEY BACK

I have never been as disappointed in something from Amazon as I was with this stuff! I had seen them mention it on a TV show. Im thinking maybe GMA...but anyway....so then i checked AZ and it had a ton of positive reviews so I just knew this was going to be the answer to our fruit fly problem. In another few days it will be an entire month that these 2 bottles have sat out in our house where we see these flies the most. Believe it or not....not one single fruit fly is in the liquid of either of these! We've stood and watched them fly to the bottle, and even down in it but pretty soon they fly right back out! Finally yesterday I decided to top each one of them off with a bit more apple cider vinegar..thinking POSSIBLY it hadnt been strong enough originally. NOPE! It's still not working. But yet there are still people saying it worked great for them. So Ive finally started a return today since I feel like Ive given it a decent chance with no success.

I am not sure where they came from (or keep coming from), but I have a small swarm of fruit flies in my kitchen that I have been trying to get rid of for several weeks. I take my trash out daily, have cleaned my kitchen from top to bottom including the drains and trash can, no longer leave fruit (or any other kind of food) out on the counter, and have moved my cat's food and water dishes to another room. I also have tried several DIY natural remedies. Yet the fruit flies persist. I do not want to use an insecticide spray in my home because I have multiple chemical sensitivities. I read about Aunt Fannie's online -- the product got good reviews, so I thought it would be worth a try. The Aunt Fannie's FlyPunch! arrived and I put it out on the counter above where my kitchen trash can is and waited hopefully for it to work its magic. It has been five days and I am still waiting. In five days, Aunt Fannie's has trapped five fruit flies. In the same time period, I have swatted down 29 fruit flies and a sinkful of soapy water that I soaked a couple of pots in for an hour one day trapped seven fruit flies. I am really disappointed in this product -- it was definitely not worth the money. At this point, I am not sure what will work to rid my kitchen or fruit flies, but I feel fairly certain that it will not be Aunt Fannie's.
This is a hit or miss product. It did not work for me at all. It's a cheap alternative but you risk not having the results you want. Strong vinegar smell. Wouldn't recommend.

This is nothing more than apple cider vinegar in a cup! You can Google home fruit fly remedies, and they all suggest to put apples cider vinegar in a cup with A drop of dish soap so that the flies sink into the liquid. Once I open this product, it was clear that it is just apple Cider vinegar that you can put in a cup for less than a quarter. Yes, this is effective, but you can make yourself at home. Don’t spend six dollars on this.
I can't tell you if it works or not because the smell is awful. If you like the smell of vinegar airfreshener, then you'll be ok. The next day after opening it, I put the top back on!

I was expecting more for the extra money I spent on this Raid product. Like maybe a liquid product that wouldn't spill stuff all over when I opened it. When I unscrewed the lid, Raid had one of those protective plastic coverings over the opening of the jar that you have to peel back by gripping the extra piece of attached material that's supposed to make it easier to peel, but doesn't actually WORK since it's glued on so firmly. Oh well. OK. So I grabbed a sharp knife and carved away the temporary protective covering, which exposed the jar of apple cider vinegar that is said to kill the fruit flies. For the price, I expected another lid with holes in it to prevent it from spilling when I put it down near my compost pail. But no. It was just a jar of open liquid that had already spilled once when I carved away the protective covering! So I had to place the $8.18 jar of Raid in a sealed plastic sandwich bag with holes in it. I don't know yet how well it works. But here's the issue. If I wanted to create a DIY fruit fly disposal system, I would get my own jar of apple cider vinegar and put it in a sealed sandwich bag with holes in it. AND I would have saved myself $8.18! There are other, better designed fruit fly apple cider products on Amazon that have holes in the lids to prevent spillage. I just expected more from a big brand name like Raid. Silly me.

I bought this with the hopes of catching gnats. It only caught two. I watched the gnats just walk along the rim. I wouldn’t recommend it. I ended up doing baking soda and vinegar in my drain and that worked.

This was not as effective as advertised. It collected a small amount of fruit flies, but it also served as an attractant that they wouldn't touch. I had it open for 2 weeks and the amount of flies only got worse. At the end of the 2 weeks there were maybe 20 in the jar, and still dozens in the air. As soon as I closed the jar, they were gone in about a day. I would not recommend this item. It also smells awful- if using it indoors, prepare for your house to smell like vinegar-soaked work socks.

It makes your whole house smell like vinegar with a hint of sweetness. The smell is very strong and spread throughout the entire house overnight. I had to close it up and run a fan to get rid of the smell. It only caught one gnat overnight, even though there was a light trap in the same room plugged into an outlet (Zevo) that had just caught over 50 gnats in the previous 48 hours.

I have several apple cider vinegar/dish soap traps all around my house already. I bought this to take it up a notch because they aren't working! I had high hopes, until I opened the container. It smells just like apple cider vinegar. Very strong apple cider vinegar. I put it on the counter anyways, hoping there is some specific secret ingredient to attract and catch them. I have massive amounts of these awful annoying little bugs. 48 hours later and there are 2 in the Raid jar. I am so disappointed! Also, the liquid being completely open on my counter doesn't give me the best feeling. I feel like maybe a grid design on top or something to block spillage would be a good idea. I cannot recommend this product.
Draws little fruit flies. Had out on counter for a week but flies still around

I do not usually write reviews on products but felt compelled due to my disappointment in this. First- I have had this massive amount of fruit flies that have appeared out of nowhere. I ordered this, as I thought the reviews were great. When first opened, as other products said- wow! That is a strong odor that is not terribly welcoming. But I was thinking that I was more than willing to deal with it if it meant that I was fruit fly free. Sadly, those pesky bugs seemed to have outsmarted the trap and only a fraction of them have lost their life to this product. I somehow think (irrational and though it may be) that this product has somehow made them not only stronger but smarter. If you have a small amount of irritants, this may be helpful. But for a larger swarm, I am looking elsewhere.

The light on this does not attract bugs. I am having a fruit fly problem in my potted plants. I own a Zevo and it works wonderfully. So I figured I would try this much cheaper alternative. I put it out on the other side of the house, expecting to get the same results as the Zevo. I literally caught zero bugs the first night. The next night I switched locations. I put the INRAGEO in the location I had the Zevo previously, and the Zevo where I had plugged in the INRAGEO. The Zevo caught lots of bugs in the new location, and this one, again, caught zero. If I put them side by side, the light color is definitely different. So it could be that I got a faulty one.....but why chance it?

Unbelievable. These haven't caught one gnat, fly or anything else. The one I bought (same brand) from the store is full. I even moved them around to see if maybe it was a location issue, but that's not the problem. I just made a bad purchase. Buyer beware. I don't think these are the real deal.

Does not seem to attract bugs like the brand name system. My inlaws have the brand name system and it seems to really attract the small insects (including fruit flies). This one does not - even though to my eyes the blue light seems to be the same. I followed the directions and put it up the day we received it (3 November). It is in what we would call a 'high traffic' area for bugs. I just looked (9 Nov) and there is one (1) fruit fly on the sticky side while others fly around the area. Not what we had in mind. I would not purchase this again.

Would get zero stars,but not an option. Nothing but the smallest gnat in the world sticks to this. Have had plugged in for.2 weeks and there are 2 very small gnats on it. Trying to give it a chance, butttt

I actually bought this by accident thinking it was the name brand version. When it failed to trap a single fruit fly in 72 hours I decided to see if the light being emitted is actually UV or just a blue LED bulb. I use black light extensively in my recording studio so I took some phospherous items from that area over to my kitchen and held it up to the fly trap. Other than being visiable in an otherwise pitch dark room the plastic of the dayglo items were non reactive. Scam.
Absolute garbage! Didn't catch/trap anything! Replaced it with a Zevo and the very next day the zevo had caught several mosquitos! Absolute waste of money, don't waste your time and money

Yeah, it was easy to set up, but I’ve had it plugged into my room for a couple weeks now, and it literally has not caught a single bug in it. There’s plenty of little moths, and mosquitoes flying around my room. Which was particularly why I had bought this item as when I searched the features. It said that it would catch mosquitoes. It does not. I would’ve given the zero stars if that had been an option.
This product worked well for 2 days and then quit working all together. It won’t turn on. Don’t buy this product, complete waste of money.
Had this plugged in for 3 days and does not work at all! We still have fruit flies flying around.

Doesn't work and definitely not woth the money. Been plugged in for over a week and had only 2 fruit flies in it. I can still see others flying everywhere in my house. Went and got apple cider vinegar instead and caught 4 in matter of 2 days. This was a huge waste of money. Avoid and go with another option.

This is a long story. I bought canned cat food from Amazon, as usual I put it in the cupboard and went on with life. Fast forward, a few days later little bugs started flying around my kitchen, I thought they were fruit flies. For two days I put up with them then bought this. Basically this is apple cider vinegar mixed with sugar. You can make the recipe yourself, which I would do instead of buying this. Now, upon a week after the food was put in the cupboard I opened it and was attacked by these little bombers with wings. The gray of cat food was infested, hundreds with eggs. The can of food in the second tray which was covered with the standard cardboard and outer plastic was split which made a great environment for these little jerks. It took me a week to hand kill these things- thankfully they were slow. Perhaps they were not fruit flies and that is why this product didn’t work. This is not why I am giving it two stars. I am giving it 2 starts because of its application. Once the top is flipped open it is easy to tip over, it is not balanced. The top is completely open because the goal is for the flies to go in for a drink and drown. Well, unless you put this inside a mug it is very easy to accidentally tip over. The smell is nauseating, but that is not the fault of the product, sometime the “medicine” is just awful. I also think it is a little overpriced. $8. For ingredients that would cost at most $.75. I know it is the ease of use and when you are being overtaken by bugs you are willing to pay anything. I am giving it 3 stars because I am indifferent of its effectiveness. I am not sure if these were fruit flies- if they were, then this product didn’t work at all, if they weren’t then I am judging the other aspects of the product: ease of use, quality, cost. Etc. Quality: Nice looking label, The high pungent smell of vinegar is enough to make you gag- so, it is the real deal and not watered down. Ease of use: just peel off the thin protective film and flip open the lid- voila. Cost: I think it’s a little pricey for the ingredients which you could easily make yourself. (They give the recipe online) But, isn’t it easy to press the “buy now” button and have the delivery guy drop at your door 48 hrs later?

This stuff worked okay. Maybe some batches are better than others. I bought the two-pack and one seemed to attract a lot of gnats while the other caught very few gnats. I tried switching the placement to see if that helped but it didn’t seem to make much difference. It’s very easy to use-just open the package, flip open the lid and it’s ready to go. I’ve made a similar mixture before with comparable results so I probably won’t buy this again.

The product did not work for me, so I originally posted as one-star. Also, it is not eligible for return, so I was stuck with three jars. I am giving three stars now, because of the communication with Aunt Fannie's. I wrote to them hoping they could help with a return. Kelly wrote back promptly and said the return could be done only through Amazon. But, she was very friendly in e-mail and was sorry the product had not worked. She sent some questions and pictures, wondering if what I have is truly fruit flies. As it turned out and upon further research by me, what I seem to have are drain flies. I have eliminated them with baking soda/vinegar and also baking soda/essential oils. I would recommend to anyone that if FlyPunch is not working, consider another fly besides fruit flies. Also, it is worth writing to the company. I found Aunt Fannie's to be very helpful. Update 12/14/17: I have phorid flies, and they are very difficult to get rid of. My baking soda/vinegar/essential oils invention kept them at bay but didn't truly eliminate them. However, I have found a "trap" by happy accident: ruby red grapefruit juice. The little suckers are actually attracted to an open plastic glass filled with juice. It takes a while for them to figure out that they should jump right in, but they are. I am joyfully seeing dead bodies float. This, after going through frustration trying to kill them. I tried adding a drop or two of dishwashing liquid but am finding they are more attracted to just the juice alone. No ACV, orange juice, beer, or red wine for my visitors. Ruby red grapefruit juice. Who knew.

I really like this product when it works but it seems inconsistent. Some bottles I have purchased catch a zillion fruit flies and other bottles seem to be totally ineffective. Not sure what's happening there. Appreciate that there is no awful smell and the design doesn't both me sitting out in my kitchen.
I must have very smart fruit flies because I don't see this product to be very effective as of yet and it's been a couple of days

I didn't feel that this fly trap got rid of all the flies. There were a few at the top, but noticed a lot of flies that managed to avoid being near this item. I have a second one that came with the purchase and will try again next summer if it works. The strong is very pungent fyi.

Smells like an open jar of red vinegar. No film to trap flying insects in the solution, They basically sit on top of the open jar & nothing precludes the insect from flying away. Maybe it intoxicates them & they fall in & drown. Eventually some bugs do wind up dead at the bottom of in the solution. I did notice a significant decrease in the number of small flying insects in 7-10 days; just not an immediate impact. Cats do not like the vinegar smell & leave it alone wherever you put it (off the floor).

Does it work? Yes. Do I still have the same type of flies that I had before? Yes, yes I do. Have I tried moving it around where I see flies? Yes, I have. If I had to guess I have 6 flies/gnats, and the bottle has 3 of them. So I gave it a 3/5 rating for getting me 50% of the flies.

Effective, but stinky! Got all the critters quickly, but it stinks up the kitchen with strong apple vinegar smell. I have used others before that did not make such a strong smell. Next time I will just buy the little apple shaped ones. Not a great choice in a house with sensitive nosed people, and the container is quite large, always afraid it will get knocked over.
I only gave this a three because of the aroma. It does work.

Update earlier review....tried product a few more days & is attracting knats but not taking care of all of them. They are still flying around & not satisfied product works to eliminate. Will pay for product still not happy with purchase.
Was not impressed. Did not catch the flying moths. Was very unsatisfied.
Worked great until it stopped working.

I found this device for a better price than the Zevo Max when I had 2 outbreaks away from wall plugs so needed the corded option. I actually think it ATTRACTS more bugs than the Zevo Max does in same location. Have tried knockoff brands for cartridges that were not as sticky as Zevo's, and definitely didn't work as well, so I expected that going in. THESE Inrageo cartridge however are bottom of the barrel. Had both cartridges that came with it in about 6 hours when my Zevo replacement cartridge order arrived. In that 6 hrs I saw many fruit flies attracted, but they could literally walk around on the sticky part. One cartridge had 2 tiny fruit flies on it, the other had 3 or 4. I replaced the cartridge with only 2 fruit flies caught with a Zevo refill cartridge. Photo shows the bugs caught in 24 hrs time on the Zevo side of device, and 30 hrs on the Inrageo side. There are FIVE on the Inrageo cartridge side, not even going to try to count fruit flies on the Zevo side, and also caught 2 house flies. So the cartridges are 0 stars. But I do like the device base itself, which is brighter than the Zevo and attracts more flies. It is also at least $10 cheaper than the Zevo Max, hence the 3 stars. So I would recommend the device, but know the sticky part is wholly ineffective, so also order Zevo refill cartridges for effectively trapping bugs for about the same price.
Doesn’t seem to attract house flies. Needs to be in kitchen in order to trap fruit flies.
They're good replacement when time to
Offbrand. Not the same as photo. Still seems to work!
Fruit fleas not go thru
It’s not working as good as I figured it would. I’m a little disappointed.

I ordered the wrong one. The sticky cartridge does not fit securely in place like the other brand, it falls out easily when tipped over a little to far. It is triggering the sensor to turn on the blue light, which is good; time will tell how well it attracts and traps the flying insects, especially mosquitoes. Had a hard time turning the plug to keep from unplugging it everytime we have to open the mirrored door of our medicine cabinet. I will keep it as the only real issue for me is the secure fit of the sticky cartridge.

I was originally going to write my review yesterday morning. I was going to give the product 3 stars. My original review was going to stat that the product was very sticky and had a good stick power so it worked great if the bugs got on it, but it has no attractant. When I hang fly tape in my house in spring the tape would literally be covered in 2 days. I hung this up for 2 weeks and had some bugs on it, but still tons of them sitting in my window. So unless they happened to touch the trap, it didn't really help. I was going to give it 3 stars because its sticky, just doesn't have an attracting power that I need in my old farm house in spring. Also, the little hanger it comes with is a super cheap flexible plastic, so you have to figure out something else to hang it. And since its so flexible you'd almost need to tie a wire or string around the trap itself because your can't trust the little hanger. Then, later that morning, I changed my mind to give it 1 star instead. The crappy little hanger it comes with gave out and the trap fell to the floor. My cat went to investigate, and got the trap stuck on him. When I found him the trap had been stuck across his mouth, the side of his face and neck, and his paw was stuck. He had been struggling to pull it off his face and hurt his face. I had to cut his fur off his face and paw to get the trap off and had to pull it off his mouth and it made his mouth bleed. I had to give him a bath in vinegar to get his one eye open and all the stick off his fur. I will stick with fly tape that attracts the bugs and pins to the ceiling. Plus when I hang fly tape, all the bugs are on it, there are never any still hanging around my window like with this thing. Plus for $1 more you get a box of 10 fly tapes instead of one trap.

I've used these before and while never great, and pretty gross, they're cheap, so I bought them again and this time it was WAY more difficult to open and I ended up scrubbing sticky goo off my hands for a good long while and then the roll did not simply unroll as I remembered; instead, it came out twisted and I had to fiddle with it for another 10 or 15 minutes which, given how it's sticky paper, was both challenging and unpleasant. But, I had a tool shed that had gotten, for some reason, a sudden swarm of flies so I was highly motivated to get it unrolled so I could install it inside. And I finally did, and I put it right in the middle of dozens and dozens of large flies and then shut the tool shed door. The next morning, the flies were nearly all gone but there was not a single one stuck to the trap. And every day for a week I checked and every day, I saw zero to a handful of flies but never one fly trapped on the paper. Mind you, this product does not repel flies, so their absence is not due to the item working. On the contrary, it's supposed to either attract or passively trap them. It does neither. In reviewing the description, I do not see any mention of any attractant, so I guess the idea is to capture flies by dumb luck, and I guess I am just unlucky. But given how difficult and unpleasant it was to unroll and install this, I would not do it again. Indeed, I threw the unused rolls away. I remember this product from many years ago and it was easy to use and moderately effective so I do not think this is the same fly trap Black Flag has made for decades, but something cheaper and less effective.

Cannot get product our of container. Fly paper (very sticky) rolled up tightly inside what looks like an old 3mm film vile. You cannot remove without: A getting sticky goo on your hands B product is twisted up and mangled upon removal. USELESS JUNK

Fly trap? No, no, no. What this really is is an object you'll find lying on the floor at some point. You'll see it from across the room and wonder, "What in the world is that white cardboard thing? Did someone make something out of posterboard and leave it laying around the house? Maybe a cardboard airplane?" You'll bend over to grab it, and your one of your fingers will invariably touch the sticky trap surface inside. You might try to stick it back to the window, but it didn't stick the first time when the adhesive was fresh, so it won't likely stick with a second attempt. These seem like a great idea, keeping the fly carcasses out of sight, but they just don't stick. These are the ones you really want! http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00AN8BWG0?redirect=true&ref_=cm_cr_ryp_prd_ttl_sol_56 They're clear, so they don't show until they start doing their job. If you have a BBQ, or the kids and their friends are in and out forgetting to close the door behind them, and lots of flies get in the house, they will eventually go to the windows to try to find their way out. They tend to start at the top of the window and work their way down, always ending up at the bottom. So if you don't have curious children in the house, you can mount these down low. I happen to have a very high arch window in the living room, and I put two of these on the bottom of that window, at each side, and it catches so many flies! I put them in lots of windows when in and out traffic is very heavy. It doesn't take long until we notice that we haven't seen any flies for a while. Simply pull the trap off, fold it in half, and toss it in the trash! It sure beats stinky chemical sprays! The only thing more fun for killing flies is the new and improved Bug Zapper! Five bucks at Walmart keeps the kids busy for a long time, especially outside at night near the porch light!

No catchabilty. It’s fly season. I put three in the flies favorite sunny windows, after a week I got 3 flies. They sit on it, they don’t go in. I dropped in some bits of bread and honey and fried chicken. They don’t go in. I’m desperate, I cut one open, it’s only sticky on one side! I made a smorgasbord of tiny treats on the sticky side and put it on the kitchen counter. Nothing. It’s got an anti-fly something going on. The only thing that sort of works are those long sticky ribbons that are just disgusting. The reviews look like this window box has 4.2 stars. But it’s for all the fly catchers and wanna be fly catchers. Not the little triangular shaped box.

I recently purchased the Black Flag Fly Stick Insect Trap with high hopes of ridding my home of pesky house flies. My door is frequently opened by my grandchildren, which unfortunately results in an influx of flies. I despise house flies, so I was looking for an effective, hands-off solution. After setting up the Fly Stick, I was disappointed to find that it only trapped three fruit flies over a period of two weeks. Despite being placed in a high-traffic area where flies are known to gather, the Fly Stick failed to capture the numerous house flies that constantly buzz around my home. The product did not meet my expectations. It was frustrating to realize that I had essentially wasted my money, as I still had to resort to manually chasing down and eliminating the flies myself. For anyone in a similar situation, I cannot recommend the Black Flag Fly Stick Insect Trap. It's ineffective for larger house flies and not worth the investment. In summary: Pros: Easy to set up. Cons: Ineffective against house flies, only trapped three fruit flies in two weeks, ultimately a waste of money. For those dealing with a significant house fly problem, I suggest exploring alternative solutions. This product, unfortunately, does not deliver on its promise.

So I'm sure there is a fly type out there that this product, when used as directed, works well for. The flies I had, it was irrelevant to them however. SO after a few days of having this repurposed paper napkin roll left out, with syrup in the bottom dish, i have caught a total of zero flies and one tiny gnat. Fly swatter i had managed to get all the flies over the course of owning this. It didnt work. One time a fly did land on the white top thing but it needs to land on the yellow tube for the glue to work. Now that the fly outbreak is resolved i have kept it out for the 0.00001% chance a random fly will decided to land on the glue....and maybe then...the 6 tubes for $18 will have been worth the price paid. Right now, i regret the order. Not only do i have tons of packaged unused rolls that ill never use, but it was 100% ineffective and unable to catch even a single fly when at the time the room being used was loaded with flies due to forgetting to take the trash out and leaving a window cracked when away for a long period of time. Even in this environment, a small room swarming with flies, it managed to catch nothing. You are better off opening a window, or, keeping a fly swatter in reach. I wouldnt waste your money unless you are sure you have flies that will care about putting syrup or other things flies typically like in the dish, and then have them land on the yellow glue tube.

The window strips seem to be just 2-sided pieces of scotch tape. Flies fly all around it and crawl around it and somehow never manage to crawl or land onto it. For all I know it might repeal flies. Sure they are cheap but the value is zero.

I bought a 6-pack of these sticky traps to put in my garage and by the trash can outside that gets a lot of flies buzzing around in the warm months. The trash can is close to the house so I don't want to use the stinky disposable bag kind. I've had 3 of them out for 2 days, in areas that are swarming with house and horse flies, and they've caught nothing but a few gnats, a couple tiny flies, and two little sweat bees. It's not that the bugs aren't landing on them, either. I literally watched a housefly land on it, struggle for a second, pull itself free and fly away! The picture was after 3 days, before the bottom fell off and the sticky part fell to the ground. I put a Rescue brand sticky trap in the exact same spot and it's FULL of flies after just 4 days. These are a total and absolute WASTE OF MONEY. These are going in the garbage and I'll pay more for the Rescue brand traps that bugs actually get stuck to.

Garbage. I would've been better off buying two 4-packs from the dollar store. Those have never failed me. These have been up over 6 hours and NOT ONE FLY has landed. They just fly around it. Maybe it's old and the scent has faded. There's no expiration date but the year 2017 is on bottom of box. That's probably when they were made. I doubt it has a 8 year shelf life. It's still sticky but doesn't work regardless. Amazon likes to sell expired/expiring products so that's probably why it's not working. And it came late. I had to spend an extra night trying to sleep in a fly infested room. The only good thing that came out of ordering this is, I realized how useless Prime is. So much for overnight and next day delivery. The shipping logistics aren't what they used to be!

I received this product today and upon opening it, it had already been used. I mean, I’m glad to see that the product works, but it was slightly upsetting for me to receive the package in a used condition. Aside from being grossed out, I’m hoping it’ll work the same way for me.
Looks nothing like the add. We'll see if it works - too soon to tell, but it's very cheaply made. Buy the name brand instead.
I didn’t pay attention that this wasn’t the Zevo brand. Initially I was going to keep it, but it’s large in size. I prefer the smaller profile of the Zevo.
I have 4 of these in my kitchen but I still have flying insects!
Traps do not appear to be sticky enough to catch bugs.
This trap does not attract any insects.

I have a Zevo 3 feet away from this one and it doesnt catch nearly as many fruit flies as the Zevo. Ive even swapped these to see if it was the position that was causing it. It was not. I would buy the Zevo again if i ever need another cuz its just way more effective. I mean like 5 times more effective.
Caught fruit flies but not the annoying bigger flies who find their way into my house!
I’ve had it up almost a week and not one fly has been caught. So pissed.
Doesn't trap the fruit fly
Veyofly catches about half as many flys as Zevo when placed adjacent to each other.
I have had it up for days and captured nothing.
It works well, not sure how many bugs it kills or maybe I just don’t have a ton at its location. I do find some so obviously it is effective and the quality seems good.

During the summer we occasionally have a fruit fly or two but never a huge issue until this year. Trying to find a solution that takes them completely out is hard to find so we started trying everything. We set out this light where they always hangout and after a couple weeks it’s only caught a maybe 10-15… not giving it one or two starts bc it’s done the job of attracting them and if you have a small issue but definitely recommend just doing dawn dish soap and apple cider vinegar in a shallow bowl is you have a major problem. The home remedy caught threes times as much as this trap.
We had such high hopes. It didn't work cery well for us. At the most we got a gnats. Wouldn't buy again.
After 3 days the trap caught 1 fruit fly, apple cider vinegar in a jar caught over 10.
Had them for a week and only got a few bugs.
Not many flies have been trapped by the light but the amount of mosquitos has diminished a bit it seems.
the glue doesn't really keep bugs stuck (moth and German cockroach both escaped after I saw them stuck). THe scent does not attract fruit flys, they seem to just ignore it.
Does not catch as many bugs as competitors units.

Purchased this and the fruit ninja liquid traps at the same time after 1 week the liquid trap had a ton in it this had 2 stuck to it literally 12 inches apart both by the sink sink flies are gone but not because of this.

i refuse to ever buy this again .i recieved it on sep.152025 two of them they have a little night light on them one is in my special needs sisters roon it catches nats well it needed changing i followed wht is says to do i took the one that had nats on it. and put a new one in the light did not come on call amozon sorry outsided the rtime fram the day was 10-14-2025 so my word is do not buy rip off 1 star they make you put or i would not give rhem no stars
I have had this in a heavy fly spot (there was some flooding) for like 2 weeks. I have filled a few fly tapes and there is still zero flies on this trap. I would have given a zero but not allowed.

This does not work. It's just a sticky trap with a blue light plugged into your wall. I only caught a few bugs on the sticky place. It's on the back of a square piece about 3 by 3 inches. It makes no sound waves or anything to encourage bugs to come to it. I wasted my money.

We have had some fruit fly issues. I purchased a 2 pack and installed them in close proximity to the issue and each other. Not a single fruit fly or any other flying insect have been caught by them. We have been joking about how ineffective they are, calling them our “fancy blue nightlights” as that is the only purpose they serve. I do not recommend buying these. They do not work at all.
I have not found veyofly trap to be effective at all.i am finding the cheaper sticky ribbons more effective.

This product is horrible. I never do reviews, literally ever. But I just had to warn people to not buy this. I had high hopes as I really had a bad gnat problem, but this didn’t even catch a single one of them and it’s been plugged in for four days if not longer. I’ve turned off all the lights around it, I even put an apple cider vinegar trap by the light, nothing helped. I got the box of two and put them both in two different locations where it should’ve helped, but it didn’t. Neither of the traps caught a single gnat. A waste of money.

I read other reviews of this product so I know it was probably not going to work but decided to give it a try.I have many fruitflies at the spot where I put this to the test. After two days, it had captured nothing. I then put my homemade trap of plastic cup with banana chunks, water and a little detergent covered with plastic wrap punctured with very small holes. After several hours, the cup had captured at least 20 flies. And the winner is - homemade!
Don’t like these as well as the one with the bulbs. The bulbs ones are amazing - these don’t work as well .

I went into this purchase in the hopes that I could get rid of the annoying little gnat and fruit flies that keep getting into my home office. I followed the directions to the letter. The product just did not work. The quality of the base unit is good and it even has a rotating two prong socket so you can have the unit mounted straight up, or to the side. Sadly, the sticky cards just did not entice those pesky flies, and I have to chase them down with a old fashioned fly swatter. I am mad at myself for wasting $20 on this purchase. I will not recommend this product to anyone. I called Amazon about my disappointment in the product, but was told that since I had opened the box, and I had opened one of the fly sticky packets, that I could not return the product. Lesson learned.

I saw so many positive reviews I was excited to get this product and finally find a way to get rid of my fruit flies. They landed near it and on other sticky yellow traps I have (took care of a few by hand), but not one found my Black Flag fly paper attractive enough to actually land on the four strips I've had hanging for the last two days. Disappointed! NOTE: Will come back to change my review if things change.

Back story: we had a sudden influx of aggressive flies that appeared after we had the gas company contractor came to repair a wall in our basement where the washer and meter were. They replaced the mercury regulator. I kept accusing the husband of leaving the door open for the cats too long. I asked our WhatsApp ladies in the cul de sac if they had any more flies than usual. None of them did. One did suggest that flies loved natural gas… while trying to figure out the source and deal with the problem I bought several of these because they were cheap and would arrive the next day. I hung it in our kitchen and filled it with syrup and the flies ignored it. My research also indicated that they might be drain flies… But none of them had the flight profile, wing shape that I could see when they alighted to say for sure. Inspiration struck and I poured boiling water down the basement drain that our washer drains into that also protects if the water heater leaks and that seemed to stem the influx of new flies. Also, I noticed more flies in my Zevo blue light traps and when viewed up close it does appear that we had drain flies. As others have said, gnats seem to love the yellow sticky sticks so I count that as a win. It catches the overflow from the Zevo. The yellow sticks are very sticky and have no odor like I remember the old fly paper wands. While it didn’t work for me that doesn’t mean it wouldn’t work for others.

We had an overabundance of flies this past summer. The flies did not seem to be attracted to this. I tired a different product and the flies piled up on that so fast I had to throw it away after two weeks. The other product was purchased at WalMart and was the rigid tube sticky fly paper. I would recommend that over this any day.

Since I don't have many places to hang these where I need them I like to keep them in the box with 3 sides open. Box flimsy and hard to cut to open the 3 sides. Don't catch as many flies like a different brand that actually has perforations to open 3 sides on the box. Other brand significantly more expensive but works better. Not going to mention other brand name but do a search and you will see it. Comes in 4 or 6 per box. Box mostly white and red in color.

I bought this to get rid of one fly. That pesky fly would buzz my head once or twice a day for several days. After it flew by, it would disappear. Normally I would hear it or see it again, but not this fly. No, this fly was crafty. After he buzzed my head, he'd be gone. I had a can of flying insect killer nearby, but by the time I grabbed it the fly would be gone for the day. This product arrived and I happily hung it up in a nice open area, in plain view of any fly. However, the fly ignored it. I don't know if it has an attractant built in, but whatever it was, my fly didn't care for it. After a few more days, my fly stopped coming. I assumed it had died somewhere. Then, after a two-day absence, my fly reappeared. This time I was able to grab the can of insect spray and locate the fly. He got a good dose and that ended the life of the fly. The Black Flag product waved the white flag and went into the trash, untouched by any flies. I mounted the fly's head on my wall as a trophy of our week long struggle.

Bought this 6-pack because my dad leaves his doors open like he's trying to invite every fly in a 3-mile radius. Followed the instructions exactly and used high-quality Kirkland organic maple syrup as bait (because I'm fancy like that). After two days? Caught maybe six gnats. Not a single housefly, which is what I was actually trying to kill. Yeah, the stick is sticky—credit where it’s due—but that’s about all it has going for it. Without a real attractant included, this thing’s just a sad yellow pole waiting for bugs that don’t care. Unless you already have a killer attractant recipe or just enjoy watching flies ignore your traps, I wouldn’t recommend it.

I've tried putting them in the flight path of the flies that zip by this way and then that but they don't lite on it. I'm not sure they land anywhere; I can't even swat them. It's as if they're desperate to breed, not eat or rest and they just keep going until they run out of energy and die. I wish these would attract them but they don't seem to. Once I tried adding a bit of canned dog food to the cavity at the bottom. It may have helped a tiny bit but I'm not sure and I hate to have to add rotting dog food. :) I tried a Victor M380 Outdoor Reusable Fly Magnet once and that sure did attract flies. But it stank too much to use indoors. I've hung these between the lights in my grow tent. I may have caught two in as many weeks. I hung one on my back porch. That one has caught several flies but those weren't the ones that bother me. They drive my dog crazy just zipping by. I hate to use spray because I have to pick up water bowls, search for hidden treats, etc. but that's what I usually have to resort to. I was hoping if I gave these a second try they'd maybe work this time but no such luck.

The fly paper is easy to prep and is certainly sticky, but there doesn’t appear to be any embedded attractant. Flies simply do not care about the paper at all. I’m sure if a fly accidentally bumped into it, it wouldn’t be able to leave. I looked at the company’s other products and noticed that some say to apply syrup or honey to them (the product I bought had no such instructions) so I tried that. Still nothing. We have so many flies, so having 3 of these up for a couple of days with zero flies falling victim to the paper is surprising and disappointing. The fly paper does have a smell when you first open it, but it’s not terrible and I haven’t noticed it spreading to the rest of the room in any way. If the produced attracted flies in any way, I’m sure it would be an excellent product. But as it stands, it’s just useless.
Sticks to everything. Messy. Got two flys in a week!

My first time purchasing this fly paper. I had a problem with fruit flys in my apartment and I thought these would help. Definitely didn't help with the bugs but stuck to everything else, a mess waiting to happen. I'd say the consistency is something like a very thick sticky honey or like tree sap. I do not recommend this.

First off I'll tell you that the sticky part works. I found that out first hand when I placed it on the counter to grab honey to add in the little dish, and my cat decides to inspect this new thing on the counter. He touches it with his paw when my back is turned, FREAKS OUT, and then jumps, while the trap is stuck to his paw! The trap manages to get stuck on his back and I turn around and catch a glimpse of orange dash by with the trap attached. He's meowing the whole time and I'm freaking out because now he's pissed and is hissing and hiding and running. Eventually I catch him and try to take the trap off, but that sucker is on there good. So I rush him in the bathroom, place him in the tub, and this stupid cat jumps while I'm holding the trap! It comes off him, but chunks of glue is missing for parts of the trap and other parts of it have chunks of hair. I quickly grab him again in the tub and turn on the water. I don't want him to lick the remaining glue so I need to wash it off. Well. WARNING: IT DOESN'T WASH OFF. Freaking out, I lock him in the bathroom and Google what to do. It says use oil. So I grab some vegetable oil and rush back. My poor cat is sitting there meowing his little heart out. I grab him again, place him in the tub, and pour the oil over the glue spots. I begin massaging out the glue, his fur is all weird. And it starts to come out. Thank god. He doesn't let me finish washing out the oil before he runs. But we got it out and he's in no danger. So.. yeah. adding the honey sucked because it's a tiny little dish. And the little clip to hang it up is super small. But it came in fast and its super effective. I just don't think I'll ever buy more of these and try to find something ready to go.
Didn’t work

These are very sticky which is what everyone would want. The problem with mine was when I pulled out the top portion to extend the sticky trap it stuck to itself so much that it wouldn't expand like it was designed to do. So instead of a wider surface area I wound up with a long slender trap. Still works but I'm a bit disappointed that it's not working the way it should.

In the Oklahoma area right now, the flies are so bad, it is like the one of the plagues from the Bible! Inside, outside, everywhere! Our neighbor got these and recommended them to us and after reading a lot of the reviews, I decided to give these type of "fly traps" a third try. I have used this type (not sure if they were the same brand as this) of trap 2 other times in my life and I was unhappy. They caught mostly gnat type guys and the tiniest little flying insects that I have never even seen before - if they were alive and flying around, they would be invisible to everyone around... Long review short..... When these tube traps were delivered this last week, I was so thankful and excited and put up 3 of them in the rooms where these diabolical kamikaze flies congregate... followed all the instructions.... They have been hanging just waiting to catch some flies but to No Avail! Not one actual Fly has been caught up on them..... And as I am inspecting them 5x a day, hoping to see a dead fly, I get dive bombed by the flies that are still alive in my house! Like they are taunting me! I am glad it seems to have worked for some of the other reviewers.... but these have not and are not working for me. I really wish they would work for these flies, because I am loosing my mind LOL Any advice is welcomed please! I tried to "ask a question" instead of leaving this review but I couldn't.
I hung it in the barn. A bird got stuck on it. It is very sticky and catches everything that lands on it!
Hard to open and unroll, must be a technique to it that I don't get, what I did get was sticky fingures that nail polish and alcohol could not remove.
Nasty looking but, this works great for the lil pesky ones! Good price shipping not bad

Really supper sticky - certainly will catch flying pests without lure, but nesting chickadees got stuck to traps & despite carefully bathing birds in olive oil - twice - with a rinse in soapy water (dish soap) birds died. Too many feathers were stuck on the trap. Perhaps others may want to know this trap can be harmful to birds.
Does not attract or kill flies. Very lower performance than the old one that I had. Sufficiently sticky.
They catch flys but it is real hit and miss. Bait doesn't work for me. Works best on its side and not vertical.

I have bought lots of these types of products in the past, so I may be a little extra critical. These work just as well as any and come with all the extras I like (the green sticks to stick them in the dirt are my favorite). I like that they don't have the triangle under the shape - that never works for sticking it in the dirt and look silly if you would rather hang them. The little shovel is worthless and they should save the plastic. I bought this set because my previous favorites are no longer available and these seemed to be the closest thing. What makes them not as good - the paper is MUCH harder to get off these! I hate fighting with them and getting them stuck to my fingers. The shapes aren't as cute as those I've had before. The mushroom and bird are fine, the bug and butterfly are from a strange angle and look weird. OK, but I'll look for something else next time.

The sticky traps caught a few fruit flies, but they do not work as good as the liquid traps. We put them all around the house and most of them have not caught anything yet. The metal tabletop stands it came with also tip over easily and the traps themselves are a lot bigger than I thought they would be. Not a good choice if you have smaller plants.
Some worked some didn’t. Maybe my fruit flips/gnat were on to it lol

The starburst was fine; the butterfly set was not. Something wrong with each butterfly sticker. The paper could not be peeled off as easily. The backing paper shredded when trying to peel it off, and glued itself to the sticker. We're they old? We're they heated and cooled? Something wasn't right. So, half the order was not good.
This product is ok.. the fruit fly take there time to stick

Sticky traps might do well for a sick houseplant...but they were powerless in the face of a major infestation. I had bugs all over my kitchen cabinets, walls and ceiling due to the compost container not be emptied in a timely way. The flies did not seem to be attracted to the trap at all unless it was in a spot where large numbers of them had gathered, and even then the traffic was light. Many of the traps had no customers at all. In fact I actually watched some of the flies check the trap out and then go around it....so they are not stupid! But what DID work, and work very quickly, was my vacuum cleaner. My little Hoover sucked those puppies up without mercy, after which I merely changed the bag. It has taken several sessions to really get them all, and I still have to treat my plants and the drain (where they lay their eggs) to keep from being reinfested, but this is soooo much better than resorting to chemicals or waiting forever for them to throw themselves onto a sticky trap because that was not happening.
This product is not as attracting as I thought it would be.

Does what it’s supposed to do. However, the complex shapes make it so the paper rips very easily when you try to peel it off. I took these photos after *carefully* peeling. Definitely needs a better design, I end up having to use my finger nails to pry most of the paper off and get sticky every single time. (Second photo is the point I give up and just stick them in my plants). Will look for more basic shapes in the future.

There are three traps in the photo attached. The one to the far right is completely bugs, however the other two are dirt. I don't know how or why but all of the traps pictured either one side or both side are completely covered in dirt, making them ineffective. I've replaced these three times and while they do catch some bugs, my plants are still covered in bugs. I had to use both these and bug spray, which I was trying to avoid at all cost, but as you can see using the traps alone my plants are still suffering from the bugs. As for the traps themselves, they are sticky but they are not sturdy. I know a large complaint is the shovel. I didn't agree originally with that being a waste of plastic, I thought it was super cute and would be so helpful, but your finger is much more effective for digging up dirt than the shovel is. It's like taking a plastic spoon and trying to dig but the end pops off.

I bought these for a white fly infestation on my indoor Hibiscus. They are not easy to assemble..which is silly because it is a simple design. I didn't find the flies attracted to them but by shaking the plant branches the flies swarmed out and waving the sticky traps around them caught many. I'll be using a pesticide when I bring the plants back in for the winter.

First few times ordering from this company, the fly sticky traps were great! On our last order all of the fly traps came with a small hole on the top which wasn’t a deal breaker just not great. But about 1/2 of the traps came with only one side sticky! I didn’t notice it until I used a few already or I would’ve returned them. I don’t recommend this company… obviously the quality control went down dramatically.

These did not catch a *single* fruit fly for me. I set them up in every room. Even tried putting the well-known vinegar concoction next to them. I don’t know what I could have done wrong or if my fruit flies are of a different breed lmao Two stars for the cute packaging though lol and ease of use

I have over two dozen indoor pools plants, seedlings and herbs. I also have a compost can nearby. Fruit flies everywhere! (We also live on a hobby farm and have an abundance of peaches, pears, grapes, figs and muscadines. I strategically placed 6 of these and after a week I had about six flies trapped. I purchased a “bait-based” trap and after 3 days had about a dozen and no new ones On this product. Sorry- I liked the idea but the function doesn’t work.
I had these all over the house and caught maybe three fruit flies. They totally ignored this item.
It's nearly impossible to take off the covering as the sticky has hardened. Some the cover won't come off at all. Either old or overheated. I've never had this problem before with these type.
This trap only caught 3 young gnats. As you can see in the picture, the other brand I have on the right (Safer is the name) caught SO many more.

This does not catch nearly as many bugs as the brand I had before it. I got this one because it came with better sticks and holders, which are nice but the sticky traps themselves don’t work as well for me sadly. Also harder to get the paper off them.

With 22 house plants had a major infestation and these traps were catching and killing hundreds, but not putting a dent in their proliferation. diatomaceous earth completely eliminates these bugs. After months of filling these sticky pads a friend turned me on to the Diatomaceous Earth, watched a YouTube video, dusted the top of all the plants soil, and less than one week later "poof!" No more gross bugs. These traps are great to see if they are present, but Diatomaceous earth is a game changer.

I have purchased a different brand in the past and thought this was a good deal....more for my money. However, each flower/butterfly sticky is covered with a protective paper on both sides. Great idea, I thought. No....... some paper pull off easily and others come off partially. It defeats the purpose of a sticky patch when it’s covered in paper that won’t come off. Even when I tried to scrape it off with a fingernail, my hands were so sticky I couldn’t do anything. I’ll gladly spend more money on the other brand
half of the gnats just ran across it. it did catch some but very hit and miss.

Serious design flaw! All the gnat traps I've used in the past had one portion, the part you stick in the soil, that was NOT sticky. These are sticky all over so are almost impossible to peel. I couldn't get the cover paper loose because there was nowhere to get it started. It took me several minutes per piece to peel them! Other brands take a few seconds. They ARE very sticky and started collecting gnats right away. But they're so sticky they stuck hard to my hands and destroyed any plant leaves they touched. Because I already used them to discover how bad they are, I can't return them so they're going straight in the trash. I wouldn't want to subject anyone else to them so am not even giving them to Good Will. If I could give negative stars I would, just for the aggravation factor.

Our fruit flies conveniently flew by it, sat near it and had a great time without being bothered by this product. I don't know how it worked for other people but for us, not even 1 fruit fly sat on it.

Only bought it to combat the fruit flies in the kitchen a few months back. NONE were interested in these patches, we tried for weeks, unfortunately. These have no smell at all, chemical or otherwise. I do not know how these perform with gnat though.

I have bought these for several years, and they worked well for trapping fruit flies. I started having a problem with the flies again, so I ordered a large 120 pack to have plenty on hand. Well, I completely wasted my money because they no longer work! The fruit flies will land on them and then just fly off because they aren't sticky enough. They have made these extremely cheap now, and they are a total waste of money I would give them zero stars if I could.
It’s sticky but somehow fruit flies do not stick to it

The original items I bought several years ago were large bright yellow recangles with a sticky coating. They weren't cute or pretty, but they really cought the meal moths. This time what I recieved was a pack of flower shaped 2" circles placed on a triangle like a cutsey flower. They are next to impossible to stick on the plastic sticks they included and are just plain useless. Absolute rubbish plus the plastic sticks don't look to be recyclable. I'll figure out how to use them because it's what I have, but don't make my mistake

DOES NOT WORK ON FRUIT FLIES. DO NOT WASTE YOUR MONEY. I HAVE SWARMS OF FRUIT FLIES AND NOT A SINGLE ONE WAS CAUGHT ON THE STICKY TRAP. I HAD TO MANUALLY PUSH A FEW FLIES INTO THE STICKY GLUE AS THEY WERE SITTING ON THE VERTICAL EDGE OF PRODUCT. THEY WERE LITERALLY USING IT AS A HANG OUT...SITTING ON THE EDGE AND FLYING AWAY. THIS PRODUCT IS A JOKE!!! DO NOT BUY!!!!!!!

I bought these because I've been inundated with those little fruit flies. Many people said they worked great in the reviews, but for me, there are maybe a handful of flies on the 6 traps that I put on over 3 weeks ago. They don't work for me
Useless for fruit flies. Placed these around the house and no fruit flies got stuck on them.
WORTHLESS!! PUT 7 OF THE OUT NOT ONE HAS CAUGHT ONE BUG! NOTHING!

When you unroll these by pulling them down, they won’t stay extended. As soon as you let go, they curl right back up. I tried using packing tape and attaching a screwdriver for a little extra weight to keep it straight, but after about a day, even that small amount of weight caused the hanging hoop to separate from the strip and fall to the ground. Unfortunately, the design just doesn’t hold up under even minimal tension. I won't buy them again! Don't waste your money!

No matter how slow you attempt to open, or how you twist it to avoid tearing, it seems like all of the glue is only on one part of the trap and it’s hard to get open.
