The first time I bought a brand name fly strip and I regret it. The first one didn’t pull out properly didn’t separate. Had to throw that away. The second one did not separate properly, but at least it was somewhat usable from now on I’m going to use off brand fly strips because apparently buying brand new means nothing now
I hung this in the kitchen where more houseflies are near. I did NOT catch even one single fly with this. I took it down after a few months. Did the company test this before they sell?

Don't waste your money. I put it up a week ago and still nothing. I realize it doesn't specifically say it's good for gnats, but I thought I would try it since nothing else I have tried seems to work. (and, yes, I have tried apple cider vinegar and a drop of soap.)
They do not attract flies, been hanging for 3 days and not one fly on it but they are flying all around the magnets and my garage 😡
Could not catch a fly if you gave it directions. It was a waste of money.
TERRIBLE PRODUCT! IT IS SOOOOO OLD THAT YOU CANNOT EVEN GET THE STICKY PART OUT OF THE CONTAINER

I saw mixed reviews so I tried it. Usually they suck with big flys.Flys.. but gnats are usually dumb and you can always catch those!.. Nope! I put one up in the afternoon.. 2 actually in the high traffic parts.. and we have TONS of gnats. I saw not only saw no Flys on it hours later.. but I saw lots of Flys get real close like they were gonna land.. then sped off away from it fast! They must not like something on here.. they should be attracting them not chasing them away! Sigh. I woke up the next mng thinking maybe I need to give it a night where ppl aren't walking around and be patient.. NOPE. not one single gnat. They were chillin ALL around it! Smh like they knew.. lol now after I returned it the next day... I looked and saw 3 lol and no more since then. That was yesterday. STILL I could have killed three with my hands.. that's how I've been killing them.. lol so this is pointless! (Tip ❤) when you wanna catch a gnat with your hands and kill it that way.. with a clap.. get your hands wet with lots of water! When they feel wind they fly off. When they feel water droplets pulling them in.. They are trapped! 😉) works like a CHARM

this is impossible to set up by one person. you have to twist it flat, which i understand, but it is impossible to do alone. every time the end slipped out of your hand, it would spring back, and you would lose just about all of the progress you had made unwinding. thus they are not able to be used just hanging from anything, as springing back, you just had a short tight curl, instead of a hanging strip. the tacks for the end that they sent were absoluty useless, and i tried to just stick the end of the strip to the wall and it would not stick, meanning that end had to be held in one arm while the o keep hold of the first end to be able. i am now waiting for another delivery from Amazon for something to tack the ends of these things to top of doorway, or across open areaways where they would actually have the opportunity to perform the job they were bought for intending to do. and, in touching the tape with your fingers, it took me an extremely long time to get my fingers to get unsticky. numerous washes, and rubbing. if i am able to finally be able to use these things, I will get back to you with an update.
I placed these all over the place, and they did catch fruit flies, but did not make much difference in getting rid of them. I’m still fighting the situation..
One of the packages was very difficult to peel the paper off of the plastic. Had to try and it took multiple tries w/ small amount of paper coming off each time. The second package worked fine
Wasn’t impressed. Cheap enough but not really needed for plants. However did catch a few flies & gnats, I sat up around fresh veggies brought in side from garden.
It did not meet its specified features did not capture any fruit flies
You think if you put enough out you should get a few flies but no
I would NEVER buy these again. Too sticky. EVERYTHING will stick to it. Bees, flowers, leaves, fingers and lizards. I feel so bad about the lizard. :(
Useless. Doesn’t catch flies or any flying insect
I guess if insects fly into it by accident, they'll stick
The paper is extremely hard to remove the trap structure is very flimsy. Will not buy again.
I do not think that they work any better that the easier to work with products.
Bugs flew everywhere in my house except onto these traps. The only thing that stuck to them was my husband's fingers, my cat's paw, and the rice I used to stand them up in a bowl.
These did not work at all. Maybe others have bad luck. I do not recommend.

These fruit fly sticky traps do not catch many of my fruit flies. I have had them out for 3 or 4 weeks and they have only captured 6 flies and they are placed within inches of where I ripen my tomatoes and figs. I still have fruit flies getting into everything. The little clear plastic cups with liquid bait work much better even though they can be messy. I would not recommend this product.
This one does not work well (only a few bugs stick on it) as compared to the one I used before. I have many indoor plants and there are many small black bugs fly in my house.

These are cute and easy to use, and feel very sticky. But unfortunately the bugs just didn’t care about them. I bought these after a new houseplant turned out to have aphids (shudder!) and I was trying to save it with both liquid treatments and these. But the aphids just ignored the yellow things entirely, even after trying with a new one several times. Keeping on hand in case of other pests, but not expecting much.

The product I selected for color said beige. It is not beige it is yellow. Please correct your selections I thought the picture was just a general picture and yellow being one of the colors not that they put yellow for beige. My Knat problem was too big to send back and wait for a new order. I really dislike the yellow and it clashes with my plants and walls.

I really wanted something that would attract / catch annoying house flies. I'm not an expert on species of flies. It doesn't advertise "house flies" so I guess I can't be to disappointed. It is sticky, I guess it would catch anything that landed on it. The problem is if it doesn't attract the flies, the likelihood it will catch anything is very slim :-(

While I maybe caught two or three gnats with these, I had them placed in various areas of my house and could not tell that they did anything. No trace of gnats being trapped on them and the gnat population did not decrease. Would not buy again.
Unfortunately this was a gimmick. I’ve had them for weeks now and the most they have attracted are 6 gnats. Not the miracle I was hoping for, very disappointing.

These are the worst! Yes, they are super sticky but getting them into soil is near impossible. Even after watering to soften the soil, these do not want to stick into the ground. The adhesive got all over the place and tore up some of my plants when they got stuck too. I ended up cutting these and putting them around the rim of my pots. Very disappointed.
The quality in the thickness of plastic, stickiness & ease of removing covering of sticky pads was disappointing
Sticker ( glue) is very poor
If flies pass by they stick but they don't seem to be attracted to it.
They're in every house plant, I have gnat but none are on the sticky traps
Gnats are not attracted
It's overall usage was not effective enough for me
It works
These were NOT very sticky and the bugs escaped.

Because I was too eager to clean up those flying insects and didn't take pictures. I thought this post could attract those flying insects, but in fact it was just the flying insects hitting it. I put a glass of water next to the flowerpot to have the same effect and even efficiency. Higher, which disappoints me.

I had lots of fruit flies after bringing in tomatoes from the garden before frost. Suddenly had all the flies so I ordered these traps because of appearance and description. Fruit flies were still all around but not attracted to the traps. I placed them in the kitchen and in some plants. Wish flies were more attracted to the traps!

I wanted to like the ease of use and disposability of this product, but it simply did not work and in my experience, was poorly designed. Like most fruit fly-killing concoctions, it had vinegar and dish soap. But judging by the scent, that was about it. No sweetness I could detect (admittedly I don’t know what they are attracted to precisely).Judging by the way they swarmed fruit juice or wine, that seems like a requirement. Also, while the slim container is convenient, a short open glass provided significantly more surface area, which presumably made it easier for them to land. Bottom line, the side by side trial I ran with a glass of red wine, white wine and this product. No comparison. Wines in a landslide of fly carcasses.

This product does work, sort of, for attracting fruit flies. However for the price, you can do just as well to buy the little red plastic apple baits at the grocery. Even better, just use cider vinegar in a trap, because that's basically what this is. I use it as an attractant for a heavy duty fly stick, but red wine or cider vinegar work just as well. I didn't actually catch a whole lot of flies in the little jars.
Not as powerful as making home remedy

This has been a particularly bad year for fruit flies and gnats down South, and so I needed a little extra help. I love Aunt Fannie's household products, but this one is not one that I will purchase again. The solution inside these containers do kill the pests, but it also attracts them. For every 10 little darlings floating in the bottle, there are 20 more buzzing and crawling around the container, which defeats the purpose. Also, the open container design emits a very strong vinegar odor throughout the house...which I suppose is better than a chemical smell, but it still is unpleasant. The open container design also invites spillage is accidentally knocked over, which is easy to do when participating in food prep.

I wanted this product to work on my influx of tiny flying insects. Called fruit flies. We found them moving up & reproducing. This product didn't work well for us. Thank you!. I so wanted to be successful . I wanted to it to make iyy outdoor

The products was supposed to attract the flies and kill them, right? Well the flies are still buzzing around happily. If however, the product was meant to be Birth control for flies, its efficacy remains to be seen I suppose.

I can’t speak to how well this works but I can tell you almost everyone of the 6 I bought leaked. I could smell vinegar before I opened the box, when I got into it the box was wet as were each of the jars. Every lid was loose and even with an inner seal they had all leaked. What a mess, had to clean them up before even trying to use them. I can’t imagine ordering any more.

I bought two, and when I open the lid, I get lots of little fruit flies around the jars -- and most especially on the lid rather than inside the jars. It seems to attract more fruit flies than I saw initially. I tend to wonder if flies from elsewhere (outside?) are finding their way inside to get to these little jars. I have cleaned and cleaned, and I can't seem to find a source of these little bugs. I have no fruit sitting out, or any other exposed food. I'm wondering if they're coming from the kitchen drain, so I bought some stuff to pour down the drain that's supposed to work for killing a variety of flies, but it hasn't arrived yet. So the fight continues... sooner or later, I'll rid myself of these little buggers.

Our office goes through periods where we seem to be deviled by very small flying insects, they could be gnats or fruit flies, we don't know for sure but this product has the ingredients that are recommended to work on either. We have never found a dead insect in the bottles and we still had the problem on going. We didn't have that many but they were persistent and annoying. We are in a lull currently and I'd love to say it was this product but neither of the two bottles I bought have shown any dead in them so so far, we'd have to say they didn't work for us.

This product didn't work as advertised. I had ordered another similar item and also used a "homemade" solution of apple cider vinegar and a couple of drops of dish soap. The other approaches worked well, trapping several dozen fruit flies/vinegar flies. This only trapped three.

The lid seems to just be there for cosmetic effect. It closes, it seems secure, but you can still smell the vinegar coming through. Turn the bottle upside down, and it leaks out pretty heavily. I discovered this flaw after my cat knocked the bottle over and, closed lid and all, about 1/4th of the fluid leaked out everywhere. I'm sure it works well as a product, but a product you can't secure and will you can smell through the lid isn't great. I don't know if it's jut my lid that's the problem, but the packaging seemed to have quite a few flaws.

The first time I ever used this product it worked great! So I decided to order it again, and now it lo longer works. It hasn’t caught one fly at all! Not at home nor at work. Disappointed. And it evaporates way quicker than the original!

Although generous amount of liquid and super simple to set up, it just isn't doing the job. These are definitely fruitflies & this product isn't working well. After setting next to kitchen sink there's about 10 dead ff in the container, but at least 50 still flying around. Last year when I had fruit flies I microwaved to warm up a bit of apple juice & set that out & the ff loved it, but wanted something easy so tried this Aunt Fannies. I believe the liquid is basically vinegar with some sort of dish soap in it. I can't recommend this product to others.
...and around, JUST NOT INSIDE!! Ha! It definitely attracts fruit flies but they just refuse to fly into the stuff. I did better with cider vinegar and soap.

I hoped that this product would help, but the fruit flies were not even attracted to it. The smell is horrible. It's vinegar. But I wish I could say it worked. But it didn't. I have more luck with the sticky flower pads, they work wonders and catch many fruit flies.

Ok, so I was so hopeful when I saw the reviews for this. We had a fruit fly infestation for a few days, i managed to get it mostly under control (thanks to my dyson and quick reflexes) with just some lingering fruit flies. I would see maybe 3-5 at a time. I put out this solution as directions stated for 5 days… not ONE fly succumbed to this. Very disappointing. Continuing to hunt these things with my dyson, down to about 1-2 a day now. Wish me luck! And don’t waste your money on this

Apple cider vinegar & a few drops of dish soap is cheaper and exactly the same as this. The active ingredients are malic acid and laureth sulfate. I did a direct comparison a few drops of dish soap in apple cider vinegar gently stir about 2 seconds same smell, consistency and performance. It is effective at reducing the number of fruit flies, but will never eliminate them completely without removing garbage and cleaning the area. But do not buy, make your own for about 2 cents and a spare jar or bowl

I didn’t realize when I bought this that it was basically vinegar in a plastic bottle. I had been using the plastic apples with apple cider vinegar in it and it was catching the fruit flies but I was looking for something different, something better. I saw this and thought that I’d try it but it was pretty much the same as what I already had been using. It hasn’t caught any fruit flies and it’s been a few days. I’m afraid that the plastic bottle will topple over and spill vinegar so I put it in the window sill. I hope it works for others, it just hasn’t worked for me.
Does not work. Useless

I'd say there were about 40 or 50 fruit flies trapped in it but that's about it and I had about 100 fruit flies it did somewhat work but but only enough to make a slight impact on my fruit fly infestation.
Works well if you’re able to deploy it correctly. Half of the ones I attempted to deploy fell apart as I was trying to pull the sticky strip out of the cardboard outer lining.
Doesn't seem to attract flies like other fly strips I've used. The flies just seemed to ignore them. We took them down after a week of catching almost NO flies.

May work ok for house flies but the little fruit flies were not attracted to it, we caught maybe 3 and that was over the kitchen sink.

Just didn't work very well. Didn't catch any flies. I used this in conjunction with a small bait trap (the TERRO Fruit Fly Traps for Indoors). The fly tape caught about 2 flies, and the traps caught a million. Whatever is supposed to attract the flies on the tape just doesn't work. It's also pretty ugly, so I can't say I'd recommend this over the traps.

I literally saw a fly land on this, pause a moment, and then fly away. Another landed, walked around a bit, walked some more... I got tired of watching and left. The point is, the paper is not sticky enough to have maximum effect. A few bugs seemed to get tired and die on it, but others just went on their way. I have used fly paper before, but this just hasn't worked well enough. 2/5 stars, will not purchase again if I can help it.

Bought it based on the good reviews it has to trap the flies, but when I set it up, it looked ugly dangling from the ceiling(I stuck it to the chandelier). It sat there for a week and ended up getting hold of ONE fly. Didn't work at all for us. Returning.

**Notice, I'd love to have a picture of these, but they went into the trash bag because 3 out of 6 went there while hanging! The rest are in the drawer! _________________________________ The ease of unrolling is fraught with trying to untwist it & then try to hang up.... The included thumbtack is more of being able to slice your fingers than anything! It bends so easy & just won't go through drywall, firm cardboard or something else. To unroll it would be great if red string was looped out of the roll. Then before you do that STOP! Pull string out & mount with duct tape or something very strong. And from there start un-ravelling(twist) to straighten out.... But(yes another BUT) put a weight on the tube end to keep from twisting back up... Confused or annoyed with my review? Try doing it... And then you will understand.... Sadly

We had made our own fruit fly traps (apple cider vinegar with a few drops of dish soap) but read about these so decided to try them. When we received, I realized they are…apple cider vinegar with a few drops of dish soap. And, our homemade version has caught more fruit flies, are much cheaper, and these are more precarious and easier to accidentally knock over than if you build your own in a mug. Don’t bother.

Could this be a “it gets worse before it gets better” situation? I bought the trap because I noticed only a few flies (maybe 5) around my kitchen I’ve only had the trap out for 24 hours now but I’ve noticed MORE fruit flies than before as if it’s attracting more flies with its smell. There are maybe 10 fruit flies in the trap but at least 10 more flies flying in places around the trap. I’ll try keeping it up a while longer to see if there is improvement but otherwise I might have to throw it away and try something else.

Basically the same as using a home vinegar mix except this worked less than a home mix. We had an infestation of fruit flies and gnats for a short period of time and even with using 2 of these in different locations of the house, we only caught 3 in a week long period. Caught more using home made mix which was much cheaper and a bug zapper.
Its not working and fruit fly still around
I did not care for the ACV all the time. Also with the completely open lid it was not very accident friendly or capable of keeping a bug in unless it was fully covered in ACV.

This product is okay. It definitely attracted fruit flies but a homemade container of red wine vinegar and dish soap did the exact same level of job. I still have a fruit fly problem and it seems to have multiplied.
Didn't have any fruit so I guess they came up the drain. Too slow to work and too few dead. I can do the same with just apple cider vinegar

Basically, it did nothing but stink up the kitchen. I placed it in the kitchen where flies zooming around. In a week the flies were still airborne and not a single fly in the jar. I think it's design is poor. Just a tall and narrow jar, it is easy to tip over, and unlike similar products the top is nothing but an open jar. Other products to kill fruit flies I have used are lower and wider making them more stable and have several small holes in top so if it does tip it isn't a complete loss.
I don’t think it was worth the money or even works at all. I have had it out all night and all day today and only see 2 fruit flies in there. Not worth the money
After the first week of using these fruit fly traps, fruit flies stop getting trapped - maybe the scent wears off? I wasn't impressed with these.

Otherwise 1. Guys. This overpriced apple cider vinegar with a drip of dish soap. The DIY way works way better. Get a clear up, put a few drops of dish soap in it. Pour in apple cider vinegar so that there is a light "soap lining" over the top. Then cover the glass with well stretched cling wrap. Use a fork to poke s few small holes (easy to get on, hard for them to get out which is a big thing this product misses). The fruit flies smell it and crawl into the holes, ho toward the food-smell and get stuck from the layer of soap. They can't crawl out because of the plastic wrap. Bingo. $1 worth of supplies

Fruit flies were not attracted to it. Easy to use, just remove the lid. The scent is fine, not even detectable, it is a great concept but the t e r r o apple shaped fruit fly traps work 100x better. I actually even used both in my home at the same time and this container was knocked over (This is easy to knock over & waste ALL) and hardly had any caught fruit flies in it when the t e r r o apples had caught as many flies as they could catch, so the one minute it takes to squeeze the liquid into the little apples is definitely worth it even though this natural product just requires a person to remove a lid. Would not buy again, and I hate that because this product is reasonably priced and easy to use. It just didn't work.

I purchased Aunt Fannie's FlyPunch last summer due to an extreme amount of gnats and flies in our home. I felt the product worked extremely well and I knew I was going to buy it again a year later. A year later came, and i purchased 5 of them, one for each room that the gnats usually show up in. I received the 5 and not a single one of them I can get open due to the cap not coming off. I have to take a small flat head screwdriver and pry the cap off and then I'm able to get it off. When I finally get it off it also rips part of the seal off as well, so it makes a mess getting the liquid inside all over. I'm not sure if this was a production error or it's a problem with the new design of the product. But I've got to say I'm pretty disappointed this time.

I bought these because the description on the web site really seemed promising. I put them where we get these damn fruit flys. Bathroom sink, a few areas in the kitchen. The pictures above aren’t good, but there are only 2 out 3 flies in that bottle after over a week. Same with ALL of the bottles I placed. Please- don’t waste your time or money. NOT WORTH IT!!!
Not very effective. Caught some fruit flies but caught way more with homemade traps.

The positive is that I really like the bottle that it comes in. The negative is that it is just, in my opinion, diluted apple cider vinegar. This does not work! I did buy a three pack, and added a few drops of Dawn dishwashing liquid into each bottle and then it worked like crazy! I did have to tweak it in order to get it to work. The first 24 hours, before I added the Don dishwashing liquid, it did nothing

We just recently went on a vacation. Before we left one of our kids left a peach on the counter that rotted. When we got home we were super infested with fruit flys. So I bought this product to help get rid is of them. After 3 days it had caught just 4 fruit flies out of thousands of them.

Purchasing this product was a waste of money. First of all, the package was insecurely packaged. One bottle was empty when l opened the package. The liquid leaked all over the place. Secondly, the product caught very few gnats. I would have been better off just buying fly stickers or using apple cider vinegar and dish lotion.

My house has become infested with these stupid little fruit flies/drain flies/gnats whatever you want to call them. I have tried the homemade DIY traps and they catch some but not enough. This started out pretty good. I think my apple cider vinegar and dish soap attracted them more though. After having it out for a few weeks I finally just threw it away. I’m putting a pic of how many it caught. But keep in mind I still have them everywhere. And I stopped buying fresh fruit a couple months ago to try to help the situation(which it did improve but I miss fruit). Overall I would buy it again but there has to be a better way, a permanent way, to get rid of these things!!! Every time I purchase something online, I always check the reviews. I count on others to help me choose the best option. My reviews are always my honest opinion and I try to include photos if possible. If my review has helped you, please click the "helpful" button below, and if you have any questions feel free to ask! Thank you!

Bad fruit fly problem this summer. Got these amongst other products because I was desperate. Sad to say it didn't work. The flies would hover around it but never actually went in. If anything, I think they took a drink then flew away. Homemade apple cider vinegar traps worked better. It did arrive in a timely manner, however.
Dried up and didn’t catch any gnats.
DIDN'T WORK VERY WELL

I had such a bad gnats problem in the house and I was recommended these. As much as I wanted them to work, they barely did anything. I ended up investing is some Zevo lights and those work wonders. No more gnats or flies for that matter.

Didn’t work for me. I think I have even more bugs than before! I think the produce inside may work but the opening is narrow. I’m in Texas where these bugs are everywhere so it’s disappointing it didn’t work.

I had gnat-sized fruit flies in the kitchen, then noticed they would trawl around the house- bumbling just above the side tables in the living room, then I'd see them in the windows too. I thought I'd try Aunt Fannie's Flypunch (the vinegar scent smells CLEAN to me); it did attract them, but I watched them- this one-of-many species of fruit fly was smart enough to land on the sides of the jar inside, take a few steps to drink the liquid, then back up and fly out. When the jar was empty, there was a grand total of TWO flies in the bottom. I had high hopes, but no luck... My hubby had better luck with the little butterfly-shaped sticky stakes. He hung one a few inches under the shade on the side table lamp, and cut a slit in the lid of an old plastic margarine dish to slide another one into. Later, his little stakes had 40+ on each one. Of course right now we have no kids or pets around to spread the sticky all over, but the contest is done. 😄
This product works. I have ordered it before. But this time, even though the product is sold as a set, they only sent me one bottle.
Vinegar with a squirt of dish soap in a small bowl works just as well. We did a side-by-side test. Use this only if you don’t have any vinegar and dish soap.
Didn’t really work, was trying to find way to refill Taro containers for fruit flies but all this did was make kitchen smell of vinegar.

The product attracted a few drain flys put didn't reduce their life cycle as advertised. It has the same vinegar smell that attracts the pests and a few will go in. Not worth the money. Won't buy again.

Apple cider vinegar in a small glass, Saran Wrap over the top, rubber band keeping the wrap on the glass. Wrap taut like a drum, poke three holes about 3mm long; long enough for the little so-and-so’s to crawl in. They can’t crawl out, and will eventually pass out and drown. Two or three of these glasses around the kitchen took care of everything. This product, by comparison, smelled worse and grabbed maybe 1/10th the amount of each wrapped glass.

Not sure if this is typical of product, but really fairly useless compared to a fly swatter. Had hoped these would attract the flies, but the only get an occasional fly that happens to land on it, which is very rare.
THey worked but you can get better price on 40 pack.
not really working. I can see flies passing by it but didn't get attracted at all.
It work, for what I have is fruit fly, 3 star because nothing is perfect, it did the job tho. Don't know how long it last when is open so yea.

Tried putting these in several places around the house and, despite there being multiple flies around, they did not seem to be attracted to the sticky tape, at all. I have used these in the past (not this particular brand, but other brands, and the ones I've used in the past would collect flies very quickly), so it's hard to say if the issue is with this product, or just insect evolution.
Hard to put up without a ladder and stick to everything. Need a special cleaner to take the sticky remnants off walls and cabinets. Did not catch most of the flys and nats.

hese fly traps are okay — they do the job and help catch flies, but they’re a bit tricky to pull out. Sometimes they work perfectly, and other times they don’t come out as easily as expected. Still, for the price, they’re worth it and get the job done.

I can't explain how they will work in one area of my house but not in others. I put these in my kitchen where I get most flies but the one I hung in the den window gets more on it. You will get flies on it but if you don't see results after 2 weeks move it
Wasn’t easy to unravel - and I made sure they were warm before opening. They would’ve caught more insects, quicker, if the tape unraveled better.

Minus 1 star because they don't unfurl like they are supposed to, no matter how carefully, slowly, and gently you twist and pull. They just rip instead because they are so sticky. Minus a second star because it can be very stressful to remove the stickum from yourself if you accidentally come into contact with it--it is stubborn stuff if you don't have the right solutions. Keep three stars because this stuff is more than sticky enough to catch flies. It can catch a lot of flies, and it can even catch people lol. If you buy this, proceed with patience to unfurl it, and be sure to have a variety of de-gunkifying solutions on standby in case you accidentally get some of the stickum on you. Seriously. I tried dish soap, oil, nail polish remover, rubbing alcohol, and pomice scrub and still had difficulty getting it all off of my hand. Maybe goo-gone could do it but I couldn't find mine.
I’ve had 2 of these right next to a bunch of bananas for a week now Still have a lot of fruit flies on/around the bananas, but only two flies on the trap

I hate fruit flies. But apparently they love me and my house. Sigh. I bought these thinking they would help control the fruit fly population. Alas, they don't. Yes, they are sticky. And yes, when the ocasional fruit fly decides to land on them they do get stuck. However, these sticky pads do not appear to be attracting the flies. I often find myself holding one of the sticky pads on a stick and trying to smoosh the flies against the window or against the fruit they are sitting on. Or heck sometimes I"m even waving the sticky thing in the air trying to catch the flies in mid-flight. I wish they had some sort of attraction scent or some other way to lure the flies onto the sticky pad.

Bought this as I had some gnats get into the house and were driving me nuts so I tried this. Not sure if I did something wrong, but not impressed. Then I remembered the traps that I have seen that involve creating a small opening just big enough for the pests to get in and then they never figure out how to get back out so I took an empty Gatorade bottle, poured a little Gatorade at the bottom, got some paper and made the paper into a cone shape with a opening and inserted it into the opening, added some tape around the edges to make it stay in place and also seal any gaps and BAM! Works perfectly. The pests fly into the bottle and cant figure out how to get back out. Since I drink Gatorade and have it always around I will be using this little DIY trick and the bottle/liquid just need to be sweet/tempting to the bugs, it does not have to be a Gatorade bottle. It could be a water bottle that you add some sugar and water to.

The idea was good but this firm did not spend enough on performance. I ended up with half of the butterfly and half of the tree and the other half was covered in strips of paper because I couldn't get it off. Lots of glue all over my hands I spoke to other people who had had a different manufacturer and they had not had any problem but they were a lot more expensive! I had to throw most of mine out and I was covered in glue!

Do they work? Well, yes. The gnats do stick to them, the traps just don't stick well to anything else... These are my issues. The white paper is hard to get off. Especially if you don't have finger nails. The traps simply fall off of the sticks they comes with. Yes you can use the twist ties to hang them, but I like them closer to the soil in the pot. I even tried sticking them to the side of the pot, and they just fall off. I did have some luck punching an extra hole in the bottom to slide the stake through both holes. That's the only thing that worked so far. And I may need to use dowel rods instead as these stakes are very small trying to use this way. I prefer the ones that are sticky trap with the stake all as 1 piece so there no chance.

I purchased this item hoping to capture flies around my patio area. Took 2 days to catch 2 flies. Mean while flies are buzzing all around us as we sit outside. Did some research and found out YELLOW is one of the least attractive colors for flies....why make a product with a color that flies are least likely to be attracted to? Misleading advertising, shame on the mfg for stating it will attract flies. Do some research, you will find that blue is the color of choice. I actually watched flies land on the blue section of a beach towel, avoiding the yellow, further backing the science. Shame on me, got me with this one.

This definitely won't solve your fruit fly problem. It only works if you place it right next to a bait (ripe fruit, for example). Otherwise no fly will even come close to it. Even when they do land on it, it usually only takes them a little extra effort and they fly off, because the glue is not good enough. Major disappointment.

These are great! Only issue i have is when I opened the package is sticky tags and started using found a stack already used and covered in dirt, missing everything else but the sticky tags. Kinda disappointed but can’t send back since i already opened and had used some before I found the ones that had already been used in the package!

It works great as a sticky trap but it is difficult to peel in one piece. Especially the butterfly design. When it doesn't peel on one go and you try to remove more, your fingers get sticky and stuck as well which gets even more frustrating. Wish they thought of a better design. I don't need cute designs. A circle or square will work just fine.

Product description says these can be used indoors or outdoors, and they work really well; however, they should only be used indoors because lizards get stuck on them and die (also dragonflies and ladybugs - both of which help to keep plant eating pests under control outside). They are also a risk to honeybees and hummingbirds. I bought them for use outdoors and will not be able to use them

I looked at all the DIY suggestions on how to get rid of my fruit flies that seem to be multiplying exponentially. None of them really worked well and so I bought this product. Frankly, for me it didn't work any better than what I had tried and the formula seems to be relatively the same as the DIY

I have had much better success using apple cider vinegar and dish soap. Pour about a half inch of apple cider vinegar into a coffee cup and add a drizzle of dish soap. No need to mix, just leave it wherever you've seen the fruit flies. This product costs $8 and the only thing it was good for is the plastic container in my case. I have seen others having a lot of success with this and had hi hopes. I have also seen that others have said that they may not have fruits flies, but I'm pretty sure the little buggars are just that. No worries though, just trying different options.
For the price point I really expected it to do a way better job. It helped a little but definetly not the fix for tge problem .

Unfortunately, this didn’t work for me. I live in an apartment of less than 600 sq feet and the fruit flies did not take. I kept it lying around to keep re-trying it but instead it eventually ended up spilling and making my apartment smell like vinegar. Don’t recommend you wasting your $ for a fancily-packaged vinegar.
Bought these a few times now and they’ve always worked great! Recently tho they have not been catching anything at all!

this pretty much smells like apple cider vinegar. not sure if it has other things in it. Did not work as i hoped. have had it for about 3 weeks. there are a few flies in it but co side ring how many i have it is about 5% the others just swarm around the top. Will not be buying again
This is just a very expensive vinegar trap. I placed it next to a homemade trap with red wine, and another with vermouth. The vermouth trapped 90% of the flies. Not worth the cost
Saved my product defective with leaking. Returned.

I've had it open for a week and caught no fruit flies. The homemade "traps" that i made (apple cider vinegar with a drop of dish soap covered with plastic with a few holes in the top with a toothpick) placed not too far away caught 5 fruit flies within 2 days.
This product (Annie's Fruit Fly) somewhat worked for my niece but it didn't work well for me.

Fall brings these little fruit fly, gnat things around my area. They are really problematic. This product was suggested to me and was said to be a miracle that would solve my problem. NOT!! While a few of these annoying little critters were attracted to the open bottle, the majority were not and kept annoying the household. Wouldn't waste my money again, would try the home remedy I was told about with apple vinegar and dish soap. Don't think it would catch any fewer. Good luck, buyer beware!

After reading reviews and press for this product, I had high hopes. Unfortunately, the product didn’t quite deliver on my expectations. When the fruit flies first became an issue in my apartment, I tried out a DIY concoction of apple cider vinegar, water, sugar and dish soap. It caught a lot of flies, but not all of them. I ordered this product and put it out in the same room as the DIY solution. Unfortunately, it only caught one fly while my mixture caught 15-20 flies in the same time span. I have continued to leave Fanny’s out, but although the other mix continues to drown flies, this one still only has that one corpse in it. Save your money.

I’ve cleaned. My drains closed off. My drains done everything that I can to mitigate the problem, but I just cannot get rid of the fruit flies. This catches some of them, but from time to time you’ll see fruit flies, flying around it, or landing on the mirror above it, but never going into the bottle. It does catch some. It’s just not luring all of them. Ive had it for several weeks. has strong smell initially.
It didn’t work as well as the first time I had it. Maybe the first week I didn’t see fruit flies but started again after that. Last year they were gone for good when I opened the bottle.

I've had the product open for two days now and it doesn't work as well as promised. It has attracted some of the fruit flies, but not nearly enough. I got this because I wanted something that was no mess and no touch. I definitely would try a different product if I need help again, which I probably will given the flies I still see.

I bought this after red wine vinegar with a drop of dish soap and apple cider vinegar with a drop of dish soap wasnt really working. I wanted something to help with my issue in a different way for my compost bucket. This product is the same that I was using, and shock… it still doesn’t work. They sit on the rim of the container. Save your money google the recipe with vinegar and do it yourself in a glass.
So I initially thought it would work because the fruit flies were all hovering around it but they never did drink it. Still have fruit flies and need to try something else. Bummer
Upon delivery one bottle was completely empty bc it leaked all in the packing . However , the other bottle worked fairly well so I bought 2 more. With a total of 4 of these it helped quite a bit.

It did work for a few fruit flies, but it makes the entire room reek of vinegar!!! If you don't mind having the entire room smelling like vinegar, then this may work for you. We used this along with other fly traps and the other traps worked more efficiently than this. Maybe because of the overwhelming smell.
Wasn’t nearly as effective as the terro traps. Dont waste your money. Left it out for 2 weeks and caught maybe 5 flys
Did not work as claimed. Simple vinegar recipe worked better

I had previously ordered this product and was very happy with the results. I reordered and when the product arrived today the seals on both of the traps had leaked into the packaging. This may have been caused by the high heat in the Phoenix area, but I won't be ordering this product again.

I bought this rather than doing a “do it yourself” remedy to get rid of fruit flies. I could have saved $$ and done it myself. You open the top of the bottle and it’s liquid- and easy to spill. While it is helping with the fruit flies, it took a few days. Save your $ and buy a cheap brand of apple cider vinegar. I think that’s all that’s in the jar!
This did work as well as other brands I tried. I won't buy it again.
It only caught a few fruit flies and then nothing

This used to work great. I used to just open it and watch as all of my fruit flies descended to their doom. My place would be rid of fruit flies within a few days. However, this last time it didn’t seem to work at all. No matter where this was put or how long it was left there, it didn’t seem to trap any of the fruit flies. I’m sad I’ll need to find a new solution to my fruit fly woes as this no longer cuts it

I was expecting that for the amount of liquid and compared to the other options in the market this product was going to catch many more flies and it turns out it didn’t. In fact I placed this product right next to the product that comes in the shape of an apple and the other one outlasted this one 2:1

There may be a bit of something else but it's mostly vinegar. It smells up the house but I will have to say I've only seen one pesky fruit fly since I opened the bottles, set one in kitchen and another near cat litter box. So I guess it's working...?

I haven’t seen any fruit flies actually get trapped by it yet. One was happily buzzing around it. The container itself has a wide mouth and this makes it prone to being spilled. I should have just made my own mixture - I thought this would be a more reliable alternative and a better container than mixing apple cider vinegar in a bowl.

Don't waste your money. You can easily make a fly trap out of vinegar from your pantry and put it in a cup. That is all this was. You rip off the seal and "pop" the top open and it lets the flies in. Over a few days I only noticed 3. However, this being cheap little bottle of plastic, having it open on your counters is not advised. I tipped this over several times causing the terrible vinegar smell to linger longer than desired! This tipped due to a gust of wind from my kitchen window or my maneuvering thru the kitchen just trying to make dinner.

For the cost, this is really ineffective. It works slightly better than trying to get apple cider vinegar and dish soap mixed properly but not by much. Smells about the same. It's not even worth the risk of knocking it over. I'll leave it out longer to see if I can change my mind but I don't recommend it.

I give it a poor rating simply because it didnt work for me. Been sitting out in open areas near the flies for a week (two bottle traps even) and not a single fly has landed in there, or at least died in there. I tried leaving it cracked open, and fully opened as well. One night I accidentally left a ramekin of uncooked egg on the counter ( I was making pie or something) and the next morning I noticed the ramekin had about 20 flies over just one night and the traps still had zero. It clearly works for some people, but for me it didnt. If it didnt work for you, try the egg trick.

It catches enough flies (I guess?) but honestly like others are saying - you may as well have a home remedy if you’re just going to have an open jar of vinegar around. Also, one small movement and the entire bottle can be emptied since there is absolutely no form of filter/screen etc. on the top. Not a total waste of money but other products or a home remedy work better in my opinion.
save money. pour apple cider vinegar in a glass add 2 drops dish soap. that's all this is

I have a small enclosed apt. kitchen 8x8. It has been swarming with fruit flies, due to the extra fruit I've added to my nutrition. I bought this product and put it on my counter, opened it as instructed, and left it for 5 days. It has only captured 8 fruitfulness, no more. I'm disappointed.

I read reviews before I bought these. Some weren’t pleased with the product, but most said it was amazing!! I bought 3 bottles and spent $25 hoping this would be the end of my fruit fly problems, but it doesn’t seem to be working. Each bottle has caught about 2 flies and that’s it. They’ve been out for almost a week and they aren’t really attracting the flies. The smell isnt overpowering, so that’s a plus, but aside from that, I feel like I wasted $25 The seller is great; this is in NO WAY a bad review for them as a seller. ONLY the product is being reviewed here.

Didn't really work that well for me. Only a few fruit flies were caught using these. Not sure why. Tried to make sure there was nothing else they would be attracted to like bananas on the counter, but still only caught a few.

Although highly rated, this product didn’t work well for me. I managed to kill about 6 bugs in a week. I closed the lid and waited a day then tried again. After opening the lid I noted the bug problem got even worse than before.
When I received the items one jar had totally leaked out and soaked the package.

The bait is semi-effective. There are better baits out there. But the main fault is in the shape of the trap, which isn’t much of one. The opening is too wide. It’s as if you dumped the bait into a mug. The flies mostly sit on the rim then fly off. Perhaps if one could use the bait in a more effective trap, it’d be better, but one generally doesn’t expect to have to buy separate traps at this price. At least it doesn’t smell horrible or like a chem lab; just the homey tang of apple cider vinegar.
Horrible product!!!!!! Don't waste your money. It has not caught not 1 fruit fly. They are not attracted to the yellow color.
This did not attract a single fly or other flying insect! Do not purchase this.
Doesn’t work. Fruit flies ignored these.
Did not catch even one.

These are useless. They might work if you have an infestation but I literally caught a fruit fly walking over the adhesive. Put a few out for 2 weeks and have only caught one. Still see several flying around my home.
Trash!!! Save your money staying away from this item!!!
These don't work. I am literally watching fruit flies walk across them and then fly away.
Unfortunately these have not worked for our fruit fly problem.
Put 5 in area around kitchen. Not a single catch in 2 weeks.
Did not capture 1 gnat. Do not buy.

These feel sticky to me, but I've watched enough gnats land on them and then fly away to say they're definitely not sticky enough. Not sure what that's about, but I'll be switching back to the brand I used to use instead of these. Love the mini shovel though!

I’m so incredibly frustrated and angry. I type this while my fingers are sticking to my phone screen even after washing with hot water and soap and using acetone just to try and get off what got on my fingers trying to pull the impossible to remove paper. What’s the point if you can’t get the paper off!!!

This is my second purchase and I may have gotten a defective batch. My first set worked well and captured many fungus gnats. This one I saw some fungus gnats but others also landed and fly away like it was not as sticky. I don’t know what it is, maybe weather is colder now and makes the things less sticky? But disappointed with the last batch of traps from this seller. I may have to go hunt for another supplier of sticky traps.
These don’t work. I still have tons of gnats on my plants and not a single one has been stuck.

Impossible to peel the paper on the flower pattern design. The paper pull tab is not designed to peel the entire paper. Once it fails, there is no clear way to pick the other jagged edges of paper off of the glue. And if you are successful with one side, you’re gifted with an opportunity to then flip it and try the same crazy process on the other side but have to fight with with exposed glue on the peeled side. Sticky fingers and frustration made me quit this failed experiment.

Not effective for fruit flies. Found that putting vinegar in a cup with a dropper to a soap stir it in and let it sit on the countertop works the best. You have to add a couple drops of soap to change the surface tension of the water because a fruit fly can land on the apple cider of vinegar. This so-called fruit-fly trap was just a container of liquid that smelled like vinegar. It is more of a fruit fly feeding station. It did provide inspiration for a trap I made myself. I used an old spice bottle with a perforated top. I filled it half-way with a mixture of cider vinegar and dishwashing liquid. The fruit flies crawled into the bottle and were unable to get back out.
